title: Português de Portugal rumo ao DIPLE

days.title: Imperfeito do conjuntivo
days.objective: Form the imperfect subjunctive from the 3rd-person plural of the perfect and build hypothetical 'se' sentences.
days.paretoFocus: One mechanical rule — falaram → falasse — unlocks every hypothetical, wish and 'as if' sentence in Portuguese, with all the irregularities already built in for free.
drills: Convert out loud: falaram, comeram, fizeram, tiveram, foram → falasse, comesse, fizesse, tivesse, fosse.
Build five daydreams about your ideal life: 'Se pudesse, ...', 'Se fosse rico, ...', each finished with the imperfeito (viajava, comprava).
Tell an imaginary friend three things you wanted from them last week: 'Queria que viesses...', 'Queria que fizesses...'

items: Recall: which verb form gives you the stem of the imperfeito do conjuntivo?
Say the eu-forms of fazer, ter, ser and poder in the imperfeito do conjuntivo.
Translate: 'If I were rich, I would travel more.'

activities.title: Se eu tivesse...
activities.intro: The imperfect subjunctive: take the eles-form of the pretérito perfeito, drop -ram, add -sse. It powers unreal conditions, past wishes and 'as if'.

items.hr: Falaram → falasse, fizeram → fizesse, foram → fosse: a forma vem sempre do pretérito perfeito.
items.en: Falaram → falasse, fizeram → fizesse, foram → fosse: the form always comes from the perfect.
items.note: drop -ram, add -sse — every irregularity is inherited automatically

items.hr: Se eu tivesse mais tempo, aprendia a tocar guitarra.
items.en: If I had more time, I would learn to play the guitar.
items.note: spoken pt-PT happily uses the imperfeito (aprendia) instead of the conditional

items.hr: Se pudesse, viajaria muito mais.
items.en: If I could, I would travel much more.
items.note: written pattern: se + imperfeito do conjuntivo, condicional (viajaria)

items.hr: Se fosse rico, comprava uma casa no Alentejo.
items.en: If I were rich, I would buy a house in the Alentejo.
items.note: foram → fosse: ser and ir share the same forms

items.hr: Queria que viesses comigo à reunião.
items.en: I wanted you to come with me to the meeting.
items.note: a past trigger (queria que) pulls the imperfeito do conjuntivo: vieram → viesses

items.hr: Ele falou como se soubesse tudo sobre o assunto.
items.en: He spoke as if he knew everything about the matter.
items.note: como se (as if) ALWAYS takes the imperfeito do conjuntivo

items.hr: Era importante que fizéssemos o trabalho a tempo.
items.en: It was important that we did the work on time.
items.note: the nós-form gains an accent: fizéssemos, falássemos, tivéssemos

activities.title: Mundos hipotéticos
activities.intro: Build the imperfect subjunctive and use it in unreal conditions and past wishes.

questions.prompt: The imperfeito do conjuntivo is formed from which base?
options: the 3rd-person plural of the pretérito perfeito minus -ram
questions.answer: the 3rd-person plural of the pretérito perfeito minus -ram
questions.explanation: Falaram → falasse, tiveram → tivesse: because it comes from the perfect, all irregular stems come along for free.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Se eu ___ rico, comprava um barco.'
questions.explanation: An unreal condition about the present takes se + imperfeito do conjuntivo: se eu fosse rico.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive of 'fazer': 'Se ele ___ menos barulho, eu conseguia dormir.'
questions.explanation: Fizeram minus -ram plus -sse gives fizesse — the irregular stem is inherited from the perfect.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Queria que tu ___ comigo ao médico amanhã.' (vir)
questions.explanation: The past trigger 'queria que' demands the imperfeito do conjuntivo: vieram → viesses for tu.

questions.prompt: 'Ele gasta dinheiro como se ___ milionário.' Which form follows 'como se'?
questions.explanation: 'Como se' (as if) always takes the imperfeito do conjuntivo, never the indicative.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'If I had time, I would go with you to Porto.'
questions.explanation: Se + imperfeito do conjuntivo in the condition, imperfeito (ia) in the result — the everyday spoken pattern.

activities.title: Se ganhasses o Euromilhões...
activities.intro: Daydreaming with a friend at a café in Coimbra. Play 'Me' and keep the hypotheses flowing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Imagina: se ganhasses o Euromilhões, o que é que fazias?
lines.en: Imagine: if you won the EuroMillions, what would you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se ganhasse, deixava de trabalhar e comprava uma casa com vista para o mar.
lines.en: If I won, I'd stop working and buy a house with a sea view.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Só isso? Eu queria que a minha família viesse toda viver para perto de mim.
lines.en: Just that? I'd want my whole family to come and live near me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa ideia. E se pudesses viver em qualquer cidade, escolhias Lisboa?
lines.en: Good idea. And if you could live in any city, would you choose Lisbon?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não, escolhia o Porto. Falas como se Lisboa fosse a única cidade do país!
lines.en: No, I'd choose Porto. You talk as if Lisbon were the only city in the country!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão... Se os bilhetes não fossem tão caros, até jogava mais vezes!
lines.en: You're right... If the tickets weren't so expensive, I'd even play more often!

days.title: Futuro do conjuntivo
days.objective: Use the future subjunctive after quando, se, assim que and enquanto whenever they point at the future.
days.paretoFocus: The future subjunctive is the signature tense of European Portuguese — master 'quando puderes' and 'se fores' and every future plan, promise and instruction becomes natural.
drills: Convert out loud: falaram, fizeram, foram, puderam, vieram → falar, fizer, for, puder, vier.
Make three promises to a friend: 'Quando chegares...', 'Assim que souberes...', 'Se vieres...'
Describe your next trip with three future-subjunctive clauses: 'Quando estiver lá...', 'Se tiver tempo...', 'Enquanto puder...'

items: Recall: which four conjunctions most often trigger the futuro do conjuntivo?
Say the tu-forms: fizeres, tiveres, fores, puderes, vieres.
Translate: 'When you can, call me.'

activities.title: Quando puderes...
activities.intro: Portuguese keeps a living future subjunctive — formed, like the imperfect, from the eles-form of the perfect, this time minus -am.

items.hr: Falaram → falar, tiveram → tiver: nos verbos regulares o futuro do conjuntivo parece o infinitivo.
items.en: Falaram → falar, tiveram → tiver: in regular verbs the future subjunctive looks like the infinitive.
items.note: it hides in plain sight — only the irregulars (fizer, for, puder, vier) reveal it

items.hr: Quando puderes, telefona-me.
items.en: When you can, call me.
items.note: puderam → puder; quando + future reference = futuro do conjuntivo

items.hr: Se fores a Lisboa, visita o Mosteiro dos Jerónimos.
items.en: If you go to Lisbon, visit the Jerónimos Monastery.
items.note: foram → for: ser and ir share the forms for, fores, formos, forem

items.hr: Assim que chegares a casa, manda-me uma mensagem.
items.en: As soon as you get home, send me a message.
items.note: assim que / logo que + futuro do conjuntivo for future actions

items.hr: Enquanto quiseres, podes ficar cá em casa.
items.en: As long as you want, you can stay here at our place.
items.note: quiseram → quiser; enquanto also triggers the tense

items.hr: Faz como quiseres; eu apoio-te.
items.en: Do as you wish; I support you.
items.note: como, o que and quem also point at the open future

items.hr: Quem vier atrasado terá de esperar lá fora.
items.en: Whoever comes late will have to wait outside.
items.note: vieram → vier — a classic irregular worth memorising

activities.title: O futuro em aberto
activities.intro: Choose and build future-subjunctive forms after quando, se, assim que and enquanto.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Se ___ a Lisboa no verão, vai à Feira do Livro.'
questions.explanation: Se + future reference takes the futuro do conjuntivo: foram → for, fores.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quando ___ (poder, tu), liga-me — preciso de falar contigo.'
questions.explanation: Puderam minus -am gives puder; the tu-form adds -es: puderes.

questions.prompt: Why does the future subjunctive of regular verbs 'hide in plain sight'?
options: It looks identical to the infinitive
It looks like the present indicative
It is only used in writing
It only exists for irregular verbs
questions.answer: It looks identical to the infinitive
questions.explanation: Falar, comeres, partirmos look like infinitives — only irregulars like fizer, for and puder show the tense openly.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Assim que o comboio ___ (chegar), avisa-me.'
questions.explanation: Assim que + future action takes the futuro do conjuntivo — for a regular verb it looks just like the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Enquanto ___ em Portugal, aproveito para ver a família.'
questions.explanation: Enquanto with future reference triggers the futuro do conjuntivo: estiveram → estiver.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Whoever arrives late waits outside.'
questions.explanation: Quem + futuro do conjuntivo (vier) covers 'whoever does X in the future'.

activities.title: Planos para o Algarve
activities.intro: You are off to the Algarve next week and a friend gives you instructions. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então sempre vais ao Algarve na próxima semana?
lines.en: So you're definitely going to the Algarve next week?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vou! Assim que sair do trabalho na sexta, apanho o comboio para Faro.
lines.en: I am! As soon as I leave work on Friday, I'm catching the train to Faro.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quando chegares, manda-me uma mensagem, está bem?
lines.en: When you arrive, send me a message, okay?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Claro. E se tiveres tempo no fim de semana, vem ter connosco!
lines.en: Of course. And if you have time at the weekend, come join us!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se puder, vou. Enquanto estiveres lá, prova o peixe assado — é ótimo.
lines.en: If I can, I will. While you're there, try the grilled fish — it's great.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vou provar tudo! E tu, faz como quiseres, mas eu reservava já o bilhete.
lines.en: I'll try everything! And you, do as you wish, but I'd book the ticket right away.

days.title: O mundo do trabalho
days.objective: Apply for a job in Portugal: CV, cover letter, interview, contract and salary talk.
days.paretoFocus: Candidatura, entrevista, contrato, salário and recibos verdes are the five words that carry every Portuguese job hunt — from the ad to the signature.
drills: Present yourself for an interview in one minute: formação, experiência, competências.
Explain the difference between 'um contrato a termo' and 'passar recibos verdes'.
Negotiate out loud: ask about salário, horário flexível and teletrabalho in three polite questions.

items: Recall: what does a 'trabalhador independente' issue instead of receiving a contract?
Say the pair you send when applying: o CV e a carta de motivação.
Translate: 'After the dismissal, he received compensation.'

activities.title: Da candidatura ao contrato
activities.intro: The vocabulary of the Portuguese job market, from sending your application to signing — or not signing — a contract.

items.hr: Enviei a minha candidatura com o CV e uma carta de motivação em português.
items.en: I sent my application with the CV and a cover letter in Portuguese.
items.note: candidatar-se a uma vaga = to apply for an opening

items.hr: Fui chamado para uma entrevista de emprego na sede da empresa.
items.en: I was called for a job interview at the company's headquarters.
items.note: a sede = head office; a filial = branch

items.hr: Ofereceram-me um contrato a termo de um ano, com salário acima do salário mínimo.
items.en: They offered me a one-year fixed-term contract, with a salary above the minimum wage.
items.note: contrato a termo = fixed-term; contrato sem termo = permanent

items.hr: Muitos trabalhadores independentes em Portugal passam recibos verdes.
items.en: Many self-employed workers in Portugal issue 'green receipts'.
items.note: recibos verdes = the Portuguese freelancer invoicing regime — a very common work arrangement

items.hr: A empresa permite teletrabalho dois dias por semana e horário flexível.
items.en: The company allows remote work two days a week and flexible hours.
items.note: o teletrabalho became standard interview vocabulary

items.hr: Depois do despedimento, recebeu uma indemnização e o subsídio de desemprego.
items.en: After the dismissal, he received severance pay and unemployment benefit.
items.note: o despedimento = dismissal; a indemnização = compensation

items.hr: A formação contínua e a avaliação de desempenho fazem parte do pacote.
items.en: Ongoing training and the performance review are part of the package.
items.note: a avaliação de desempenho = performance review

activities.title: À procura de emprego
activities.intro: Choose the right word at each step of the Portuguese job hunt.

questions.prompt: What do you send together with your CV when applying for a job?
questions.explanation: The cover letter ('carta de motivação') accompanies the CV in a 'candidatura'.

questions.prompt: A freelancer in Portugal typically 'passa ___'.
questions.explanation: 'Passar recibos verdes' is the standard expression for invoicing as a self-employed worker.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Vou candidatar-me a esta ___ de gestor de projeto — o anúncio saiu ontem.' (opening)
questions.explanation: 'A vaga' is the vacancy or opening you apply for: candidatar-se a uma vaga.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for severance pay: 'Depois de dez anos na empresa, recebeu uma boa ___.'
questions.explanation: 'A indemnização' is the compensation paid on dismissal — note the pt-PT spelling with -mn-.

questions.prompt: Your boss says: 'Podes trabalhar a partir de casa às sextas.' What is this called?
questions.explanation: Working from home is 'o teletrabalho'; 'rescisão' would be terminating the contract.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I sent the application within the deadline.'
questions.explanation: 'Dentro do prazo' = within the deadline — key phrase for applications and deliveries alike.

activities.title: A entrevista de emprego
activities.intro: A job interview in Lisbon. The interviewer uses the formal register — play 'Me' and answer professionally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia, obrigado por ter vindo. Fale-me um pouco da sua formação.
lines.en: Good morning, thank you for coming. Tell me a little about your education.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Tirei o curso de gestão e trabalhei três anos numa filial em Coimbra.
lines.en: Good morning. I did a degree in management and worked for three years at a branch in Coimbra.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E porque é que se candidatou a esta vaga?
lines.en: And why did you apply for this opening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Porque as minhas competências encaixam no perfil e valorizo a formação contínua que oferecem.
lines.en: Because my skills fit the profile and I value the ongoing training you offer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muito bem. Oferecemos um contrato a termo, com teletrabalho dois dias por semana.
lines.en: Very good. We offer a fixed-term contract, with remote work two days a week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Parece-me bem. E em relação ao salário, há espaço para negociação?
lines.en: That sounds good. And regarding the salary, is there room for negotiation?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Há, dentro dos limites da tabela. Entraremos em contacto quando tivermos uma decisão.
lines.en: There is, within the limits of the pay scale. We'll be in touch when we have a decision.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Agradeço desde já a atenção. Fico a aguardar a vossa resposta.
lines.en: Thank you in advance for your attention. I'll be waiting for your reply.

days.title: Infinitivo pessoal
days.objective: Conjugate the infinitive after prepositions and impersonal expressions: para fazermos, antes de saíres, é melhor irmos.
days.paretoFocus: The personal infinitive is Portugal's elegant shortcut — after any preposition it replaces a whole subjunctive clause, and one rule of thumb (preposition → infinitive, 'que' → conjuntivo) decides every case.
drills: Conjugate out loud: fazer, fazeres, fazer, fazermos, fazerem — then the same with ir and sair.
Make three plans with 'para' + personal infinitive: 'para jantarmos juntos', 'para chegares a horas'...
Say pairs aloud: 'antes de saíres / antes que saias', 'apesar de ser tarde / embora seja tarde'.

items: Recall: which forms of the personal infinitive differ from the plain infinitive?
Say the rule of thumb: preposition → personal infinitive, conjunction with 'que' → conjuntivo.
Translate: 'It's better for us to leave before midnight.'

activities.title: O infinitivo que se conjuga
activities.intro: Portuguese can conjugate the infinitive — fazer, fazeres, fazermos, fazerem — a feature shared only with Galician. Use it whenever the infinitive has its own subject.

items.hr: Fazer, fazeres, fazer, fazermos, fazerem: só o tu, o nós e o eles ganham terminação.
items.en: Fazer, fazeres, fazer, fazermos, fazerem: only tu, nós and eles gain an ending.
items.note: for regular verbs it looks like the future subjunctive — context tells them apart

items.hr: Comprei fruta para fazermos uma salada ao jantar.
items.en: I bought fruit for us to make a salad at dinner.
items.note: para + personal infinitive when the subject is 'nós'

items.hr: Ao chegarem a casa, telefonaram-me logo.
items.en: On arriving home, they phoned me straight away.
items.note: ao + personal infinitive = when/on ...-ing

items.hr: Antes de saíres, desliga o aquecedor.
items.en: Before you leave, switch off the heater.
items.note: antes de + infinitive; compare antes que saias (conjuntivo)

items.hr: É melhor irmos embora antes da meia-noite.
items.en: It's better for us to leave before midnight.
items.note: impersonal expressions (é melhor, foi difícil) also take the personal infinitive

items.hr: Apesar de estarem cansados, continuaram a trabalhar.
items.en: Despite being tired, they kept working.
items.note: apesar de + infinitive vs embora + conjuntivo — same meaning, different grammar

items.hr: Sem saberes os factos, não podes dar uma opinião.
items.en: Without knowing the facts, you can't give an opinion.
items.note: sem, até, depois de — all prepositions welcome the personal infinitive

activities.title: Preposição ou conjunção?
activities.intro: Decide when the personal infinitive wins and build its forms.

questions.prompt: Which is correct after the preposition 'para' with the subject 'nós'?
questions.explanation: After a preposition use the personal infinitive (para fazermos); 'para que' would take the conjuntivo (para que façamos).

questions.prompt: Complete: 'É melhor ___ (ir, nós) andando — já é tarde.'
questions.explanation: Impersonal 'é melhor' plus a nós-subject takes the personal infinitive: irmos.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Antes de ___ (sair, tu), fecha as janelas.'
questions.explanation: Antes de + personal infinitive; the tu-form adds -es: saíres.

questions.prompt: 'Ao chegarem ao aeroporto, ligaram-me.' What does 'ao chegarem' mean?
questions.explanation: Ao + personal infinitive expresses 'on/when ...-ing'; the -em ending shows the subject is 'eles'.

questions.prompt: Which connector forces the conjuntivo instead of the personal infinitive?
questions.explanation: Embora is a conjunction and takes the conjuntivo (embora seja tarde); the other three are prepositions and take the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We'd better catch the eight o'clock train.'
questions.explanation: É melhor + personal infinitive (apanharmos) = it's better for us to catch the eight o'clock train.

activities.title: Jantar de sábado
activities.intro: You and a friend are organising a dinner at home in Porto. Play 'Me' and plan with personal infinitives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comprei bacalhau para fazermos um jantar cá em casa no sábado.
lines.en: I bought salt cod for us to make a dinner here at home on Saturday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa! É melhor convidarmos também a Marta e o Rui, não achas?
lines.en: Nice! It's better for us to invite Marta and Rui too, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Claro. Ao saírem do trabalho, eles podem passar no supermercado.
lines.en: Of course. When they leave work, they can stop by the supermarket.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vou mandar-lhes mensagem já, antes de me esquecer.
lines.en: I'll message them right away, before I forget.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Diz-lhes para trazerem vinho, que eu trato da sobremesa.
lines.en: Tell them to bring wine, and I'll take care of dessert.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Apesar de sermos só cinco, vai parecer um banquete!
lines.en: Deal. Despite there being only five of us, it's going to look like a banquet!

days.title: Conjuntivo composto
days.objective: Look back with the subjunctive: espero que tenha corrido bem, se tivesse sabido teria vindo.
days.paretoFocus: Two forms of ter — tenha and tivesse — plus a participle give you every past hope, doubt and regret, including the third conditional that exams love.
drills: React to yesterday's news three times: 'Espero que tenha...', 'Duvido que tenham...', 'É pena que não tenhas...'
Confess three regrets: 'Se tivesse estudado mais...', each finished with 'teria' or 'tinha' + participle.
Promise a friend: 'Quando tiveres acabado X, fazemos Y' — with three different activities.

items: Recall: which auxiliary builds all compound subjunctives?
Say the unreal-past pattern: se + tivesse feito, teria/tinha feito.
Translate: 'I hope the interview went well.'

activities.title: Tenha feito, tivesse feito
activities.intro: The subjunctive has compound tenses too: ter in the right subjunctive tense + participle. They look back at completed actions.

items.hr: Espero que a entrevista tenha corrido bem.
items.en: I hope the interview went well.
items.note: present trigger looking back → tenha + particípio (perfeito do conjuntivo)

items.hr: Duvido que eles tenham chegado a horas com este trânsito.
items.en: I doubt they arrived on time with this traffic.
items.note: doubt about a completed action: tenham chegado

items.hr: Se tivesse sabido, teria vindo mais cedo.
items.en: If I had known, I would have come earlier.
items.note: the unreal past: tivesse + participle, teria + participle

items.hr: Se tivesses estudado, tinhas passado no exame.
items.en: If you had studied, you would have passed the exam.
items.note: spoken pt-PT often replaces teria with tinha

items.hr: Foi pena que não tivesses vindo à festa.
items.en: It was a shame you hadn't come to the party.
items.note: past trigger + earlier action → tivesse + participle

items.hr: Quando tiveres acabado o relatório, avisa-me.
items.en: When you have finished the report, let me know.
items.note: even the future subjunctive has a compound form: tiver + participle

items.hr: Talvez tenham perdido o comboio das nove.
items.en: Maybe they missed the nine o'clock train.
items.note: talvez + conjuntivo — here the compound form for a past guess

activities.title: Olhar para trás
activities.intro: Pick the right compound subjunctive for hopes, doubts and regrets about the past.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Espero que tudo ___ corrido bem na viagem.'
questions.explanation: A present trigger (espero que) looking back at a completed action takes tenha + participle.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Duvido que eles ___ chegado a tempo — havia greve dos comboios.' (ter)
questions.explanation: Doubt about a finished action: tenham chegado, the perfeito do conjuntivo for 'eles'.

questions.prompt: Complete the third conditional: 'Se tivesses estudado, ___ passado no exame.'
questions.explanation: Unreal past pattern: se + tivesse feito, teria feito — in speech 'tinhas passado' also works.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Se eu ___ sabido da festa, tinha aparecido.' (ter)
questions.explanation: The if-clause of an unreal past takes tivesse + participle: se eu tivesse sabido.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quando ___ acabado o trabalho, vamos jantar fora.'
questions.explanation: 'When we have finished' = compound FUTURE subjunctive: quando tivermos acabado.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It was a pity you didn't come.'
questions.explanation: Past trigger (foi pena que) + earlier action → mais-que-perfeito do conjuntivo: tivesses vindo.

activities.title: Depois da entrevista
activities.intro: Yesterday you had a job interview. A friend wants to know everything. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, como correu a entrevista de ontem?
lines.en: So, how did yesterday's interview go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Acho que correu bem, mas duvido que tenham gostado da minha resposta sobre o salário.
lines.en: I think it went well, but I doubt they liked my answer about the salary.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não digas isso! Espero que tenhas causado boa impressão.
lines.en: Don't say that! I hope you made a good impression.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Veremos. Se tivesse preparado melhor as perguntas finais, teria estado mais calmo.
lines.en: We'll see. If I had prepared the final questions better, I would have been calmer.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Quando tiveres recebido a resposta, conta-me logo, sim?
lines.en: When you've received the answer, tell me right away, okay?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Claro. E se não tivesse sido por ti, nem me tinha candidatado — obrigado!
lines.en: Of course. And if it hadn't been for you, I wouldn't even have applied — thank you!

days.title: Sociedade e política
days.objective: Discuss elections, rights, protests and the news in Portugal with confidence.
days.paretoFocus: Eleições, governo, manifestação, direitos and orçamento are the words every Portuguese news bulletin repeats — learn them once and the telejornal opens up.
drills: Summarise a real news story from Portugal in three sentences using at least three of today's words.
Explain to a foreigner what happened on 25 de Abril de 1974 in two sentences.
Give your opinion on voting: 'Na minha opinião, votar é... porque...'

items: Recall: what is the Portuguese parliament called?
Say three words from the courtroom: tribunal, juiz, arguido.
Translate: 'Thousands of people took part in the demonstration.'

activities.title: O país em debate
activities.intro: The vocabulary of Portuguese public life: elections, parliament, protests and rights — the backbone of every news story.

items.hr: As eleições legislativas realizam-se no domingo e as sondagens preveem grande abstenção.
items.en: The parliamentary elections take place on Sunday and the polls predict high abstention.
items.note: a sondagem = opinion poll; a abstenção = abstention

items.hr: O primeiro-ministro apresentou o orçamento na Assembleia da República.
items.en: The prime minister presented the budget in the Assembly of the Republic.
items.note: a Assembleia da República = the Portuguese parliament

items.hr: Os deputados aprovaram a lei por larga maioria.
items.en: The MPs passed the law by a large majority.
items.note: aprovar uma lei = to pass a law; o deputado = MP

items.hr: Milhares de pessoas participaram na manifestação pelos direitos humanos.
items.en: Thousands of people took part in the demonstration for human rights.
items.note: a manifestação = protest march; manifestar-se = to demonstrate

items.hr: A liberdade de expressão e a liberdade de imprensa são pilares da democracia.
items.en: Freedom of expression and freedom of the press are pillars of democracy.
items.note: a imprensa = the press

items.hr: Os sindicatos convocaram uma greve geral para protestar contra o custo de vida.
items.en: The trade unions called a general strike to protest against the cost of living.
items.note: o sindicato = trade union; a greve geral = general strike

items.hr: O 25 de Abril de 1974 pôs fim à ditadura e trouxe a democracia a Portugal.
items.en: The 25th of April 1974 ended the dictatorship and brought democracy to Portugal.
items.note: a Revolução dos Cravos — the Carnation Revolution, a national holiday

activities.title: Nas notícias
activities.intro: Test the words that carry every Portuguese news bulletin and political conversation.

questions.prompt: Where do Portuguese 'deputados' sit and vote on laws?
questions.explanation: The Assembleia da República in Lisbon is the national parliament where MPs pass laws.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'As ___ indicam que a abstenção vai ser elevada.' (opinion polls)
questions.explanation: 'A sondagem' is an opinion poll — plural here: as sondagens indicam.

questions.prompt: What happened on 25 de Abril de 1974?
options: a revolução que pôs fim à ditadura
a entrada de Portugal na União Europeia
a primeira greve geral do século
questions.answer: a revolução que pôs fim à ditadura
questions.explanation: The Carnation Revolution ended the Estado Novo dictatorship and opened the way to democracy.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Os trabalhadores fizeram uma ___ geral contra a subida dos preços.' (strike)
questions.explanation: 'A greve geral' is a general strike, called by the trade unions ('sindicatos').

questions.prompt: What does 'manifestar-se' mean?
questions.explanation: Manifestar-se = to protest publicly; the noun is 'a manifestação'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The MPs passed the law yesterday.'
questions.explanation: Subject + verb + object: os deputados aprovaram a lei = the MPs passed the law.

activities.title: Conversa de café sobre política
activities.intro: The general strike is all over the news. Discuss it with a friend at the café — play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Viste as notícias? A greve geral parou os transportes todos.
lines.en: Did you see the news? The general strike stopped all the transport.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vi. Embora compreenda as reivindicações, fiquei duas horas à espera do autocarro.
lines.en: I did. Although I understand the demands, I waited two hours for the bus.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pois, mas com este custo de vida, as pessoas têm razão para protestar.
lines.en: Right, but with this cost of living, people have reason to protest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sem dúvida. E as sondagens dizem que o governo está a perder apoio.
lines.en: No doubt. And the polls say the government is losing support.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se as eleições fossem hoje, a abstenção era enorme.
lines.en: If the elections were today, abstention would be huge.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez. Quando o orçamento for discutido na Assembleia, vamos ver quem cede.
lines.en: Maybe. When the budget is debated in the Assembly, we'll see who gives in.

days.title: Conectores de argumentação
days.objective: Build a structured argument with embora, contudo, caso, desde que, por conseguinte and apesar de.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners literally count your connectors: six of them, each with its own mood rule, lift an essay or debate from B1 to B2 on their own.
drills: Defend an opinion in four sentences using embora, contudo, por conseguinte and desde que — one each.
Say the same concession two ways: 'Embora seja caro...' / 'Apesar de ser caro...'
Warn a colleague with 'caso': 'Caso surjam problemas...', 'Caso não possas vir...'

items: Recall: which mood follows embora, and which follows apesar de?
Say two contrast connectors that take the plain indicative.
Translate: 'You can use the car, provided you return it by eight.'

activities.title: Construir o argumento
activities.intro: Each connector family has its own grammar: contrast takes the indicative, concession the conjuntivo, and apesar de wants the infinitive.

items.hr: Embora haja riscos, o projeto deve avançar.
items.en: Although there are risks, the project should go ahead.
items.note: embora + conjuntivo, always

items.hr: O plano é caro; contudo, vale a pena.
items.en: The plan is expensive; however, it is worth it.
items.note: contudo, no entanto, porém + indicativo — full-sentence contrast

items.hr: Caso surjam problemas, contacte-nos de imediato.
items.en: Should problems arise, contact us immediately.
items.note: caso + presente do conjuntivo

items.hr: Podes usar o carro, desde que o devolvas até às oito.
items.en: You can use the car, provided you return it by eight.
items.note: desde que (provided that) + conjuntivo

items.hr: Os custos subiram; por conseguinte, os preços vão aumentar.
items.en: Costs went up; consequently, prices will rise.
items.note: por conseguinte, portanto, por isso — consequence + indicativo

items.hr: Apesar de estarmos cansados, acabámos o relatório.
items.en: Despite being tired, we finished the report.
items.note: apesar de + infinitivo pessoal — the prepositional twin of embora

items.hr: Visto que ninguém se opôs, a proposta foi aprovada.
items.en: Since no one objected, the proposal was approved.
items.note: visto que, uma vez que, dado que — cause + indicativo

activities.title: O conector certo
activities.intro: Match each connector to its meaning and its mood.

questions.prompt: Which of these connectors takes the conjuntivo?
questions.explanation: Concession with embora (and ainda que) demands the conjuntivo; the other three take the indicative.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ haja provas, o arguido continua a negar tudo.' (Although)
questions.explanation: Embora + conjuntivo (haja) introduces the concession 'although there is evidence'.

questions.prompt: Choose the connector: 'Podes ficar cá esta noite, ___ não faças barulho.' (provided that)
questions.explanation: 'Desde que' + conjuntivo sets a condition: provided that you don't make noise.

questions.prompt: Complete the consequence connector: 'As vendas caíram; por ___, teremos de reduzir despesas.'
questions.explanation: 'Por conseguinte' = consequently — a formal alternative to 'portanto' and 'por isso'.

questions.prompt: After 'apesar de' you use...
questions.explanation: Apesar de is a preposition, so it takes the infinitive: apesar de estarmos cansados = embora estejamos cansados.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Although it's expensive, the plan is worth it.'
questions.explanation: Embora + conjuntivo (seja) opens the concession; the main clause follows in the indicative.

activities.title: Debate sobre o teletrabalho
activities.intro: A friendly debate about remote work. Play 'Me' and argue with proper connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Na minha opinião, o teletrabalho devia ser um direito de todos.
lines.en: In my opinion, remote work should be a right for everyone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo em parte. Contudo, nem todas as profissões o permitem.
lines.en: I partly agree. However, not all professions allow it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Claro, mas, desde que o trabalho seja feito, que importa o lugar?
lines.en: Of course, but provided the work gets done, what does the place matter?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Embora isso seja verdade, muita gente sente falta do contacto humano.
lines.en: Although that's true, many people miss the human contact.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Apesar de trabalhar em casa há dois anos, nunca me senti isolado.
lines.en: Despite working from home for two years, I've never felt isolated.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Visto que estamos os dois convencidos, é melhor mudarmos de assunto!
lines.en: Since we're both convinced, it's better for us to change the subject!

days.title: Registo formal e nominalização
days.objective: Write a formal letter or requerimento with the right formulas, and turn verbs into nouns for formal style.
days.paretoFocus: Half a dozen fixed formulas — Exmo. Senhor, venho por este meio, em anexo, agradeço desde já — plus nominalization carry every official email, complaint and requerimento in Portugal.
drills: Draft the skeleton of a formal email out loud: opening, purpose, attachment, thanks, closing.
Nominalize five verbs: chegar, decidir, aumentar, abrir, enviar → a chegada, a decisão, o aumento, a abertura, o envio.
Rewrite informally-formally: 'Quero pedir uma declaração' → 'Venho por este meio solicitar uma declaração.'

items: Recall: how do you open a formal letter to a man you don't know?
Say two formal closings, from less to more formal.
Translate formally: 'The arrival of the flight is scheduled for ten o'clock.'

activities.title: Escrever como deve ser
activities.intro: The fixed formulas of Portuguese formal writing, plus its favourite trick: replacing verbs with nouns.

items.hr: Exmo. Senhor Diretor, venho por este meio solicitar uma declaração de matrícula.
items.en: Dear Sir (Director), I hereby request an enrolment certificate.
items.note: Exmo. Senhor / Exma. Senhora — the standard formal salutation

items.hr: Junto envio, em anexo, o requerimento e os documentos solicitados.
items.en: Please find enclosed the application form and the requested documents.
items.note: em anexo = attached; o requerimento = formal written request

items.hr: Agradeço desde já a atenção dispensada.
items.en: Thank you in advance for your attention.
items.note: a fixed formula — learn it whole

items.hr: Com os melhores cumprimentos, / Atentamente,
items.en: Best regards, / Yours faithfully,
items.note: two standard closings; Atentamente is the more formal

items.hr: Chegar → a chegada: a chegada do voo está prevista para as dez.
items.en: To arrive → the arrival: the arrival of the flight is scheduled for ten.
items.note: nominalização — formal writing prefers nouns to verbs

items.hr: Decidir → a decisão: a decisão do júri será anunciada amanhã.
items.en: To decide → the decision: the jury's decision will be announced tomorrow.
items.note: aumentar → o aumento, abrir → a abertura, enviar → o envio

items.hr: Solicita-se o envio dos documentos com a maior brevidade possível.
items.en: You are requested to send the documents as soon as possible.
items.note: impersonal se + nominalization — peak formal register

activities.title: Do rascunho ao requerimento
activities.intro: Pick the right formula and the right noun for each formal situation.

questions.prompt: How do you open a formal letter to a (male) director you don't know?
questions.explanation: 'Exmo. Senhor' (Excelentíssimo Senhor) is the standard formal salutation; the others are far too informal.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal formula: 'Venho por este ___ solicitar informações sobre as propinas.'
questions.explanation: 'Venho por este meio' = I hereby — the classic opening of a formal request.

questions.prompt: What is the noun formed from 'decidir'?
questions.explanation: Decidir nominalizes to 'a decisão' — formal style prefers 'a decisão do júri' over 'o júri decidiu'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun from 'chegar': 'A ___ do comboio está prevista para as nove.'
questions.explanation: Chegar → a chegada; nominalization turns the event into a noun, typical of announcements.

questions.prompt: Which closing suits a formal complaint letter?
questions.explanation: 'Atentamente' is the formal closing; the other three belong to messages between friends.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Thank you in advance for your attention.'
questions.explanation: A fixed formula meaning 'thank you in advance for your attention' — memorise it as one block.

activities.title: Ajuda com o requerimento
activities.intro: A colleague helps you draft a formal request to the university. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Precisas de ajuda com esse requerimento para a universidade?
lines.en: Do you need help with that application to the university?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Preciso! Como é que começo? 'Olá' não me parece adequado.
lines.en: I do! How do I start? 'Hi' doesn't seem appropriate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Nem pensar. Escreve 'Exmo. Senhor Presidente do Conselho' e depois 'venho por este meio solicitar...'.
lines.en: No way. Write 'Dear Mr President of the Council' and then 'I hereby request...'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E no fim? Posso pôr só 'obrigado'?
lines.en: And at the end? Can I just put 'thanks'?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Melhor: 'Agradeço desde já a atenção dispensada' e fechas com 'Com os melhores cumprimentos'.
lines.en: Better: 'Thank you in advance for your attention' and you close with 'Best regards'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Perfeito. E os documentos vão em anexo, certo?
lines.en: Perfect. And the documents go attached, right?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Certo. E em vez de 'quando o semestre abrir', escreve 'a abertura do semestre' — fica mais formal.
lines.en: Right. And instead of 'when the semester opens', write 'the opening of the semester' — it's more formal.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nominalização, já percebi. Vou impressionar os serviços académicos!
lines.en: Nominalization, got it. I'm going to impress the academic services!

days.title: Mesóclise
days.objective: Recognise and form mesóclise — dir-te-ei, far-lhe-ia — and know when formal writing demands it.
days.paretoFocus: You will rarely say it, but banks, courts and newspapers write it daily: one pattern (stem + pronoun + ending) makes every formal letter instantly readable.
drills: Assemble three forms out loud: direi + te → dir-te-ei; faria + lhe → far-lhe-ia; contaremos + vos → contar-vos-emos.
Cancel each of them with 'não': não te direi, não lhe faria, não vos contaremos.
Translate each mesóclise into everyday speech: dir-te-ei → vou dizer-te / digo-te amanhã.

items: Recall: with which two tenses does the pronoun go inside the verb?
Say what happens to mesóclise after 'não' or 'nunca'.
Translate formally: 'I shall tell you the truth.' — and then say it as you would in conversation.

activities.title: O pronome no meio do verbo
activities.intro: With the simple future and the conditional, the clitic slips inside the verb: dizer + te + ei → dir-te-ei. Learn it to read formal Portuguese, not to chat.

items.hr: Dir-te-ei a verdade quando chegar a altura certa.
items.en: I shall tell you the truth when the right time comes.
items.note: dizer + te + ei → dir-te-ei: infinitive stem + pronoun + future ending

items.hr: Far-lhe-ia esse favor com todo o gosto.
items.en: I would do him/her that favour with great pleasure.
items.note: the conditional works the same way: fazer + lhe + ia

items.hr: Contar-vos-emos tudo em breve.
items.en: We shall tell you all everything soon.
items.note: typical of official letters and announcements

items.hr: Dá-lo-emos ao cliente amanhã.
items.en: We shall give it to the client tomorrow.
items.note: o/a after the stem's final -r become the l-forms: dar + o → dá-lo

items.hr: Não te direi nada até teres a certeza.
items.en: I won't tell you anything until you are sure.
items.note: any proclisis trigger (não, nunca, que...) cancels mesóclise completely

items.hr: Na conversa, ninguém usa mesóclise: vou dizer-te amanhã, digo-te já.
items.en: In conversation, nobody uses mesóclise: I'll tell you tomorrow, I'll tell you right now.
items.note: speech restructures with ir + infinitive or the present

activities.title: Montar e desmontar o verbo
activities.intro: Build mesóclise forms, cancel them with proclisis triggers, and translate them into everyday speech.

questions.prompt: In formal writing, where does the pronoun go with the simple future?
questions.explanation: Future and conditional split around the clitic: stem + pronoun + ending — that is mesóclise.

questions.prompt: 'Fazer + lhe + ia' gives...
questions.explanation: Dizer, fazer and trazer use the shortened stems dir-, far-, trar- in the future and conditional.

questions.prompt: Complete the mesóclise: 'Dir-___-ei tudo amanhã, prometo.' (to you, informal singular)
questions.explanation: The clitic 'te' sits between the stem dir- and the future ending -ei: dir-te-ei.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct after the negation?
questions.explanation: A proclisis trigger like 'não' cancels mesóclise: the pronoun moves before the verb.

questions.prompt: In everyday speech, instead of 'dir-te-ei' people say...
questions.explanation: Conversation restructures with ir + infinitive (vou dizer-te) or the present (digo-te amanhã).

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I would do you that favour with great pleasure.'
questions.explanation: The mesóclise block far-lhe-ia counts as one verb: I would do him/her that favour gladly.

activities.title: A carta do banco
activities.intro: A friend got a letter from the bank full of strange verb forms. Play 'Me' and explain mesóclise.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Recebi uma carta do banco que diz: 'Informá-lo-emos logo que possível.' Que forma é esta?
lines.en: I got a letter from the bank that says: 'We shall inform you as soon as possible.' What form is this?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É mesóclise: no futuro, o pronome vai dentro do verbo — informar + o + emos.
lines.en: It's mesóclise: in the future tense, the pronoun goes inside the verb — informar + o + emos.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que estranho! E as pessoas falam assim?
lines.en: How strange! And do people talk like that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não! Na conversa dizemos 'vamos informá-lo' ou 'depois dizemos-te'.
lines.en: No! In conversation we say 'we're going to inform you' or 'we'll tell you later'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então só aparece na escrita formal?
lines.en: So it only shows up in formal writing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sim, em cartas, na lei e nos jornais. E com 'não' desaparece: 'não o informaremos'.
lines.en: Yes, in letters, in law and in newspapers. And with 'não' it disappears: 'we shall not inform you'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Percebido. Dir-to-ei... quer dizer, digo-te já: prefiro a versão falada!
lines.en: Understood. I-shall-tell-it-to-you... I mean, I'll tell you right now: I prefer the spoken version!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Toda a gente prefere. Mas agora já consegues ler as cartas do banco sem susto!
lines.en: Everybody does. But now you can read the bank's letters without a fright!

days.title: Cultura e artes
days.objective: Talk about fado, literature, heritage and shows — and give a short cultural review.
days.paretoFocus: Fado, património, obra-prima, estreia and azulejo are Portugal's cultural calling cards: they open every museum visit, concert review and conversation about the arts.
drills: Review the last film, concert or exhibition you saw in three sentences: what, where, your verdict.
Explain to a visitor what fado is and where to hear it, in two sentences.
Name one obra-prima of Portuguese architecture and say why it deserves the title.

items: Recall: which instrument accompanies a fadista?
Say three words from the theatre: palco, plateia, estreia.
Translate: 'The Belém Tower is a masterpiece of Manueline architecture.'

activities.title: O cartão de visita cultural
activities.intro: From fado houses to tiled train stations: the vocabulary of Portuguese culture and the arts.

items.hr: O fado foi declarado Património Imaterial da Humanidade pela UNESCO em 2011.
items.en: Fado was declared Intangible Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO in 2011.
items.note: o património = heritage; a big word in Portuguese cultural life

items.hr: A fadista subiu ao palco acompanhada pela guitarra portuguesa.
items.en: The fado singer went on stage accompanied by the Portuguese guitar.
items.note: a guitarra portuguesa — the pear-shaped twelve-string guitar of fado

items.hr: O Mosteiro dos Jerónimos é uma obra-prima do estilo manuelino.
items.en: The Jerónimos Monastery is a masterpiece of the Manueline style.
items.note: a obra-prima = masterpiece; o estilo manuelino = Portuguese late Gothic

items.hr: Os azulejos contam a história de Portugal nas paredes das estações.
items.en: The glazed tiles tell the history of Portugal on the walls of the stations.
items.note: o azulejo — see São Bento station in Porto for the finest azulejaria

items.hr: José Saramago ganhou o Prémio Nobel da Literatura em 1998.
items.en: José Saramago won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1998.
items.note: the only Portuguese-language Nobel in literature so far

items.hr: A estreia da peça esgotou e a plateia aplaudiu de pé.
items.en: The play's premiere sold out and the audience gave a standing ovation.
items.note: a estreia = premiere; a plateia = the audience/stalls

items.hr: Fui à exposição de escultura na galeria e adorei o restauro dos vitrais.
items.en: I went to the sculpture exhibition at the gallery and loved the restoration of the stained-glass windows.
items.note: a exposição = exhibition; o restauro = restoration

activities.title: Noite de cultura
activities.intro: Test the words of Portuguese music, literature, heritage and the stage.

questions.prompt: Which instrument traditionally accompanies fado?
questions.explanation: The pear-shaped guitarra portuguesa gives fado its unmistakable sound.

questions.prompt: Who won the Prémio Nobel da Literatura in 1998?
questions.explanation: Saramago remains the only Portuguese-language Nobel laureate in literature; Pessoa and the others predate the prize's reach.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'O fado é ___ Imaterial da Humanidade desde 2011.' (heritage)
questions.explanation: 'Património Imaterial da Humanidade' is the UNESCO title fado carries since 2011.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for masterpiece: 'A Torre de Belém é uma ___ da arquitetura manuelina.'
questions.explanation: 'A obra-prima' = the masterpiece; the plural is 'obras-primas'.

questions.prompt: 'A estreia' of a play is...
questions.explanation: A estreia = the premiere; the final rehearsal is 'o ensaio geral' and backstage is 'os bastidores'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The fado singer sang on stage until midnight.'
questions.explanation: A fadista sings 'no palco' (on stage); 'até à meia-noite' closes the sentence with the time.

activities.title: Depois da casa de fado
activities.intro: Last night you went to a fado house in Alfama. Plan a cultural weekend with a friend — play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, gostaste do espetáculo de fado ontem à noite?
lines.en: So, did you enjoy the fado show last night?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Adorei! Quando a fadista começou a cantar, a plateia ficou em silêncio absoluto.
lines.en: I loved it! When the fado singer started to sing, the audience fell completely silent.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a guitarra portuguesa? Não há som mais bonito.
lines.en: And the Portuguese guitar? There's no more beautiful sound.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo. Agora percebo porque é que o fado é património da humanidade.
lines.en: I agree. Now I understand why fado is heritage of humanity.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se quiseres, no sábado vamos à exposição de azulejaria no museu.
lines.en: If you want, on Saturday we can go to the tile-art exhibition at the museum.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Boa ideia! Dizem que o restauro dos painéis do século dezoito é uma obra-prima.
lines.en: Good idea! They say the restoration of the eighteenth-century panels is a masterpiece.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Depois podíamos passar na feira do livro — ando à procura de um romance histórico do Saramago.
lines.en: Afterwards we could stop by the book fair — I'm looking for a historical novel by Saramago.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. Embora o fim de semana seja curto, vamos enchê-lo de cultura!
lines.en: Deal. Although the weekend is short, we're going to fill it with culture!

days.title: Formação de palavras
days.objective: Build and decode whole word families using the prefixes des-, in- and re- and the suffixes -mente, -ção, -eiro, -oso and -vel.
days.paretoFocus: One root plus a handful of affixes multiplies your vocabulary for free: feliz alone unlocks infeliz, felicidade and felizmente — and B2 texts are full of derived words you can now decode instead of memorising.
drills: Negate three adjectives out loud with the right prefix: possível, legal, real → impossível, ilegal, irreal.
Build the full family of 'feliz' from memory: infeliz, a felicidade, felizmente, infelizmente.
Turn five verbs into -ção nouns: formar, informar, traduzir, organizar, inovar.

items: Recall: which negative prefix goes with 'legal' and which with 'real'?
Say four members of the 'feliz' family without looking.
Translate: 'Unfortunately, the meeting was cancelled.'

activities.title: Uma raiz, muitas palavras
activities.intro: The affixes that turn one known word into four or five — the cheapest vocabulary you will ever acquire.

items.hr: Liguei o ecrã, mas depois desliguei-o para poupar bateria.
items.en: I switched the screen on, but then I switched it off to save battery.
items.note: des- reverses a verb: ligar → desligar, fazer → desfazer, confiar → desconfiar

items.hr: É impossível, e até ilegal, estacionar em cima do passeio.
items.en: It's impossible, and even illegal, to park on the pavement.
items.note: in- becomes im- before p/b, i- before l, ir- before r: impossível, ilegal, irreal

items.hr: O texto estava confuso, por isso decidi refazê-lo e reler tudo.
items.en: The text was confusing, so I decided to redo it and reread everything.
items.note: re- = again: refazer, reler, reabrir, reutilizar

items.hr: Felizmente, a formação decorreu sem problemas.
items.en: Fortunately, the training session went ahead without problems.
items.note: -mente builds adverbs from the feminine adjective; -ção turns verbs into nouns (formar → formação)

items.hr: O sapateiro da esquina arranja malas e cintos também.
items.en: The shoemaker on the corner fixes bags and belts too.
items.note: -eiro/-eira gives professions and holders: sapato → sapateiro, pão → padeiro, cinza → cinzeiro

items.hr: Foi um dia chuvoso, mas a viagem foi maravilhosa.
items.en: It was a rainy day, but the trip was wonderful.
items.note: -oso/-osa turns nouns into adjectives: chuva → chuvoso, perigo → perigoso, maravilha → maravilhoso

items.hr: A letra dele é ilegível, mas o recado era compreensível.
items.en: His handwriting is illegible, but the note was understandable.
items.note: -vel = '-able': compreender → compreensível; stack affixes: in + legível → ilegível

items.hr: A família de 'feliz': infeliz, a felicidade, felizmente, infelizmente.
items.en: The family of 'feliz' (happy): unhappy, happiness, happily, unfortunately.
items.note: learn families, not single words — every B2 text becomes easier to guess

activities.title: Fábrica de palavras
activities.intro: Build the missing member of each word family — prefix or suffix, your choice must be the real Portuguese form.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct negative of 'possível'?
questions.explanation: Before p or b the prefix in- becomes im-: impossível, impaciente, imbatível.

questions.prompt: Choose the noun formed from 'informar': 'A ___ chegou tarde demais.'
questions.explanation: -ção builds action nouns from verbs: informar → informação, formar → formação.

questions.prompt: Complete with the adverb built from 'infeliz': '___, o voo foi cancelado por causa do nevoeiro.'
questions.explanation: -mente attaches to the adjective: infeliz → infelizmente (unfortunately).

questions.prompt: The road is dangerous. Which adjective comes from 'perigo'?
questions.explanation: -oso turns a noun into an adjective: perigo → perigoso, chuva → chuvoso.

questions.prompt: Complete with the -vel adjective from 'aceitar': 'A proposta não é ___ para o sindicato.'
questions.explanation: -vel means '-able': aceitar → aceitável (acceptable), compreender → compreensível.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Happiness cannot be bought with money.'
questions.explanation: Feliz → a felicidade: the -dade noun of the family; 'não' triggers próclise, so the pronoun se comes before the verb.

activities.title: O jogo das famílias
activities.intro: Studying in a café in Coimbra, a friend challenges you to a word-family game. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vamos jogar às famílias de palavras? Eu digo a raiz e tu constróis o resto.
lines.en: Shall we play word families? I say the root and you build the rest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pode ser! Começa com uma fácil.
lines.en: Alright! Start with an easy one.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: 'Feliz'. Quero três palavras da mesma família.
lines.en: 'Feliz'. I want three words from the same family.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Infeliz, a felicidade e felizmente. Foi facílimo!
lines.en: Unhappy, happiness and happily. That was super easy!

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Agora uma mais difícil: 'ler'.
lines.en: Now a harder one: 'to read'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Reler, ilegível e... a leitura! Este jogo devia chamar-se 'multiplicação de vocabulário'.
lines.en: Reread, illegible and... reading! This game should be called 'vocabulary multiplication'.

days.title: Ciência e saúde
days.objective: Understand health and science texts and handle appointments, tests and treatments in European Portuguese.
days.paretoFocus: Words like investigação, vacina, tratamento and análises unlock the health pages of any Portuguese newspaper — and every conversation at the centro de saúde.
drills: Describe out loud your last visit to a doctor: a consulta, as análises, o tratamento.
Summarise this imaginary headline in one Portuguese sentence: 'Novo tratamento reduz os casos graves em Portugal.'
Explain in two sentences why 'a prevenção é mais barata do que o tratamento'.

items: Recall: what do you call the blood tests the doctor orders — and in what state must you arrive to do them?
Say the difference between 'uma epidemia' and 'uma pandemia'.
Translate: 'The family doctor referred me to a specialist appointment.'

activities.title: Da investigação à consulta
activities.intro: The science-and-health core of B2: research and trials in the news, appointments and tests in your own life.

items.hr: A investigação sobre o cancro recebeu novos fundos europeus.
items.en: Cancer research received new European funding.
items.note: a investigação = research, the usual European term in science news

items.hr: O novo medicamento está na última fase dos ensaios clínicos.
items.en: The new drug is in the final phase of clinical trials.
items.note: o ensaio clínico = clinical trial

items.hr: Depois da vacina, o corpo desenvolve imunidade contra o vírus.
items.en: After the vaccine, the body develops immunity against the virus.
items.note: a vacina / vacinar; a imunidade = immunity

items.hr: O médico de família encaminhou-me para uma consulta de especialidade.
items.en: The family doctor referred me to a specialist appointment.
items.note: consulta de especialidade = specialist consultation; in the SNS you go via referral (encaminhar)

items.hr: O médico pediu-me análises ao sangue; fazem-se de manhã, em jejum.
items.en: The doctor ordered blood tests for me; they are done in the morning, on an empty stomach.
items.note: as análises (usually plural) = medical tests; em jejum = fasting

items.hr: O tratamento é eficaz, mas tem alguns efeitos secundários.
items.en: The treatment is effective, but it has some side effects.
items.note: o tratamento = treatment; efeito secundário is the European term for side effect

items.hr: Um diagnóstico precoce aumenta muito as hipóteses de cura.
items.en: An early diagnosis greatly increases the chances of a cure.
items.note: o diagnóstico; hipóteses here = chances

items.hr: O centro de saúde organiza um rastreio gratuito do cancro da pele.
items.en: The health centre is organising a free skin cancer screening.
items.note: o rastreio = health screening — a very European word

activities.title: Ler saúde e ciência
activities.intro: Health and science the way B2 texts serve them — pick the meaning the context demands.

questions.prompt: In a Portuguese science article, what is 'a investigação'?
questions.explanation: In science contexts 'investigação' means research; 'investigador' is a researcher.

questions.prompt: Your GP says: 'Vou encaminhá-lo para uma consulta de especialidade.' What happens next?
options: You will see a specialist doctor.
You will be admitted to hospital immediately.
You will repeat the same appointment with the GP.
You will change your family doctor.
questions.answer: You will see a specialist doctor.
questions.explanation: 'Encaminhar para uma consulta de especialidade' = to refer to a specialist consultation, the normal SNS route.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for medical tests: 'As ___ ao sangue fazem-se de manhã, em jejum.'
questions.explanation: 'As análises' (usually plural) are the lab tests; blood tests are done fasting — 'em jejum'.

questions.prompt: 'O medicamento tem efeitos secundários.' What does this mean?
options: The medicine must be taken twice.
The medicine is only for minor illnesses.
questions.explanation: 'Efeito secundário' is the European Portuguese term for a side effect.

questions.prompt: Complete with the word for vaccine: 'A ___ contra a gripe chega aos centros de saúde em outubro.'
questions.explanation: 'A vacina' = the vaccine; the flu campaign starts every autumn in Portugal.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'An early diagnosis increases the chances of a cure.'
questions.explanation: Adjective after the noun (diagnóstico precoce); 'hipóteses de cura' = chances of a cure.

activities.title: Os resultados das análises
activities.intro: At the centro de saúde, the doctor goes over your test results. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Já recebi os resultados das suas análises.
lines.en: Good afternoon. I've received the results of your tests.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E então, doutora? Está tudo bem?
lines.en: So, doctor? Is everything alright?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: No geral, sim, mas o colesterol está um pouco alto. Vou receitar-lhe um tratamento ligeiro.
lines.en: In general, yes, but your cholesterol is a bit high. I'm going to prescribe you a mild treatment.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Esse medicamento tem efeitos secundários?
lines.en: Does that medicine have side effects?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pode causar algum cansaço nos primeiros dias, nada de grave. E, por precaução, vou encaminhá-lo para uma consulta de especialidade.
lines.en: It may cause some tiredness in the first few days, nothing serious. And, as a precaution, I'll refer you to a specialist appointment.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Obrigado, doutora. Devo mudar alguma coisa na alimentação?
lines.en: Thank you, doctor. Should I change anything in my diet?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Menos gorduras e mais exercício. Lembre-se: a prevenção é o melhor tratamento.
lines.en: Less fat and more exercise. Remember: prevention is the best treatment.

days.title: Registo coloquial europeu
days.objective: Recognise and use everyday colloquial European Portuguese — cá/lá, a gente, se calhar, pois, pronto — and know exactly when to switch it off.
days.paretoFocus: Cá, lá, pois, pronto and se calhar fill every real conversation in Portugal; using them well makes you sound native, and knowing to drop them in a formal letter is what B2 register control means.
drills: Say three colloquial classics out loud with feeling: 'anda cá', 'deixa lá', 'vem cá jantar'.
Express the same idea twice: 'Se calhar vou' (colloquial, indicative) and 'Talvez vá' (neutral, conjuntivo).
React out loud with 'pois', 'pronto' and 'fixe' to three pieces of imaginary news from a friend.

items: Recall: which verb form goes with 'a gente' — and what does the expression mean?
Say two colloquial words for 'cool/nice' and their neutral equivalents.
Name two situations where colloquial register would cost you marks or respect.

activities.title: Como se fala no café
activities.intro: The spoken flavour of Portugal — learn it to understand real conversations and to sound natural among friends, never in formal writing.

items.hr: Anda cá! E deixa lá isso, não foi nada.
items.en: Come here! And never mind that, it was nothing.
items.note: cá/lá replace aqui/ali in relaxed speech: anda cá, vem cá jantar, deixa lá

items.hr: A gente encontra-se lá às oito, está bem?
items.en: We'll meet there at eight, okay?
items.note: a gente + 3rd-person-SINGULAR verb = informal 'we'; keep nós for neutral and formal use

items.hr: Se calhar fico em casa este fim de semana.
items.en: Maybe I'll stay home this weekend.
items.note: se calhar + indicative is the everyday cousin of talvez + conjuntivo (talvez fique)

items.hr: Pois é, tens toda a razão. Pronto, está decidido.
items.en: That's right, you're absolutely right. Right then, it's settled.
items.note: pois agrees, pronto closes a topic — the two great fillers of Portugal

items.hr: Não falava inglês, mas desenrascou-se muito bem na entrevista.
items.en: He didn't speak English, but he improvised his way through the interview just fine.
items.note: desenrascar-se = to improvise a way out of a fix; o desenrascanço is famously a national talent

items.hr: A tua mota nova é mesmo gira! E o concerto de ontem foi fixe.
items.en: Your new motorbike is really nice! And yesterday's concert was cool.
items.note: giro/gira and fixe = informal 'nice/cool'; fixe never changes form

items.hr: O exame foi bué difícil, quase ninguém acabou a tempo.
items.en: The exam was really hard, almost nobody finished in time.
items.note: bué = very (youth slang, from Angola); great to understand, never write it

items.hr: Numa carta formal, nada disto entra: escreve 'talvez', 'muito' e 'nós'.
items.en: In a formal letter, none of this goes in: write 'talvez', 'muito' and 'nós'.
items.note: register control is the B2 skill: colloquial with friends, neutral or formal everywhere else

activities.title: Falar como cá se fala
activities.intro: Choose the natural colloquial form — and spot where colloquial language does not belong.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct verb with 'a gente': 'A gente ___ ter contigo ao café às oito.'
questions.explanation: 'A gente' means 'we' but grammatically takes the 3rd person singular: a gente vai, a gente faz.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is correct?
options: Se calhar vou ao Porto amanhã.
Se calhar vá ao Porto amanhã.
questions.answer: Se calhar vou ao Porto amanhã.
questions.explanation: 'Se calhar' takes the plain indicative; its formal twin 'talvez' demands the conjuntivo: talvez vá.

questions.prompt: Where does 'bué' NOT belong?
options: numa mensagem ao teu colega de casa
questions.explanation: 'Bué' is youth slang: fine among friends, a register error in any formal text or exam letter.

questions.prompt: Complete with the very Portuguese verb for improvising a way out: 'Não tínhamos plano nenhum, mas conseguimos ___-nos.'
questions.explanation: Desenrascar-se = to get by, to improvise a solution — the celebrated 'desenrascanço' in verb form.

questions.prompt: Complete the colloquial agreement meaning 'that's right': '— O trânsito em Lisboa está impossível. — ___ é, nem me digas nada.'
questions.explanation: 'Pois é' agrees with what was just said; on its own, 'pois' is the all-purpose Portuguese nod.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Maybe we'll watch the game tonight.'
questions.explanation: Three colloquial markers in one line: se calhar + indicative, a gente + singular verb, and 'logo à noite' = later tonight.

activities.title: Jantar cá em casa
activities.intro: A tired friend calls after work and you invite them over. Play 'Me' — as colloquially as you can.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Epá, que dia... Só me apetece ir para casa e não fazer nada.
lines.en: Man, what a day... All I want is to go home and do nothing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deixa lá, já passou. Anda cá jantar connosco, a gente faz uma massa.
lines.en: Never mind, it's over now. Come have dinner with us, we'll make some pasta.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa! E se calhar depois vemos o jogo, pode ser?
lines.en: Nice! And maybe afterwards we watch the match, sound good?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pode, pois. O ecrã novo da sala é bué grande, vais adorar.
lines.en: Sure it can. The new screen in the living room is huge, you'll love it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fixe! Levo eu a sobremesa — comprei uns pastéis mesmo giros na pastelaria nova.
lines.en: Cool! I'll bring dessert — I bought some really nice pastries at the new bakery.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pronto, está combinado. A malta do escritório também aparece.
lines.en: Right then, it's settled. The office crowd is coming along too.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ainda bem que não tenho de cozinhar — hoje não me desenrascava nada.
lines.en: Good thing I don't have to cook — today I wouldn't manage at all.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não faz mal, cá em casa há sempre comida a mais.
lines.en: No problem, at our place there's always more than enough food.

days.title: O pretérito mais-que-perfeito simples: reconhecer na escrita
days.objective: You can recognise one-word pluperfect forms such as dissera, fizera and saíra in written Portuguese and convert them into the spoken tinha + participle equivalent.
days.paretoFocus: Novels, editorials and news reports in Portugal use this tense constantly, so failing to spot it means misreading the time sequence of a whole paragraph.
drills: Take five sentences with tinha + participle and rewrite each one with the single-word pluperfect.
Read any Portuguese news article and underline every verb ending in -ara, -era or -ira that is not a simple past.
Write three sentences about your own week where one event clearly happened before another, using the one-word form.

items: How do you build the one-word pluperfect from the third person plural of the simple past?
Which two very common words look identical to pluperfect forms but are not verbs there?
Why does this tense almost never appear in casual speech?

activities.title: Já o dissera antes
activities.intro: Written Portuguese has a one-word pluperfect that replaces tinha + participle. You build it by taking the third person plural of the simple past, dropping the final -ram, and adding -ra, -ras, -ra, -ramos, -reis, -ram. It is a marker of narrative and journalistic style, and you need to read it rather than speak it.

items.hr: Quando cheguei, ela já saíra de casa.
items.en: When I arrived, she had already left the house.
items.note: saíra = tinha saído (saíram minus -ram, plus -ra)

items.hr: O ministro repetiu o que já afirmara na véspera.
items.en: The minister repeated what he had already stated the day before.
items.note: afirmara = tinha afirmado; very common in press reports

items.hr: Nunca vira nada tão bonito.
items.en: He had never seen anything so beautiful.
items.note: vira here is from ver, not from the verb virar; context decides

items.hr: Os jornais publicaram a carta que o autor escrevera em 1974.
items.en: The newspapers published the letter that the author had written in 1974.
items.note: escrevera = tinha escrito

items.hr: A cidade já não era o que fora nos anos sessenta.
items.en: The city was no longer what it had been in the sixties.
items.note: fora serves both ser and ir; here it means tinha sido

activities.title: Reconhecer as formas em -ara, -era, -ira
activities.intro: Practise spotting the one-word pluperfect and producing it from common irregular verbs.

questions.prompt: In 'Quando o júri chegou, o público já se levantara', the form 'levantara' means:
questions.explanation: The one-word pluperfect always corresponds to tinha plus the past participle, marking an action completed before another past action.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses a pluperfect verb form rather than a noun or an adverb?
options: Percebi tarde de mais que ele me mentira.
Aquilo não passou de uma mentira.
Ficámos lá fora à espera do táxi.
Ela vive fora do país há dez anos.
questions.answer: Percebi tarde de mais que ele me mentira.
questions.explanation: Here mentira is the pluperfect of mentir (tinha mentido). In the other options mentira is a noun and fora is an adverb.

questions.prompt: Complete with the one-word written pluperfect of 'dizer': 'Repetiu o que já ___ na reunião anterior.'
questions.explanation: Disseram minus -ram gives disse-, plus -ra gives dissera, equivalent to tinha dito.

questions.prompt: Complete with the one-word written pluperfect of 'fazer': 'Ninguém sabia o que ele ___ durante aqueles anos.'
questions.explanation: Fizeram minus -ram gives fize-, plus -ra gives fizera, equivalent to tinha feito.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'When we arrived, the train had already left.'
questions.explanation: Partira is the one-word pluperfect of partir and places the departure before the arrival. Comboio is the word for train used in Portugal.

activities.title: A conversa sobre o romance
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are discussing a novel with a friend and you both use the one-word pluperfect to talk about what had happened before the story begins.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Acabaste o romance? Aquilo começa com o protagonista a voltar a uma casa que abandonara vinte anos antes.
lines.en: Did you finish the novel? It starts with the protagonist returning to a house he had abandoned twenty years earlier.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Acabei ontem. Confesso que ao princípio não percebi que ele já vivera ali em criança.
lines.en: I finished it yesterday. I admit that at first I did not realise he had already lived there as a child.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pois, o autor nunca explica isso. Só na página cem é que se percebe o que acontecera à família.
lines.en: Right, the author never explains that. Only on page one hundred do you understand what had happened to the family.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exatamente. E a irmã, que se mudara para o estrangeiro, aparece sem aviso nenhum.
lines.en: Exactly. And the sister, who had moved abroad, turns up with no warning at all.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achei curioso que ele nunca lhe tivesse escrito. Ela dissera que voltaria, e voltou mesmo.
lines.en: I found it curious that he had never written to her. She had said she would come back, and she really did.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A mim custou-me mais o fim. Percebi que o narrador já sabia tudo o que nos escondera até ali.
lines.en: For me the ending was harder. I realised that the narrator already knew everything he had hidden from us until then.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: É isso que torna o livro bom. Nada é o que parecera nos primeiros capítulos.
lines.en: That is what makes the book good. Nothing is what it had seemed in the first chapters.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo. Vou reler o início, agora que sei o que se passara antes.
lines.en: I agree. I am going to reread the beginning, now that I know what had happened before.

days.title: A modalização: matizar uma afirmação
days.objective: You can soften or qualify a claim in Portuguese using hedging adverbs, the conditional and impersonal expressions instead of stating things bluntly.
days.paretoFocus: Educated speakers in Portugal rarely assert anything flatly, so hedging is what makes an opinion sound informed rather than aggressive.
drills: Take three blunt opinions you hold and rewrite each one twice, once with an adverb and once with the conditional.
List five expressions that let you avoid committing to a fact, and write a sentence for each.
Say aloud a prediction about next year using a hedge, then repeat it without the hedge and notice the difference.

items: Which mood follows talvez when it comes before the verb?
How does the conditional of dizer soften an opinion?
Name two impersonal phrases that introduce evidence without naming a source.

activities.title: Ao que parece, diria eu
activities.intro: Modalisation is the way a speaker signals how strongly they stand behind a statement. Portuguese does this with adverbs, with conditional verb forms, and with impersonal phrases that point at evidence. Getting the hedge right matters more at this level than adding new vocabulary.

items.hr: Ao que parece, a medida não teve o efeito esperado.
items.en: Apparently, the measure did not have the expected effect.
items.note: ao que parece attributes the claim to appearances, not to you

items.hr: Diria que o problema é sobretudo cultural.
items.en: I would say the problem is above all a cultural one.
items.note: the conditional diria turns an assertion into a personal reading

items.hr: Talvez valha a pena esperar mais uns dias.
items.en: Perhaps it is worth waiting a few more days.
items.note: talvez before the verb requires the conjuntivo: valha, not vale

items.hr: Não deixa de ser curioso que ninguém tenha reagido.
items.en: It is still rather curious that nobody reacted.
items.note: não deixa de ser makes a judgement sound understated

items.hr: Tudo indica que os preços vão subir outra vez.
items.en: Everything suggests that prices are going to rise again.
items.note: tudo indica que presents a prediction as evidence based

activities.title: Suavizar o que se afirma
activities.intro: Practise turning flat statements into carefully qualified ones.

questions.prompt: Which version commits the speaker least to the accusation?
options: Tudo indica que o relatório não conta toda a história.
O relatório não conta toda a história.
O relatório esconde sempre os dados.
questions.answer: Tudo indica que o relatório não conta toda a história.
questions.explanation: Tudo indica que attributes the conclusion to the available evidence rather than to the speaker, which is the weakest commitment of the four.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct verb form: 'Talvez ___ melhor adiar a reunião para a semana.'
questions.explanation: When talvez comes before the verb it triggers the present conjuntivo, so seja is required.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of the verb 'dizer', first person singular, to soften the opinion: '___ que o texto está demasiado longo.'
questions.explanation: The conditional presents the judgement as one possible reading rather than as a fact.

questions.prompt: Complete the fixed understatement with the third person singular present of the verb meaning 'to cease': 'Não ___ de ser estranho que ninguém tenha telefonado.'
questions.explanation: Não deixa de ser plus an adjective is a fixed hedging structure that asserts something while sounding mild.

questions.prompt: Build the European Portuguese for: 'Everything suggests that the decision was hasty.'
questions.explanation: Tudo indica que is followed by the indicative because it presents the claim as supported by evidence.

activities.title: Comentários sobre o relatório
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague asks for your honest reaction to a weak report and you criticise it while hedging everything you say.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Leste o relatório? Diz-me com franqueza o que achaste.
lines.en: Did you read the report? Tell me frankly what you thought.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Diria que está bem escrito, mas não deixa de ser vago em certos pontos.
lines.en: I would say it is well written, but it is nonetheless vague in certain places.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vago em que sentido? Achas que faltam dados?
lines.en: Vague in what sense? Do you think data are missing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ao que parece, os números do último trimestre não foram incluídos. Talvez ainda não estivessem disponíveis.
