days.objective: You can discuss ethical limits on research using formal impersonal and passive constructions.
days.paretoFocus: Ethical and legal debate in Portuguese is written and spoken impersonally, so these frames unlock a whole register.
drills: Rewrite three active sentences about research as passives with 'ser + past participle'.
Write two rules using 'impõe-se que' followed by the subjunctive.
Deny a false cause with 'não é por ... que ...' about a technology you know.

items: How is the agent of a passive introduced in Portuguese?
Which mood follows 'impõe-se que'?
Say 'one must distinguish' in a formal register.

activities.title: O registo formal da ética
activities.intro: Ethical debate leans on impersonal forms that hide the speaker. The passive with 'ser + past participle' names an agent with 'por', while 'impõe-se que' and 'cumpre + infinitive' state duties without saying who has them.

items.hr: A investigação com embriões é rigorosamente enquadrada pela lei, e não apenas por comités de ética.
items.en: Research on embryos is strictly framed by the law, and not only by ethics committees.
items.note: ser + past participle; the agent comes after 'por'

items.hr: Impõe-se que o consentimento seja informado, livre e revogável a qualquer momento.
items.en: Consent must be informed, freely given and revocable at any time.
items.note: impõe-se que + present subjunctive

items.hr: Não é por ser tecnicamente possível que um procedimento se torna eticamente aceitável.
items.en: It is not because something is technically possible that a procedure becomes ethically acceptable.
items.note: não é por + infinitive + que: denies a supposed cause

items.hr: Foram levantadas objeções sérias à alteração genética de células reprodutoras.
items.en: Serious objections were raised to the genetic alteration of reproductive cells.
items.note: the participle agrees with the subject: objeções -> levantadas

items.hr: Cumpre distinguir o que a ciência pode fazer daquilo que a sociedade está disposta a autorizar.
items.en: One must distinguish what science can do from what society is willing to authorise.
items.note: cumpre + infinitive, a formal way of saying 'one must'

activities.title: Normas e passivas
activities.intro: Practise stating rules and limits impersonally.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is a correctly formed passive in European Portuguese?
options: O ensaio foi suspendido pela autoridade de saúde.
O ensaio esteve suspenso pela autoridade de saúde.
O ensaio foi suspenso pela autoridade de saúde.
O ensaio tinha suspenso pela autoridade de saúde.
questions.answer: O ensaio foi suspenso pela autoridade de saúde.
questions.explanation: The passive uses 'ser' plus the short participle 'suspenso', with the agent introduced by 'por'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Impõe-se que os dados dos doentes ___ anónimos.'
questions.explanation: 'Impõe-se que' expresses an obligation and therefore governs the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete the passive with the correct form of 'ser': 'A experiência ___ interrompida logo na primeira fase.'
questions.explanation: A completed past passive uses the simple past of 'ser', giving 'foi interrompida' in agreement with 'a experiência'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não é ___ ser legal que um procedimento deixa de levantar problemas éticos.'
questions.explanation: The frame 'não é por + infinitive + que' denies that one fact explains the other.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It is essential that the patient's consent be obtained beforehand.'
questions.explanation: An impersonal expression of necessity takes 'que' plus a subjunctive passive: 'seja obtido'.

activities.title: Até onde se pode ir
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend argues that anything technically feasible should be allowed, and you answer in a formal, impersonal register.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se já se consegue corrigir o gene, porque é que havemos de esperar?
lines.en: If the gene can already be corrected, why should we wait?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Porque não é por ser possível que passa a ser aceitável. São coisas diferentes.
lines.en: Because it is not because it is possible that it becomes acceptable. Those are different things.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas há doenças que poderiam ser eliminadas de vez.
lines.en: But there are diseases that could be eliminated for good.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Impõe-se que essa decisão seja tomada com regras claras, e não caso a caso.
lines.en: That decision has to be taken with clear rules, and not case by case.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Regras impostas por quem, exatamente?
lines.en: Rules imposed by whom, exactly?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cumpre ao legislador fixá-las, depois de ouvidos os especialistas e os doentes.
lines.en: It falls to the lawmaker to set them, after specialists and patients have been heard.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso demora anos e as pessoas continuam a morrer.
lines.en: That takes years and people keep dying.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Eu sei. Mas o limite, uma vez ultrapassado, dificilmente é reposto.
lines.en: I know. But a limit, once crossed, is hardly ever restored.

days.title: A transição energética
days.objective: You can talk about future energy conditions using the future subjunctive after 'quando', 'assim que', 'se' and 'à medida que'.
days.paretoFocus: Portuguese puts a special tense after time and condition words pointing at the future, and nothing marks a fluent speaker faster.
drills: Form the future subjunctive of ser, ter, fazer, poder and vir from their third person plural preterite.
Write three predictions starting with 'Quando ...' about energy at home.
Turn two 'se' sentences about the present into future conditions.

items: How do you build the future subjunctive from the preterite?
Which conjunctions in this lesson trigger it?
Say 'as soon as the price falls' in Portuguese.

activities.title: O futuro que ainda não é certo
activities.intro: Time and condition clauses pointing at the future use the future subjunctive, formed from the third person plural of the preterite minus '-am'. So 'fizeram' gives 'fizer' and 'foram' gives 'for'. English uses a plain present here, Portuguese does not.

items.hr: Assim que o preço das baterias descer, o carro elétrico deixa de ser um luxo.
items.en: As soon as the price of batteries falls, the electric car will stop being a luxury.
items.note: descer -> descer (future subjunctive, identical to the infinitive here)

items.hr: Enquanto não houver rede suficiente, a produção renovável continuará a ser desperdiçada.
items.en: As long as there is not enough grid capacity, renewable output will keep being wasted.
items.note: haver -> houver

items.hr: Quando falarmos de custos, teremos de incluir aquilo que a poluição nos custa.
items.en: When we talk about costs, we will have to include what pollution costs us.
items.note: falar -> falarmos in the first person plural

items.hr: Se as metas forem cumpridas, o país fecha as centrais a carvão antes do previsto.
items.en: If the targets are met, the country will close its coal plants ahead of schedule.
items.note: ser -> forem, in a passive future condition

items.hr: À medida que o consumo se for deslocando para a eletricidade, a rede terá de ser reforçada.
items.en: As consumption gradually shifts to electricity, the grid will have to be reinforced.
items.note: ir -> for; próclise after 'que'

activities.title: Quando e se
activities.intro: Practise the future subjunctive in time and condition clauses.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Se o inverno ___ mais frio, o consumo de gás volta a subir.'
questions.explanation: 'Se' pointing at a real future condition takes the future subjunctive of 'ser', which is 'for'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the future subjunctive correctly after a time conjunction?
questions.explanation: 'Quando' with future meaning takes the future subjunctive, so 'ter' becomes 'tivermos'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future subjunctive of 'fazer': 'Quando ___ muito calor, o consumo de eletricidade dispara.'
questions.explanation: The third person plural preterite 'fizeram' loses '-am' and gives the future subjunctive form.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future subjunctive of 'poder': 'Logo que os países ___ armazenar energia em grande escala, o problema muda de figura.'
questions.explanation: From 'puderam' you drop '-am' and add the third person plural ending, giving a form without an accent.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'As soon as the grid is modernised, we will lose less energy.'
questions.explanation: 'Assim que' takes the future subjunctive 'for' in a passive, and the main clause uses the simple future.

activities.title: Painéis no telhado
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour asks whether solar panels are worth it, and you answer with future conditions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estás mesmo a pensar pôr painéis? Aquilo demora anos a pagar-se.
lines.en: Are you really thinking of putting up panels? That takes years to pay for itself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se a eletricidade continuar a subir como tem subido, pagam-se em seis anos.
lines.en: If electricity keeps going up the way it has, they pay for themselves in six years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E no inverno? Aqui chove metade do ano.
lines.en: And in winter? It rains half the year here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quando não houver sol, compro à rede. Não é preciso ser autossuficiente.
lines.en: When there is no sun, I buy from the grid. There is no need to be self-sufficient.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dizem que as baterias ainda são caríssimas.
lines.en: They say batteries are still extremely expensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: São. Mas assim que o preço descer, acrescento uma e deixo de vender barato o que produzo.
lines.en: They are. But as soon as the price drops, I will add one and stop selling what I produce cheaply.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se isso resultar, talvez eu faça o mesmo.
lines.en: If that works out, maybe I will do the same.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À medida que formos sendo mais, a instalação também fica mais barata para todos.
lines.en: As more of us gradually join in, installation also gets cheaper for everyone.

days.title: As desigualdades sociais
days.objective: You can describe inequality with proportion structures, contrast connectors and the language of figures.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about data and proportions is what turns an opinion into an argument, in any register.
drills: Write three sentences using 'quanto mais ... menor ...' about work and income.
Contrast two facts with 'ao passo que'.
Describe a statistic you know using 'um em cada ...'.

items: Which mood follows 'quanto mais' when it points at the future?
How do you say 'one in five children'?
Give a connector that means 'whereas'.

activities.title: Falar de números e proporções
activities.intro: Inequality is argued with proportions and contrasts. 'Quanto mais ... menor ...' links two variables and takes the future subjunctive when it points forward. 'Ao passo que' contrasts two facts without denying either.

items.hr: O fosso entre os salários mais altos e os mais baixos tem vindo a alargar-se.
items.en: The gap between the highest and the lowest wages has been widening.
items.note: ter vindo a + infinitive marks a gradual trend

items.hr: Quanto mais precário for o vínculo, menor é a probabilidade de sair da pobreza.
items.en: The more precarious the contract, the lower the chance of escaping poverty.
items.note: quanto mais + future subjunctive, then 'menor é'

items.hr: Os apoios sociais reduzem a desigualdade, ao passo que os impostos indiretos a agravam.
items.en: Social benefits reduce inequality, whereas indirect taxes make it worse.
items.note: ao passo que = whereas; 'a' is the object pronoun for 'a desigualdade'

items.hr: Uma em cada cinco crianças vive em risco de pobreza, uma proporção que não baixa há anos.
items.en: One in five children lives at risk of poverty, a proportion that has not fallen for years.
items.note: um/uma em cada + number

items.hr: Não é tanto uma questão de rendimento como de acesso: escola, saúde e habitação.
items.en: It is not so much a question of income as of access: school, health and housing.
items.note: não é tanto ... como ... shifts the terms of the debate

activities.title: Dados e contrastes
activities.intro: Practise proportions, contrasts and trends.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Os rendimentos do capital cresceram, ___ os salários estagnaram.'
questions.explanation: 'Ao passo que' marks a contrast between two simultaneous facts, unlike the time and cause connectors.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses a gradual trend rather than a single event?
options: As rendas subiram no mês passado.
As rendas têm vindo a subir.
questions.answer: As rendas têm vindo a subir.
questions.explanation: 'Ter vindo a + infinitive' describes a continuing movement up to the present, not one completed event.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ mais baixa for a escolaridade dos pais, maior é o risco de abandono escolar.'
questions.explanation: Proportional comparison opens with 'quanto mais' or 'quanto menos', followed by the future subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future subjunctive of 'ser': 'Quanto mais desigual ___ a distribuição, mais frágil fica a coesão social.'
questions.explanation: After 'quanto mais' pointing forward, 'ser' takes the future subjunctive form 'for'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'One in three families spends more than half its income on housing.'
questions.explanation: 'Uma em cada três' takes a singular verb, and 'mais de' is used before a quantity.

activities.title: Não é só uma questão de salário
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone reduces inequality to wages, and you reframe it with proportions and contrasts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O salário mínimo já subiu bastante. A desigualdade devia estar a cair.
lines.en: The minimum wage has gone up quite a lot. Inequality should be falling.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Subiu, sim, ao passo que o custo da habitação subiu ainda mais depressa.
lines.en: It has gone up, yes, whereas housing costs have risen even faster.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas quem trabalha consegue sempre safar-se.
lines.en: But anyone who works can always get by.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uma em cada cinco pessoas em risco de pobreza tem emprego. Trabalhar deixou de chegar.
lines.en: One in five people at risk of poverty has a job. Working is no longer enough.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então a solução é aumentar mais os salários.
lines.en: So the solution is to raise wages further.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não é tanto uma questão de salário como de acesso a creche, transporte e casa.
lines.en: It is not so much a question of wages as of access to childcare, transport and housing.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso custa dinheiro que ninguém tem.
lines.en: That costs money nobody has.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quanto mais tarde se investir nisso, mais caro fica reparar o estrago.
lines.en: The later we invest in that, the more expensive it becomes to repair the damage.

days.title: O sistema educativo e as suas reformas
days.objective: You can move into a formal register by turning verbs into nouns when discussing education policy.
days.paretoFocus: Nominalisation is the main difference between how you speak and how policy is written and debated.
drills: Turn five verbs about school into nouns and use each in a phrase with 'de'.
Rewrite two of your own sentences replacing a verb with its noun.
Say what you would change in a school system in one nominalised sentence.

items: Which noun comes from 'alargar' and which from 'reforçar'?
How do you link a nominalisation to its object?
Give the noun for 'avaliar' and use it in a phrase.

activities.title: Do verbo ao nome
activities.intro: Formal Portuguese prefers nouns where speech uses verbs: not 'reduzir as turmas' but 'a redução das turmas'. The object of the verb then comes in with 'de'. Learning the common endings, '-ção', '-mento', '-agem', gives you the whole register at once.

items.hr: A redução do número de alunos por turma exige a contratação de mais professores.
items.en: Reducing the number of pupils per class requires hiring more teachers.
items.note: reduzir -> a redução; contratar -> a contratação

items.hr: O alargamento da escolaridade obrigatória não resolveu, por si só, o abandono escolar.
items.en: Extending compulsory schooling did not, on its own, solve early school leaving.
items.note: alargar -> o alargamento; abandonar -> o abandono

items.hr: A avaliação dos docentes continua a ser o ponto de maior atrito com os sindicatos.
items.en: Teacher assessment remains the biggest point of friction with the unions.
items.note: avaliar -> a avaliação

items.hr: Defendo uma revisão dos programas, não a sua substituição de quatro em quatro anos.
items.en: I am in favour of revising the syllabuses, not replacing them every four years.
items.note: rever -> a revisão; substituir -> a substituição

items.hr: O reforço do apoio aos alunos com dificuldades pesa mais do que qualquer mudança curricular.
items.en: Strengthening support for struggling pupils matters more than any curricular change.
items.note: reforçar -> o reforço, a noun without a suffix

activities.title: Nomes em vez de verbos
activities.intro: Practise the formal noun forms used in education debates.

questions.prompt: Which noun corresponds to the verb 'acolher'?
questions.explanation: Verbs in '-er' of this type usually take '-imento', giving 'acolhimento'.

questions.prompt: Which version belongs to a formal written register?
options: Fecharam escolas no interior e isso foi mau.
O encerramento de escolas no interior teve custos sociais elevados.
Andaram a fechar escolas por aí e ninguém disse nada.
As escolas do interior fecharam e pronto.
questions.answer: O encerramento de escolas no interior teve custos sociais elevados.
questions.explanation: The nominalisation 'o encerramento de' replaces the verb and gives the sentence a formal, impersonal tone.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun formed from 'formar': 'A ___ contínua dos professores foi outra vez adiada.'
questions.explanation: Verbs in '-ar' commonly give nouns in '-ção', and the noun is feminine.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun formed from 'aplicar': 'A ___ da nova lei ficou adiada para o próximo ano letivo.'
questions.explanation: The same '-ção' pattern applies, and the object of the verb is linked with 'de'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Assessing the reform requires more time and fewer promises.'
questions.explanation: The verb becomes the noun 'a avaliação' and its object arrives through 'de' contracted with the article.

activities.title: Mais uma reforma
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A parent complains about constant school reforms, and you reply in the nominalised register of policy debate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Cada governo muda tudo outra vez. Os miúdos é que pagam.
lines.en: Every government changes everything again. It is the kids who pay for it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo. A substituição dos programas de quatro em quatro anos não deixa nada assentar.
lines.en: I agree. Replacing the syllabuses every four years never lets anything settle.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E agora falam outra vez em avaliar os professores.
lines.en: And now they are talking about assessing teachers again.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A avaliação em si não é o problema. O problema é a ausência de critérios estáveis.
lines.en: Assessment itself is not the problem. The problem is the absence of stable criteria.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então o que é que farias, se dependesse de ti?
lines.en: So what would you do, if it were up to you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Apostava no reforço do apoio individual e na redução das turmas, sem mexer nos programas.
lines.en: I would bet on strengthening individual support and reducing class sizes, without touching the syllabuses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso não dá manchetes a ninguém.
lines.en: That gets nobody any headlines.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois não. E é precisamente por isso que a melhoria real demora tanto.
lines.en: It certainly does not. And that is exactly why real improvement takes so long.

days.title: A justiça e o Estado de direito
days.objective: You can argue about courts and the rule of law using unreal conditionals and restrictive conjunctions.
days.paretoFocus: Saying what would happen if things were different is the backbone of any argument about institutions.
drills: Write three sentences pairing 'se + imperfect subjunctive' with a conditional.
Use 'a menos que' and 'caso' once each about a legal situation.
Say what you would change in the justice system, starting with 'Se eu fosse ...'.

items: Which two tenses pair up in an unreal conditional?
What mood follows 'caso' and 'a menos que'?
Say 'unless there is new evidence'.

activities.title: Hipóteses e condições no direito
activities.intro: Unreal conditions pair 'se' plus the imperfect subjunctive with the conditional in the main clause. 'Caso' and 'a menos que' introduce conditions too, but they take the present subjunctive and sound more formal than 'se'.

items.hr: Se os tribunais tivessem mais meios, os processos não se arrastariam durante anos.
items.en: If the courts had more resources, cases would not drag on for years.
items.note: se + imperfect subjunctive (tivessem) + conditional (arrastariam)

items.hr: Caso o arguido não constitua advogado, o tribunal nomeia-lhe um defensor oficioso.
items.en: Should the defendant not appoint a lawyer, the court assigns one to them.
items.note: caso + present subjunctive; ênclise in 'nomeia-lhe'

items.hr: A menos que haja prova nova, a decisão torna-se definitiva.
items.en: Unless there is new evidence, the decision becomes final.
items.note: a menos que + present subjunctive

items.hr: Ninguém pode ser condenado por um facto que, à data, não fosse crime.
items.en: Nobody can be convicted for an act that, at the time, was not a crime.
items.note: imperfect subjunctive inside a relative clause with hypothetical value

items.hr: Se eu fosse legislador, apostaria mais na prevenção do que no agravamento das penas.
items.en: If I were a lawmaker, I would bet more on prevention than on tougher sentences.
items.note: ser -> fosse; apostar -> apostaria

activities.title: Se fosse assim
activities.intro: Practise unreal conditions and formal condition words.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A menos que o prazo ___ suspenso, o processo prescreve em março.'
questions.explanation: 'A menos que' always takes the subjunctive, here the present subjunctive of 'ser'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence pairs the tenses of an unreal condition correctly?
options: Se a lei seria clara, discutia-se menos.
Se a lei fosse clara, discutia-se-ia menos.
Se a lei fosse clara, discutir-se-ia menos.
Se a lei for clara, discutir-se-ia menos.
questions.answer: Se a lei fosse clara, discutir-se-ia menos.
questions.explanation: The imperfect subjunctive goes in the 'se' clause and the conditional in the main clause, with the pronoun inside the verb in mesoclisis.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive of 'haver': 'Se ___ mais juízes, o atraso judicial seria bem menor.'
questions.explanation: Unreal conditions take the imperfect subjunctive, formed here from the preterite stem of 'haver'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the conditional of 'ser': 'Se a norma fosse escrita em português simples, a interpretação não ___ tão discutida.'
questions.explanation: The main clause of an unreal condition uses the conditional, built on the infinitive plus '-ia'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'If the trial were public, confidence in justice would grow.'
questions.explanation: The condition uses 'fosse' and the consequence uses the conditional 'aumentaria'.

activities.title: Se os tribunais fossem mais rápidos
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone says courts are useless, and you answer with unreal conditions and formal condition words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Um processo que demora nove anos não serve para nada.
lines.en: A case that takes nine years is of no use to anyone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se houvesse mais funcionários judiciais, metade desse tempo desaparecia.
lines.en: If there were more court staff, half of that time would disappear.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ou então mudavam-se as leis e acabava-se com tantos recursos.
lines.en: Or else the laws would be changed and all those appeals would end.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cuidado. A menos que se garanta o recurso, ninguém corrige um erro do primeiro juiz.
lines.en: Careful. Unless the right to appeal is guaranteed, nobody corrects a first judge's mistake.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se o arguido nem sequer tiver dinheiro para advogado?
lines.en: And what if the defendant does not even have money for a lawyer?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Caso não o possa pagar, o tribunal nomeia-lhe um. Isso está garantido.
lines.en: Should they be unable to pay for one, the court appoints one for them. That is guaranteed.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Continuo a achar que a justiça é para quem pode esperar.
lines.en: I still think justice is for those who can afford to wait.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nisso dou-te razão. Se eu pudesse mudar uma coisa só, era o tempo de espera.
lines.en: On that I agree with you. If I could change only one thing, it would be the waiting time.

days.title: A saúde pública e a prevenção
days.objective: You can use the personal infinitive to talk about prevention, screening and health behaviour.
days.paretoFocus: The personal infinitive lets you keep a subject without a 'que' clause, and it is everywhere in careful spoken Portuguese.
drills: Conjugate three verbs in the personal infinitive and use each after 'antes de'.
Turn two 'que' clauses into personal infinitive clauses.
Give three pieces of health advice using 'é melhor ... + personal infinitive'.

items: What endings does the personal infinitive add?
After which prepositions is it especially common?
Say 'before we take any medicine'.

activities.title: O infinitivo com sujeito
activities.intro: The personal infinitive carries its own subject through the endings '-es, -mos, -des, -em'. It replaces a heavier 'que' clause after prepositions like 'antes de', 'depois de', 'apesar de' and 'para', and after impersonal expressions such as 'é fundamental'.

items.hr: É fundamental os doentes crónicos serem acompanhados fora do hospital.
items.en: It is essential for chronic patients to be followed up outside the hospital.
items.note: ser -> serem, third person plural personal infinitive

items.hr: Antes de tomarmos qualquer medicamento, convém confirmar as interações.
items.en: Before we take any medicine, it is worth checking the interactions.
items.note: tomar -> tomarmos, first person plural

items.hr: O rastreio serve para detetarmos a doença antes de aparecerem sintomas.
items.en: Screening serves to let us detect the illness before symptoms appear.
items.note: two different subjects, so two personal infinitives

items.hr: Apesar de vivermos mais anos, nem sempre os vivemos com saúde.
items.en: Although we live more years, we do not always live them in good health.
items.note: apesar de + personal infinitive avoids 'apesar de que'

items.hr: No caso de sentires falta de ar, não esperes pela consulta de rotina.
items.en: If you feel short of breath, do not wait for your routine appointment.
items.note: tu form: sentires; negative imperative 'não esperes'

activities.title: Prevenir antes de tratar
activities.intro: Practise the personal infinitive in health advice.

questions.prompt: Which form is the personal infinitive of 'fazer' with 'nós'?
questions.explanation: The personal infinitive keeps the full infinitive and adds the ending, unlike the future subjunctive 'fizermos'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'É importante os idosos ___ acompanhados por um médico de família.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive has its own plural subject, so it carries the third person plural ending.

questions.prompt: Complete with the personal infinitive of 'ir': 'É melhor os utentes ___ ao centro de saúde do que recorrerem às urgências.'
questions.explanation: With a plural subject expressed, the infinitive takes '-em'.

questions.prompt: Complete with the personal infinitive of 'ser': 'Depois de ___ vacinados, os mais velhos ficam muito menos expostos.'
questions.explanation: 'Depois de' plus a personal infinitive keeps the subject 'os mais velhos' without needing a 'que' clause.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It is important for us to cut down on sugar before problems appear.'
questions.explanation: Two subjects, 'nós' and 'problemas', give two personal infinitives with different endings.

activities.title: Vale a pena fazer o rastreio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend avoids check-ups and you argue for prevention using personal infinitives.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Só vou ao médico quando me dói alguma coisa. Sempre foi assim.
lines.en: I only go to the doctor when something hurts. It has always been like that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O rastreio existe justamente para detetarmos a doença antes de doer.
lines.en: Screening exists precisely so that we can detect illness before it hurts.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E depois é uma série de exames por nada.
lines.en: And then it is a whole string of tests for nothing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Apesar de darem trabalho, evitam tratamentos muito mais pesados.
lines.en: Although they are a hassle, they prevent far heavier treatments.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não tenho tempo para andar a marcar consultas.
lines.en: I do not have time to be booking appointments.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É fundamental marcares uma agora, antes de o teu horário piorar.
lines.en: It is essential for you to book one now, before your schedule gets worse.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se calhar tens razão. O meu pai teve o mesmo problema aos cinquenta.
lines.en: Maybe you are right. My father had the same problem at fifty.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então com mais razão. No caso de haver histórico familiar, começa-se mais cedo.
lines.en: All the more reason, then. Where there is family history, you start earlier.

days.title: O urbanismo e a cidade do futuro
days.objective: You can describe places and plans precisely using relative pronouns such as 'cujo', 'onde', 'o qual' and 'o que'.
days.paretoFocus: Relatives let you pack two ideas into one sentence, which is exactly what B2 speech sounds like.
drills: Write three sentences with 'cujo' agreeing with different nouns.
Join two short sentences about your street using 'onde' or 'pela qual'.
Comment on a whole fact with 'o que', not 'que'.

items: Does 'cujo' agree with the owner or with the thing owned?
When do you need 'o qual' instead of 'que'?
What does 'o que' refer back to?

activities.title: Relativos para descrever a cidade
activities.intro: 'Cujo' means whose and agrees with the noun that follows it, never with the owner. After a preposition, 'o qual' and its forms are clearer than 'que'. And 'o que' comments on a whole preceding clause, not on a single noun.

items.hr: Foi aprovado um plano cujo objetivo é devolver o centro aos residentes.
items.en: A plan was approved whose aim is to give the centre back to residents.
items.note: cujo agrees with 'objetivo', the thing owned

items.hr: O bairro onde cresci foi quase todo convertido em alojamento turístico.
items.en: The neighbourhood where I grew up has been almost entirely converted into tourist accommodation.
items.note: onde = in which, for places only

items.hr: A avenida pela qual passam os autocarros vai ser reduzida a duas faixas.
items.en: The avenue along which the buses run is going to be cut down to two lanes.
items.note: preposition + o/a qual after 'por', 'com', 'sem'

items.hr: Falta habitação acessível, o que explica a saída de famílias para a periferia.
items.en: There is not enough affordable housing, which explains families moving out to the outskirts.
items.note: o que refers to the whole previous clause

items.hr: As cidades cujas ruas foram pensadas para o automóvel estão agora a ser repensadas.
items.en: Cities whose streets were designed for the car are now being rethought.
items.note: cujas agrees with 'ruas'; estar a + infinitive for the ongoing action

activities.title: Que, onde, cujo
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right relative pronoun.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Vivo numa rua ___ ninguém consegue estacionar.'
questions.explanation: The relative stands for 'in which' with a place, and that is exactly what 'onde' expresses.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the relative that comments on the whole previous idea?
options: Fecharam a ponte, que atrasou toda a gente.
Fecharam a ponte, o que atrasou toda a gente.
Fecharam a ponte, cuja atrasou toda a gente.
Fecharam a ponte, onde atrasou toda a gente.
questions.answer: Fecharam a ponte, o que atrasou toda a gente.
questions.explanation: 'O que' takes up the entire preceding clause, while plain 'que' would need a single noun to refer to.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Um projeto ___ custos ninguém consegue explicar não devia avançar.'
questions.explanation: The relative agrees with the following noun 'custos', which is masculine plural.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A praça na ___ havia um parque de estacionamento é hoje um jardim.'
questions.explanation: After a preposition with an article, Portuguese uses the forms of 'o qual' rather than plain 'que'.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The street where I live has been closed to traffic.'
questions.explanation: 'Onde' introduces the relative clause and the main verb is a passive agreeing with 'a rua'.

activities.title: A praça vai mudar
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A neighbour is against pedestrianising a square, and you defend the plan using relative clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ouviste dizer que querem tirar os carros da praça toda?
lines.en: Did you hear they want to take the cars out of the whole square?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ouvi. É um plano cujo objetivo é devolver aquele espaço a quem cá mora.
lines.en: I did. It is a plan whose aim is to give that space back to the people who live here.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E eu, que preciso do carro para trabalhar, estaciono onde?
lines.en: And where do I park, since I need the car for work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: No silo da rua pela qual entras no bairro. Fica a três minutos a pé.
lines.en: In the car park on the street along which you come into the neighbourhood. It is three minutes on foot.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pago para estacionar à porta de casa, é isso.
lines.en: So I pay to park at my own front door, is that it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pagas menos do que hoje gastas em multas, o que já é alguma coisa.
lines.en: You pay less than you currently spend on fines, which is already something.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E os comerciantes cujas lojas dependem de quem passa de carro?
lines.en: And the shopkeepers whose shops depend on people driving past?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nas ruas onde isto já se fez, as vendas subiram. Quem anda a pé pára mais.
lines.en: On the streets where this has already been done, sales went up. People on foot stop more often.

days.title: A agricultura e a alimentação
days.objective: You can qualify and restrict a claim about farming and food instead of stating it flatly.
days.paretoFocus: Hedging expressions are what stop a strong opinion from sounding naive, and they are hard to guess from English.
drills: Reject a false characterisation using 'longe de + infinitive'.
Restrict a claim twice using 'salvo' and 'salvo se'.
Downgrade a claim with 'quando muito' and with 'em rigor'.

items: What does 'quando muito' add to a sentence?
How do you say 'far from solving the problem'?
Which expression means 'strictly speaking'?

activities.title: Matizar uma afirmação
activities.intro: B2 argument lives on qualification. 'Longe de' denies a description outright, 'salvo' carves out an exception, 'em rigor' corrects a loose statement and 'quando muito' concedes only the smallest version of a claim.

items.hr: Longe de ser um luxo, a agricultura biológica é uma resposta ao esgotamento dos solos.
items.en: Far from being a luxury, organic farming is a response to soil exhaustion.
items.note: longe de + infinitive rejects a description

items.hr: Salvo nas regiões com regadio, a seca já obriga a mudar de culturas.
items.en: Except in irrigated regions, drought is already forcing a change of crops.
items.note: salvo = except, more formal than 'exceto'

items.hr: Em rigor, o desperdício alimentar começa muito antes de o produto chegar à loja.
items.en: Strictly speaking, food waste begins long before the product reaches the shop.
items.note: em rigor corrects a loose formulation

items.hr: Quando muito, os rótulos informam; não mudam sozinhos os hábitos de consumo.
items.en: At most, labels inform; they do not change consumption habits on their own.
items.note: quando muito concedes only a minimal claim

items.hr: À partida, produzir localmente polui menos, mas há que ver o gasto de água.
items.en: In principle, producing locally pollutes less, but the water use has to be taken into account.
items.note: à partida = in principle; há que + infinitive = one has to

activities.title: Nem tanto assim
activities.intro: Practise qualifying and restricting claims.

questions.prompt: What does 'quando muito' signal in an argument?
options: that only a weak version of the claim is accepted
that the claim is about the future
questions.answer: that only a weak version of the claim is accepted
questions.explanation: 'Quando muito' concedes the smallest possible version of a claim before rejecting the rest.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___, o problema não é a quantidade produzida, mas a que se perde pelo caminho.'
questions.explanation: The sentence corrects a loose way of putting things, which is exactly the job of 'em rigor'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Salvo ___ chova bastante em abril, a colheita vai ser fraca.'
questions.explanation: Before a clause, the exception marker combines with the conditional word to mean 'unless'.

questions.prompt: Complete: '___ de resolver o problema, os apoios ao gasóleo agrícola agravaram-no.'
questions.explanation: The frame rejects a description outright and is followed by 'de' plus an infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Far from disappearing, small farms are coming back.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive carries its own plural subject, and the progressive uses 'estar a' plus an infinitive.

activities.title: Comprar ao produtor
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone dismisses local food as a fashion, and you qualify every claim instead of arguing flatly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comprar ao produtor é moda de gente com dinheiro, não é?
lines.en: Buying from the producer is a fashion for people with money, is it not?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Longe de ser moda, é o que a maior parte do país fazia há trinta anos.
lines.en: Far from being a fashion, it is what most of the country did thirty years ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas fica sempre mais caro do que no supermercado.
lines.en: But it always ends up dearer than at the supermarket.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Em rigor, fica mais caro ao quilo e mais barato ao fim do mês, porque se deita menos fora.
lines.en: Strictly speaking, it is dearer by the kilo and cheaper by the end of the month, because less gets thrown away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso é bonito de dizer, mas ninguém tem tempo para andar em feiras.
lines.en: That sounds nice, but nobody has time to be going round markets.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Salvo se trabalhares aos sábados, uma manhã por semana chega perfeitamente.
lines.en: Unless you work on Saturdays, one morning a week is perfectly enough.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E resolve alguma coisa em termos ambientais?
lines.en: And does it solve anything in environmental terms?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quando muito, reduz o transporte. Não resolve a questão da água, que é a mais séria.
lines.en: At most, it cuts transport. It does not solve the water question, which is the most serious one.

days.title: Os transportes do futuro
days.objective: You can express probability and conjecture about transport using the future tense, 'dever', 'vir a' and 'haver de'.
days.paretoFocus: In Portuguese the future tense is used far more for guessing than for talking about the future, and learners miss this.
drills: Guess three things about right now using the simple future.
Guess three things about the past using 'terá' plus a participle.
Write two predictions with 'deverá' and two with 'virá a'.

items: What does the simple future usually express in speech?
How do you guess about a past event?
What is the difference between 'deve' as duty and as probability?

activities.title: Conjeturar sobre o que aí vem
activities.intro: The simple future often means 'probably', not 'later': 'Onde estará?' means where can it be. For a guess about the past, use 'terá' plus a participle. 'Dever' softens a prediction, 'vir a' marks an eventual outcome and 'haver de' a firm expectation.

items.hr: O comboio deverá voltar a ser a espinha dorsal das ligações entre cidades.
items.en: The train is likely to become the backbone of intercity links again.
items.note: dever here means probability, not obligation

items.hr: O atraso terá sido causado por uma avaria na catenária.
items.en: The delay was probably caused by a fault in the overhead line.
items.note: terá + participle = guess about the past

items.hr: O carro autónomo virá a ser banal, mas não na próxima década.
items.en: The self-driving car will eventually become ordinary, but not in the next decade.
items.note: vir a + infinitive = come to be, eventually

items.hr: Estarão hoje mais pessoas a andar de autocarro do que há dez anos?
items.en: Are there more people riding the bus today than ten years ago, I wonder?
items.note: future in a question = wondering about the present

items.hr: Havemos de precisar de menos estacionamento e de mais espaço para peões.
items.en: We are bound to need less parking and more space for pedestrians.
items.note: haver de + infinitive = firm expectation

activities.title: Suposições sobre a mobilidade
activities.intro: Practise guessing and predicting.

questions.prompt: What does 'O metro deverá abrir em setembro' most naturally mean?
options: The metro is obliged to open in September.
The metro is expected to open in September.
The metro had to open in September.
The metro wants to open in September.
questions.answer: The metro is expected to open in September.
questions.explanation: With a non-personal subject and a future reference, 'dever' expresses likelihood rather than duty.

questions.prompt: Which sentence guesses about something that already happened?
options: O comboio terá partido antes de chegarmos.
O comboio há de partir às nove.
O comboio vem a partir às nove.
questions.answer: O comboio terá partido antes de chegarmos.
questions.explanation: The future of 'ter' plus a participle expresses a supposition about a completed event.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future of 'estar' to express a guess: 'Onde ___ o autocarro? Já devia ter passado.'
questions.explanation: In a question about the present, the simple future turns the sentence into a wondering.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A greve ___ sido convocada ontem à noite, a avaliar pelos jornais.'
questions.explanation: A supposition about the past uses the future of 'ter' before the participle.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The plane will probably have landed by now.'
questions.explanation: 'Já terá aterrado' is a guess about a past event seen from the present moment.

activities.title: À espera na estação
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are waiting for a delayed train and speculating about transport, using future forms as guesses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vinte minutos de atraso e nem sequer anunciaram nada.
lines.en: Twenty minutes late and they have not even announced anything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Terá havido algum problema na linha. Choveu muito esta noite.
lines.en: There must have been some problem on the line. It rained a lot last night.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se calhar era mais rápido ir de carro.
lines.en: Maybe it would be faster to go by car.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A esta hora estarás uma hora e meia na autoestrada. O comboio, mesmo atrasado, ganha.
lines.en: At this hour you would be an hour and a half on the motorway. The train, even late, wins.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dizem que daqui a uns anos ninguém precisa de conduzir.
lines.en: They say that in a few years nobody will need to drive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O carro autónomo virá a existir, sim, mas não resolve o espaço que ocupa na cidade.
lines.en: The self-driving car will come to exist, yes, but it does not solve the space it takes up in the city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então apostas tudo no comboio.
lines.en: So you are putting everything on the train.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aposto. Havemos de perceber que uma via bem servida vale por três faixas de trânsito.
lines.en: I am. We are bound to realise that one well served line is worth three traffic lanes.

days.title: A economia de partilha
days.objective: You can talk about platforms and shared services using the impersonal 'se' and place pronouns correctly.
days.paretoFocus: The impersonal 'se' is how European Portuguese avoids naming a subject, and pronoun position is a constant giveaway.
drills: Write three sentences with 'se' where the verb agrees with a plural noun.
Take two of them and add 'não' or 'nunca' in front, moving the pronoun.
Say what is rented, sold or shared in your street, without naming who does it.

items: When does the pronoun go before the verb?
Does the verb agree with the noun in 'alugam-se quartos'?
Rewrite 'as pessoas alugam casas' without a subject.

activities.title: Aluga-se, partilha-se, paga-se
activities.intro: The impersonal 'se' removes the subject: 'aluga-se' means it is rented, no agent named. The pronoun attaches after the verb in a plain affirmative main clause, but jumps in front after a negative word, a subordinating conjunction or most adverbs.

items.hr: Aluga-se hoje o que antes se comprava: carros, ferramentas, até roupa.
items.en: Today people rent what they used to buy: cars, tools, even clothes.
items.note: ênclise in the main clause, próclise after 'que'

items.hr: Recorre-se cada vez mais às plataformas, mas ninguém se responsabiliza pelo serviço.
items.en: People turn to platforms more and more, but nobody takes responsibility for the service.
items.note: recorrer a; the pronoun follows the first verb

items.hr: Não se pode dizer que a partilha saia sempre mais barata.
items.en: It cannot be said that sharing always works out cheaper.
items.note: próclise after 'não'; 'dizer que' in the negative takes the subjunctive

items.hr: Quando se avalia um utilizador, avalia-se também a plataforma.
items.en: When you rate a user, you are also rating the platform.
items.note: próclise in the subordinate clause, ênclise in the main one

items.hr: Encontram-se apartamentos por toda a cidade, mas poucos para quem cá vive.
items.en: Flats can be found all over the city, but few for those who actually live here.
items.note: the verb agrees with 'apartamentos', hence the plural

activities.title: Sem dizer quem
activities.intro: Practise impersonal 'se' and pronoun placement.

questions.prompt: Which sentence places the pronoun correctly after a negative word?
options: Nunca fala-se do custo real do serviço.
Nunca se fala do custo real do serviço.
Nunca falase do custo real do serviço.
Nunca se falar do custo real do serviço.
questions.answer: Nunca se fala do custo real do serviço.
questions.explanation: Negative adverbs such as 'nunca' attract the pronoun to a position before the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with correct agreement: 'Nesta plataforma ___ bicicletas à hora.'
questions.explanation: With the impersonal 'se' the verb agrees with the noun, and 'bicicletas' is plural.

questions.prompt: Complete with the missing pronoun: 'Ninguém ___ queixa enquanto os preços forem baixos.'
questions.explanation: 'Queixar-se' is pronominal, and 'ninguém' forces the pronoun in front of the verb.

questions.prompt: Complete with 'vender' in the impersonal form: 'Nas plataformas ___ tudo, do sofá à bicicleta.'
questions.explanation: The subject is the singular 'tudo', so the verb stays singular and the pronoun follows it with a hyphen.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Rooms are rented here by the night.'
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with 'quartos' and carries the pronoun after it, since the clause opens with the verb.

activities.title: Vale a pena ter carro?
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is thinking of giving up their car for shared services, and you discuss it impersonally.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estou a pensar vender o carro e usar só os partilhados.
lines.en: I am thinking of selling the car and using only the shared ones.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Faz as contas primeiro. Poupa-se muito, mas nem sempre tanto como se diz.
lines.en: Do the maths first. You save a lot, but not always as much as people claim.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Só pago quando uso. Parece-me evidente.
lines.en: I only pay when I use it. It seems obvious to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pagas, sim, mas cobra-se por minuto e ao fim de semana os preços sobem.
lines.en: You do, but they charge by the minute and at weekends the prices go up.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ainda assim, não se paga seguro nem inspeção.
lines.en: Even so, you do not pay insurance or the road test.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Isso é verdade. E encontram-se carros quase sempre, pelo menos no centro.
lines.en: That is true. And cars can almost always be found, at least in the centre.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quando avaria? Quem responde?
lines.en: And when it breaks down? Who is responsible?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aí está o problema: ninguém se responsabiliza depressa, e tu ficas na berma.
lines.en: That is exactly the problem: nobody takes responsibility quickly, and you are left on the hard shoulder.

days.title: O teletrabalho e a organização do trabalho
days.objective: You can correct a misunderstanding and reformulate your point when discussing how work is organised.
days.paretoFocus: 'Não é que ...' plus a reformulation marker is the fastest way to rescue an argument that has been misread.
drills: Correct three misunderstandings with 'não é que ... ; é que ...'.
Reformulate two statements with 'ou seja' and 'isto é'.
Emphasise one idea with the frame 'mais do que X, o que ... foi Y'.

items: Which mood follows 'não é que'?
Give two markers that introduce a rephrasing.
What does 'longe disso' do to the previous sentence?

activities.title: Não é bem isso que eu quis dizer
activities.intro: 'Não é que' plus the subjunctive rejects a reading of your words before you give the right one. 'Ou seja' and 'isto é' introduce a rephrasing, and 'longe disso' pushes back hard on what was just suggested.

items.hr: Não é que o teletrabalho seja menos produtivo; é que exige outra forma de gerir.
items.en: It is not that remote work is less productive; it is that it demands a different way of managing.
items.note: não é que + present subjunctive, then 'é que' with the indicative

items.hr: O horário passou a ser flexível, ou seja, contam os resultados e não as horas.
items.en: The schedule has become flexible, that is, results count and not hours.
items.note: passar a + infinitive = start doing something new

items.hr: Trabalhar a partir de casa não significa estar disponível a qualquer hora, longe disso.
items.en: Working from home does not mean being available at any hour, far from it.
items.note: longe disso closes the sentence with a firm denial

items.hr: Mais do que o local, o que mudou foi a fronteira entre a vida pessoal e a profissional.
items.en: More than the place, what changed was the border between personal and professional life.
items.note: o que ... foi ... puts the new information at the end

items.hr: O direito a desligar existe no papel; na prática, poucos o exercem.
items.en: The right to disconnect exists on paper; in practice, few people exercise it.
items.note: the object pronoun 'o' sits before the verb after 'poucos'

activities.title: Reformular e corrigir
activities.intro: Practise rejecting a reading and rephrasing your own point.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não é que a equipa ___ desmotivada; está exausta, que é diferente.'
questions.explanation: 'Não é que' presents the idea as denied rather than asserted, so it takes the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Which structure puts the new information at the end of the sentence for emphasis?
options: A confiança mudou mais do que o local.
Mais do que o local, o que mudou foi a confiança.
O local mudou e também a confiança.
Mudou o local, mudou a confiança.
questions.answer: Mais do que o local, o que mudou foi a confiança.
questions.explanation: The cleft 'o que ... foi ...' delays the key term and gives it the weight of the sentence.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não é que eu ___ contra as reuniões; sou contra as reuniões inúteis.'
questions.explanation: The denied clause takes the present subjunctive of 'ser' in the first person.

questions.prompt: Complete the rephrasing marker: 'Trabalho por objetivos, isto ___, ninguém me pergunta a que horas começo.'
questions.explanation: 'Isto é' introduces a restatement of what was just said, like 'that is' in English.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It is not that I miss the office; I miss my colleagues.'
questions.explanation: The denied part takes the subjunctive 'tenha' and the asserted part stays in the indicative.

activities.title: Dois dias no escritório
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your manager reads your position on remote work wrongly and you keep correcting and rephrasing it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Percebi que não queres voltar ao escritório de todo.
lines.en: I gather you do not want to come back to the office at all.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não é que eu não queira voltar; é que quatro dias presenciais não se justificam.
lines.en: It is not that I do not want to come back; it is that four days on site are not justified.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então quantos propões?
lines.en: So how many are you proposing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dois, coincidentes para toda a equipa, ou seja, os dias em que há reuniões a sério.
lines.en: Two, the same for the whole team, that is, the days when there are real meetings.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso parece-me uma forma de trabalhar menos.
lines.en: That looks to me like a way of working less.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Longe disso. Mais do que as horas, o que muda é onde elas rendem.
lines.en: Far from it. More than the hours, what changes is where they pay off.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a integração de quem entra agora?
lines.en: And what about integrating the people who are just joining?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aí concordo consigo. Os novos precisam de mais presença, isto é, de alguém ao lado nos primeiros meses.
lines.en: There I agree with you. New people need more presence, that is, someone beside them in the first months.

days.title: A juventude e o emprego
days.objective: You can build a chain of cause and consequence about young people and the labour market.
days.paretoFocus: Connectors of cause and consequence are what hold a two-minute argument together instead of a list of facts.
drills: Link three pairs of facts with 'uma vez que' and 'visto que'.
Write two consequences using 'daí que' plus the subjunctive.
Close an argument with 'pelo que' instead of starting a new sentence.

items: Which connector of consequence needs the subjunctive?
What does 'pelo que' add at the end of a clause?
Give two formal ways of saying 'because'.

activities.title: Causas e consequências
activities.intro: 'Uma vez que' and 'visto que' state a cause the listener already accepts, so they usually open the sentence. 'Daí que' introduces a consequence and takes the subjunctive, while 'pelo que' links a consequence to what precedes without a new sentence.

items.hr: Uma vez que os estágios não pagos continuam a ser legais, muitos jovens acumulam dois trabalhos.
items.en: Since unpaid internships are still legal, many young people hold down two jobs.
items.note: uma vez que + indicative, cause taken as given

items.hr: Os salários de entrada estagnaram, daí que tantos licenciados emigrem.
items.en: Starting salaries have stagnated, hence so many graduates emigrating.
items.note: daí que + present subjunctive (emigrar -> emigrem)

items.hr: Exige-se sempre mais experiência do que se pode ter aos vinte e cinco anos, pelo que o ciclo se fecha.
items.en: More experience is always demanded than one can have at twenty five, and so the circle closes.
items.note: pelo que = and therefore, inside the same sentence

items.hr: Visto que a habitação absorve metade do ordenado, sair de casa dos pais tornou-se um projeto a longo prazo.
items.en: Given that housing swallows half the salary, moving out of the family home has become a long term project.
items.note: visto que = given that, formal and slightly written

items.hr: Tantos contratos a prazo levam a que ninguém consiga planear a vida.
items.en: So many fixed term contracts mean that nobody can plan their life.
items.note: levar a que + subjunctive (conseguir -> consiga)

activities.title: Ligar os argumentos
activities.intro: Practise connectors of cause and consequence.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a consequence and requires the subjunctive?
questions.explanation: The other three introduce a cause with the indicative, while this one presents a consequence in the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A oferta de emprego qualificado é escassa, ___ muitos aceitam funções abaixo da sua formação.'
questions.explanation: The second clause is a result of the first, and only this connector expresses result with the indicative.

questions.prompt: Complete with the subjunctive of 'partir': 'Os ordenados não sobem, daí que tantos jovens ___ para o estrangeiro.'
questions.explanation: This consequence connector governs the present subjunctive, third person plural.

questions.prompt: Complete the causal connector: 'Uma ___ que o mercado exige experiência, o primeiro emprego é sempre o mais difícil.'
questions.explanation: The three word connector states a cause the listener is expected to accept.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Given that rents are high, many young people stay at home.'
questions.explanation: The cause opens the sentence with the indicative, and the main clause follows after the comma.

activities.title: O primeiro emprego
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. An older relative thinks young people are simply picky, and you answer with a chain of causes and consequences.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: No meu tempo aceitava-se o que aparecia. Hoje ninguém quer começar por baixo.
lines.en: In my day you took whatever came up. Today nobody wants to start at the bottom.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Visto que um ordenado de entrada já não paga uma renda, começar por baixo deixou de ser possível.
lines.en: Given that a starting salary no longer covers rent, starting at the bottom has stopped being possible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas há vagas por todo o lado. Vê-se nos anúncios.
lines.en: But there are vacancies everywhere. You can see it in the adverts.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Há, e quase todas pedem cinco anos de experiência, pelo que ficam por preencher.
lines.en: There are, and almost all of them ask for five years of experience, so they stay unfilled.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então que se faça um estágio primeiro.
lines.en: Then let them do an internship first.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Uma vez que muitos estágios continuam sem remuneração, só quem tem família a pagar é que aguenta.
lines.en: Since many internships are still unpaid, only those with family paying for them can hold out.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E é por isso que emigram todos?
lines.en: And is that why they all emigrate?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Em grande parte. Os salários não acompanham os preços, daí que tanta gente faça as malas.
lines.en: Largely. Wages do not keep up with prices, hence so many people packing their bags.

days.title: O envelhecimento da população
days.objective: You can present demographic projections and trends with the language of forecasting.
days.paretoFocus: Forecast verbs plus the subjunctive let you talk about anything statistical without sounding like a translation.
drills: Write three projections beginning 'Prevê-se que ...' with the subjunctive.
Describe two trends with 'tender a' plus an infinitive.
State one ratio using 'por cada'.

items: Which mood follows 'prevê-se que'?
How do you express a ratio like 'for every hundred'?
Give a verb that expresses a gradual tendency.

activities.title: Projeções e tendências
activities.intro: Forecasts use impersonal verbs of expectation, 'prevê-se que', 'espera-se que', 'estima-se que', all followed by the subjunctive. Trends take 'tender a' plus an infinitive, and ratios are built with 'por cada'.

items.hr: Prevê-se que, dentro de vinte anos, um terço da população tenha mais de sessenta e cinco anos.
items.en: It is forecast that, within twenty years, a third of the population will be over sixty five.
items.note: prevê-se que + present subjunctive (ter -> tenha)

items.hr: A esperança de vida tende a subir, ao contrário da natalidade.
items.en: Life expectancy tends to rise, unlike the birth rate.
items.note: tender a + infinitive; ao contrário de = unlike

items.hr: Por cada cem jovens há hoje bastante mais de cem idosos.
items.en: For every hundred young people there are now well over a hundred older people.
items.note: por cada + number to express a ratio

items.hr: O número de cuidadores informais duplicou no espaço de uma geração.
items.en: The number of informal carers has doubled in the space of one generation.
items.note: duplicar, triplicar, for multiples

items.hr: Envelhecer não é o problema; o problema é envelhecer sem rede de apoio.
items.en: Growing old is not the problem; the problem is growing old without a support network.
items.note: the plain infinitive works as the subject of the sentence

activities.title: O que dizem as projeções
activities.intro: Practise forecasts, ratios and trends.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Estima-se que o interior ___ um quinto dos habitantes até 2040.'
questions.explanation: Impersonal verbs of estimation and forecast govern the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Which sentence states a ratio correctly?
options: Para cada dez trabalhadores existem quatro reformados.
Por cada dez trabalhadores existem quatro reformados.
De cada dez trabalhadores existem quatro reformados.
Em cada dez trabalhadores existe quatro reformados.
questions.answer: Por cada dez trabalhadores existem quatro reformados.
questions.explanation: Ratios are introduced by 'por cada' and the verb agrees with the second quantity.

questions.prompt: Complete with the subjunctive of 'haver': 'Prevê-se que ___ falta de mão de obra em vários setores.'
questions.explanation: The forecast verb requires the subjunctive, and 'haver' meaning 'there to be' stays singular.

questions.prompt: Complete with the verb of tendency: 'A população ___ a concentrar-se no litoral, deixando o interior sem serviços.'
questions.explanation: The verb of tendency is followed by 'a' plus an infinitive and describes a direction, not a fact.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It is expected that the country will lose one million inhabitants.'
questions.explanation: The impersonal 'espera-se que' takes the subjunctive 'perca', not the future indicative.

activities.title: Quem vai cuidar de quem
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Someone shrugs off demographic change and you answer with projections, ratios and trends.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Andam sempre a dizer que o país está a envelhecer. E depois?
lines.en: They are always saying the country is ageing. So what?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prevê-se que, daqui a vinte anos, haja quase dois reformados por cada três trabalhadores.
lines.en: It is forecast that, twenty years from now, there will be almost two pensioners for every three workers.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: As pessoas trabalham até mais tarde e resolve-se.
lines.en: People work until later and that sorts it out.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Em parte. Mas o número de pessoas dependentes tende a crescer mais depressa do que a idade da reforma.
lines.en: Partly. But the number of dependent people tends to grow faster than the retirement age.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: As famílias sempre cuidaram dos seus.
lines.en: Families have always looked after their own.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Cuidaram quando havia quatro filhos. Hoje, muitas vezes, é um só, e a viver noutra cidade.
lines.en: They did when there were four children. Today it is often just one, and living in another city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então a solução é abrir mais lares.
lines.en: So the solution is to open more care homes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Espera-se que o apoio domiciliário cresça mais do que os lares. É o que as pessoas preferem.
lines.en: Home care is expected to grow more than residential homes. It is what people prefer.

days.title: A habitação e a especulação imobiliária
days.objective: You can express degree and consequence about housing costs with 'tão ... que', 'de tal modo que' and 'ao ponto de'.
days.paretoFocus: Consecutive structures let you show how far a problem has gone, which is exactly how housing is discussed.
drills: Write three sentences with 'tão' or 'tanto' followed by a consequence clause.
Use 'ao ponto de' with a personal infinitive carrying its own subject.
Present a dilemma with the frame 'ou ... ou ...'.

items: What is the difference between 'tão' and 'tanto' before a consequence?
Which form follows 'ao ponto de' when the subject changes?
Say 'prices rose to such an extent that ...'.

activities.title: A tal ponto que já não dá
activities.intro: Consecutive clauses say how far something has gone. Use 'tão' before adjectives and adverbs, 'tanto' before nouns and after verbs, and 'de tal modo que' for a whole situation. 'Ao ponto de' takes an infinitive, personal when the subject changes.

items.hr: Os preços subiram de tal modo que um professor já não consegue viver onde ensina.
items.en: Prices have risen to such an extent that a teacher can no longer live where they teach.
items.note: de tal modo que + indicative for a real consequence

items.hr: As rendas aumentaram ao ponto de as famílias gastarem mais de metade do ordenado.
items.en: Rents have gone up to the point of families spending more than half their salary.
items.note: ao ponto de + personal infinitive (gastarem)

items.hr: Há tanta procura de curta duração que praticamente desapareceram os contratos anuais.
items.en: There is so much short term demand that yearly contracts have practically disappeared.
items.note: tanta + noun + que

items.hr: Ou se constrói habitação a preços acessíveis, ou não há solução à vista.
items.en: Either affordable housing gets built, or there is no solution in sight.
items.note: ou ... ou ... presents two exclusive options

items.hr: Não vale a pena limitar as rendas se não se aumentar a oferta.
items.en: There is no point capping rents if supply is not increased.
items.note: future subjunctive after 'se'; próclise after 'não'

activities.title: Chegar a este ponto
activities.intro: Practise degree and consequence structures.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'O centro ficou ___ caro que já quase ninguém lá dorme.'
questions.explanation: Before an adjective the intensifier is 'tão', and only it can be picked up by a consequence clause with 'que'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence expresses a real consequence rather than a purpose?
options: Construíram para que os preços descessem.
Construíram de tal modo que os preços desceram.
Construíram a fim de que os preços descessem.
questions.answer: Construíram de tal modo que os preços desceram.
questions.explanation: A real result keeps the indicative, while purpose clauses take the subjunctive or an infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'A especulação é tal ___ os jovens já nem procuram casa no centro.'
questions.explanation: 'Tal' announces a consequence and is picked up by the linking word that opens the result clause.

questions.prompt: Complete with the personal infinitive of 'sair': 'Os preços subiram ao ponto de muitos moradores ___ do bairro.'
questions.explanation: The infinitive has its own subject, 'muitos moradores', so it takes the third person plural ending and keeps the accent.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Housing is so expensive that many people share flats.'
questions.explanation: 'Tão' modifies the adjective and 'que' opens the clause stating the real consequence.

activities.title: Procurar casa na cidade
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend is hunting for a flat and you discuss how far prices have gone, using consecutive structures.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vi um apartamento de dois quartos por mil e duzentos euros. Fiquei sem palavras.
lines.en: I saw a two bedroom flat for one thousand two hundred euros. I was speechless.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: As rendas subiram de tal modo que já ninguém compara com o que pagava há cinco anos.
lines.en: Rents have risen to such an extent that nobody even compares with what they paid five years ago.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E os senhorios preferem alugar a turistas por semanas.
lines.en: And landlords prefer renting to tourists by the week.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Há tanta procura de curta duração que os contratos de um ano quase desapareceram.
lines.en: There is so much short term demand that one year contracts have almost disappeared.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estou a pensar procurar do outro lado do rio.
lines.en: I am thinking of looking on the other side of the river.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muita gente fez isso, ao ponto de os preços de lá subirem também.
lines.en: A lot of people did that, to the point of prices there going up as well.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então não há saída nenhuma?
lines.en: So there is no way out at all?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ou se constrói para arrendar, ou continuamos todos a disputar as mesmas casas.
lines.en: Either we build to let, or we all go on fighting over the same homes.

days.title: O património e a sua preservação
days.objective: You can argue about protecting built heritage and use the personal infinitive to show clearly who does what.
days.paretoFocus: The personal infinitive keeps long argumentative sentences light: it marks the subject without forcing a full subordinate clause with the subjunctive.
drills: Write three sentences about an old building in your area, opening with 'é fundamental', 'apesar de' and 'antes de', each followed by a personal infinitive.
Take three sentences you would normally build with 'que' plus subjunctive and rewrite them with a personal infinitive.
Describe in five lines what should happen to an abandoned building, changing the subject of the infinitive at least twice.

items: Say the six personal infinitive endings out loud, using a regular verb.
Recall five words for parts of a historic town centre.
Explain in one sentence when a personal infinitive is preferred over a clause with 'que'.

activities.title: Preservar o que resta
activities.intro: Portuguese has an infinitive that carries person endings: eu preservar, tu preservares, ele preservar, nós preservarmos, vocês preservarem. It is used after prepositions and impersonal expressions whenever you need the subject to be explicit. It sounds lighter and more formal than a full clause with 'que'.

items.hr: É fundamental preservarmos os centros históricos sem os transformarmos em cenários para turistas.
items.en: It is essential that we preserve historic centres without turning them into stage sets for tourists.
items.note: preservar -> preservarmos (nós); transformar -> transformarmos

items.hr: Apesar de as autarquias terem verbas limitadas, muitos imóveis classificados foram recuperados.
items.en: Although local councils have limited funds, many listed buildings have been restored.
items.note: apesar de + personal infinitive replaces a clause with 'que'

items.hr: Antes de os arquitetos intervirem, o edifício estava em risco de ruir.
items.en: Before the architects stepped in, the building was at risk of collapsing.
items.note: intervir -> intervirem (eles)

items.hr: O facto de estes azulejos serem do século XVIII obriga a um restauro muito cuidadoso.
items.en: The fact that these tiles are from the eighteenth century calls for very careful restoration.
items.note: o facto de + personal infinitive is very common in written argument

items.hr: No caso de encontrarem vestígios arqueológicos, as obras têm de parar imediatamente.
items.en: If they find archaeological remains, the works have to stop immediately.
items.note: encontrar -> encontrarem (eles)

activities.title: Quem faz o quê
activities.intro: Practise marking the subject of an infinitive clause.

questions.prompt: Complete (nós): 'É importante ___ o património local antes que se perca.'
questions.explanation: After an impersonal expression the personal infinitive marks the subject: nós defendermos.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the personal infinitive correctly after 'apesar de'?
options: Apesar de eles serem poucos, os voluntários limparam a muralha.
Apesar de eles ser poucos, os voluntários limparam a muralha.
Apesar de eles forem poucos, os voluntários limparam a muralha.
Apesar de eles sendo poucos, os voluntários limparam a muralha.
questions.answer: Apesar de eles serem poucos, os voluntários limparam a muralha.
questions.explanation: The subject 'eles' requires the third person plural ending -em: serem. 'Forem' is future subjunctive and never follows a preposition.

questions.prompt: Complete with the personal infinitive (nós) of the verb meaning 'to visit': 'Depois de ___ o mosteiro, percebemos o valor do restauro.'
questions.explanation: Depois de takes the personal infinitive; the nós ending -mos is added to the full infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the personal infinitive (eles) of the verb meaning 'to detect': 'No caso de ___ fissuras na abóbada, os técnicos suspendem os trabalhos.'
questions.explanation: Third person plural of the personal infinitive: the ending -em is added to the full infinitive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'It is essential that we protect these tiles.'
questions.explanation: Impersonal expression plus personal infinitive marked for nós: é essencial protegermos.

activities.title: Na assembleia sobre o mercado antigo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You defend restoring an old iron market building, using personal infinitive clauses to say who is responsible for what.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Acha mesmo que vale a pena gastarmos tanto dinheiro num mercado abandonado?
lines.en: Do you really think it is worth us spending so much money on an abandoned market?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Acho que sim. Apesar de o edifício estar degradado, é a última construção em ferro da cidade.
lines.en: I do. Although the building is run down, it is the last iron structure in the city.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas, antes de avançarmos com o restauro, não devíamos resolver problemas mais urgentes?
lines.en: But before we go ahead with the restoration, shouldn't we deal with more urgent problems?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O facto de haver outras urgências não justifica deixarmos ruir aquilo que é irrecuperável.
lines.en: The fact that there are other urgent matters does not justify us letting something irreplaceable fall down.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E no caso de os custos dispararem a meio da obra?
lines.en: And what if the costs shoot up halfway through the works?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nesse caso, faz sentido faseá-la, sem pormos em causa a estrutura original.
lines.en: In that case it makes sense to do it in stages, without us compromising the original structure.

days.title: As indústrias culturais
days.objective: You can concede a point about culture and money using embora, ainda que, mesmo que, por muito que and se bem que with the subjunctive.
days.paretoFocus: Conceding before you argue is the single most useful move in an educated discussion, and in Portuguese every concessive conjunction pulls the subjunctive with it.
drills: Write five sentences about films, books or music, each starting with a different concessive conjunction.
Say aloud three objections to your own opinion, opening each with 'ainda que' plus a subjunctive verb.
Rewrite three sentences joined by 'mas' so that the first half becomes a concessive clause.

items: List five conjunctions that force the subjunctive in a concession.
Recall eight words connected with publishing, cinema or live music.
Say why 'embora' is never followed by the indicative.

activities.title: Cultura, dinheiro e mercado
activities.intro: Concessive conjunctions admit something that works against your point, and then you make the point anyway. In Portuguese they all take the subjunctive: embora, ainda que, mesmo que, por muito que, se bem que. Choose the tense of the subjunctive according to the time you are talking about.

items.hr: Embora o cinema português tenha pouco financiamento, continua a ganhar prémios lá fora.
items.en: Although Portuguese cinema has little funding, it keeps winning awards abroad.
items.note: embora + presente do conjuntivo

items.hr: Ainda que as plataformas paguem pouco aos autores, mudaram a forma como ouvimos música.
items.en: Even though the platforms pay authors little, they changed the way we listen to music.
items.note: ainda que + presente do conjuntivo

items.hr: Por muito que se fale em cultura, o orçamento do setor mantém-se residual.
items.en: However much people talk about culture, the sector's budget remains tiny.
items.note: por muito que = however much

items.hr: Mesmo que o livro venda bem, a editora dificilmente recupera o investimento.
items.en: Even if the book sells well, the publisher hardly recovers the investment.
items.note: mesmo que introduces a hypothesis, not a fact

items.hr: Se bem que o público seja fiel, as salas de espetáculos enchem cada vez menos.
items.en: Although the audience is loyal, venues are filling up less and less.
items.note: se bem que is the most formal of the five

activities.title: Conceder antes de argumentar
activities.intro: Practise concessive conjunctions and the mood they require.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Embora o festival ___ gratuito, atrai muitos patrocinadores privados.'
