questions.explanation: Embora always takes the subjunctive, and a present fact takes the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Which conjunction introduces a concession with the subjunctive?
questions.explanation: Assim que and logo que are temporal and take the future subjunctive; visto que gives a cause and takes the indicative.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (eles) of the verb meaning 'to have': 'Por muito que os artistas ___ talento, sem distribuição ninguém os ouve.'
questions.explanation: Por muito que requires the subjunctive; the third person plural of the present subjunctive is irregular here.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (nós) of the verb meaning 'to be able to': 'Mesmo que ___ ver tudo em casa, a experiência da sala é outra.'
questions.explanation: Mesmo que introduces a hypothesis and takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Although the theatre is small, the programme is demanding.'
questions.explanation: The concessive clause comes first with the subjunctive, then the main clause in the indicative.

activities.title: Sala de cinema ou ecrã de casa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You defend paying for culture, conceding your friend's points with concessive clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Já não vou ao cinema. Porque hei de pagar bilhete se vejo tudo em casa?
lines.en: I don't go to the cinema any more. Why should I pay for a ticket if I can watch everything at home?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda que vejas tudo em casa, o dinheiro do bilhete é o que sustenta as salas pequenas.
lines.en: Even if you watch everything at home, ticket money is what keeps small venues going.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas a assinatura mensal também paga aos criadores, não paga?
lines.en: But the monthly subscription pays the creators too, doesn't it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Paga, embora a fatia que chega ao autor seja quase simbólica.
lines.en: It does, although the share that reaches the author is almost symbolic.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Por muito que insistas, ninguém volta atrás nos hábitos.
lines.en: However much you insist, nobody goes back to the old habits.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mesmo que os hábitos não mudem, podemos escolher onde gastamos os cinco euros seguintes.
lines.en: Even if habits do not change, we can choose where we spend the next five euros.

days.title: A desinformação e o espírito crítico
days.objective: You can give conditional advice about checking information using the future subjunctive after se, quando, sempre que and assim que.
days.paretoFocus: The future subjunctive has no equivalent in English and is unavoidable in Portuguese: every open condition or future time clause needs it.
drills: Write four pieces of advice for a friend who shares news too quickly, each with 'sempre que' or 'se' plus a future subjunctive.
Conjugate ver, dizer, pôr, ser and ter in the future subjunctive out loud.
Describe in five lines how you would check a suspicious message before passing it on.

items: Explain how the future subjunctive is formed from the third person plural of the preterite.
Recall six words about sources, evidence and corrections.
Say two sentences expressing doubt with 'duvido que'.

activities.title: Antes de acreditares
activities.intro: The future subjunctive is built from the third person plural of the simple past: eles viram gives vir-, so se eu vir, se tu vires. Use it after se, quando, sempre que, assim que and enquanto whenever the situation is not yet a fact. Verbs of doubt, by contrast, take the present subjunctive.

items.hr: Sempre que vires um título alarmista, verifica quem o publicou.
items.en: Whenever you see an alarming headline, check who published it.
items.note: ver -> vires (futuro do conjuntivo)

items.hr: Se uma notícia não disser de onde vêm os dados, desconfia dela.
items.en: If a news item does not say where the data come from, be suspicious of it.
items.note: dizer -> disser

items.hr: Duvido que essa estatística seja fiável sem uma fonte identificada.
items.en: I doubt that statistic is reliable without an identified source.
items.note: duvidar que + presente do conjuntivo

items.hr: Quando puseres uma informação em causa, procura o documento original.
items.en: When you question a piece of information, look for the original document.
items.note: pôr -> puseres

items.hr: Não é por uma mensagem ser muito partilhada que se torna verdadeira.
items.en: A message does not become true just because it is widely shared.
items.note: não é por ... que is a common way to reject a false cause

activities.title: Verificar antes de partilhar
activities.intro: Practise the future subjunctive and expressions of doubt.

questions.prompt: Complete (tu): 'Assim que ___ o texto até ao fim, percebes que o título engana.'
questions.explanation: Assim que with a future reference takes the future subjunctive, not the present indicative.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Duvido que eles ___ o desmentido com o mesmo destaque.'
questions.explanation: Duvidar que expresses doubt and governs the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future subjunctive (tu) of the verb meaning 'to be' in the sense of a lasting quality: 'Se ___ capaz de explicar a notícia por palavras tuas, é porque a compreendeste.'
questions.explanation: The future subjunctive of this verb comes from eles foram, giving for- plus the person ending.

questions.prompt: Complete with the future subjunctive (nós) of the verb meaning 'to have': 'Quando ___ dúvidas, o melhor é confrontar várias fontes.'
questions.explanation: From eles tiveram comes the stem tiver-, plus -mos for nós.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Whenever you receive a video like that, confirm the date.'
questions.explanation: Sempre que with an open future takes the future subjunctive; the main clause is an imperative.

activities.title: A mensagem reencaminhada
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A relative forwards you a shocking message and you explain, with future subjunctive clauses, how to check it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Reencaminhei aquilo ao grupo da família. Se for verdade, as pessoas têm de saber.
lines.en: I forwarded that to the family group. If it is true, people need to know.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sempre que receberes uma coisa dessas, repara primeiro se tem data e autor.
lines.en: Whenever you get something like that, first check whether it has a date and an author.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas vinha com uma fotografia e tudo.
lines.en: But it came with a photograph and everything.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Duvido que a fotografia seja recente. Quando puderes, procura a mesma imagem noutro sítio.
lines.en: I doubt the photograph is recent. When you can, look for the same image somewhere else.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se eu não encontrar nada?
lines.en: And if I don't find anything?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se não encontrares nada, não partilhes. O silêncio faz menos estragos do que um erro.
lines.en: If you don't find anything, don't share it. Silence does less damage than a mistake.

days.title: As redes sociais e a vida privada
days.objective: You can describe what is done to users' data using the passive with ser plus participle and the impersonal passive with se.
days.paretoFocus: The passive lets you talk about actions whose agent is unknown or deliberately hidden, which is exactly how privacy is discussed.
drills: Turn five active sentences about an app into passives with ser plus participle.
Write three sentences with the impersonal passive with se, making the verb agree with the plural subject.
Describe in five lines what happens to a photograph after you publish it, avoiding any human subject.

items: Explain the difference between 'foi apagada' and 'estava apagada'.
Recall eight words about accounts, settings and consent.
Say one sentence with 'terão sido' expressing a supposition about the past.

activities.title: O que se faz com os nossos dados
activities.intro: The passive with ser plus participle focuses on the action and lets you leave the agent out, or add it with por. The impersonal passive with se does the same thing more informally, and the verb agrees with the thing affected. In European Portuguese the pronoun se comes after the verb at the start of a sentence.

items.hr: Os nossos dados são recolhidos mesmo quando não publicamos nada.
items.en: Our data are collected even when we do not publish anything.
items.note: ser + particípio, agent left out on purpose

items.hr: A imagem foi partilhada milhares de vezes antes de ser retirada.
items.en: The image was shared thousands of times before it was taken down.
items.note: the participle agrees with the subject: imagem, partilhada

items.hr: Vendem-se perfis falsos a quem quiser inflacionar o número de seguidores.
items.en: Fake profiles are sold to anyone who wants to inflate their follower count.
items.note: passive with se; the verb is plural because perfis is plural

items.hr: Muitas contas terão sido criadas por sistemas automáticos.
items.en: Many accounts were probably created by automatic systems.
items.note: terão sido expresses a supposition about the past

items.hr: As definições de privacidade deviam vir ativadas por defeito e não escondidas em submenus.
items.en: Privacy settings should come switched on by default and not hidden away in submenus.
items.note: participles used as adjectives after vir

activities.title: Quem é o agente
activities.intro: Practise both passive constructions and the agreement they require.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'As fotografias ___ apagadas do servidor ao fim de trinta dias.'
questions.explanation: Ser plus participle reports the action itself; estar plus participle would describe a resulting state.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is a correct impersonal passive in European Portuguese?
options: Se publicam demasiadas fotografias de crianças.
Publicam se demasiadas fotografias de crianças.
questions.explanation: The verb agrees with the plural subject and the pronoun is attached after the verb with a hyphen, since nothing before it pulls it forward.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle of the verb meaning 'to delete': 'A publicação já tinha sido ___ quando os jornalistas chegaram.'
questions.explanation: In a passive the participle agrees in gender and number with the subject, here a feminine singular noun.

questions.prompt: Complete with the simple past of the auxiliary used to build the passive, third person singular: 'A notícia ___ divulgada sem qualquer verificação.'
questions.explanation: The passive auxiliary carries the tense, and the participle stays feminine singular.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The comments were deleted by the moderators.'
questions.explanation: The agent is introduced by por, which contracts with the article to give pelos.

activities.title: As fotografias dos miúdos
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend posts photos of their children and you raise the privacy question using passive constructions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ponho fotografias dos miúdos porque só os amigos é que veem.
lines.en: I post photos of the kids because only friends can see them.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: As imagens são guardadas nos servidores mesmo depois de as apagares do teu perfil.
lines.en: The images are stored on the servers even after you delete them from your profile.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estás a exagerar. Isso é feito com toda a gente.
lines.en: You are exaggerating. That is done to everybody.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É, mas o rosto de uma criança pode ser usado sem que ela alguma vez tenha dado consentimento.
lines.en: It is, but a child's face can be used without her ever having given consent.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então o que se faz? Deixa-se de registar a vida da família?
lines.en: So what does one do? Do we stop recording family life?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Regista-se tudo, partilha-se pouco. As fotografias podem ser enviadas só a quem interessa.
lines.en: You record everything and share little. Photos can be sent only to the people who matter.

days.title: O turismo de massas e os seus efeitos
days.objective: You can describe consequences with tão, tanto, de tal modo que and ao ponto de when discussing tourism pressure on a city.
days.paretoFocus: Consequence structures let you turn a description into an argument: not just that something is large, but what it causes.
drills: Write four sentences about your own city using tão, tanta, tantos and tanto plus a consequence clause.
Rewrite three cause and effect sentences using 'de tal modo que'.
Describe in five lines how a neighbourhood changed, with at least two consequence clauses.

items: Say when to use tão and when to use tanto.
Recall eight words about housing, rents and short term letting.
Give one sentence with 'ao ponto de' plus an infinitive.

activities.title: Uma cidade cheia demais
activities.intro: Tão goes before adjectives and adverbs, tanto goes with verbs, and tanto, tanta, tantos, tantas go before nouns and agree with them. All of them can open a consequence clause with que. De tal modo que and ao ponto de are more formal ways of doing the same thing.

items.hr: O centro ficou tão caro que as famílias tiveram de se mudar para a periferia.
items.en: The centre got so expensive that families had to move to the outskirts.
items.note: tão + adjective + que

items.hr: Há tantos alojamentos locais que já quase não se ouve falar português na rua.
items.en: There are so many short term rentals that you hardly hear Portuguese in the street any more.
items.note: tantos agrees with the plural noun

items.hr: A praça encheu de tal modo que atravessá-la demora quase vinte minutos.
items.en: The square filled up to such an extent that crossing it takes almost twenty minutes.
items.note: de tal modo que is more formal than tanto que

items.hr: Os cruzeiros despejam gente ao ponto de o comércio tradicional desaparecer.
items.en: The cruise ships pour out people to the point where traditional shops disappear.
items.note: ao ponto de + infinitive, with the subject before it

items.hr: O turismo cresceu tanto que se tornou a principal receita e o principal problema.
items.en: Tourism grew so much that it became the main source of income and the main problem.
items.note: tanto after a verb

activities.title: Causas e consequências
activities.intro: Practise choosing the right intensifier before a consequence clause.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'As rendas subiram ___ depressa que ninguém conseguiu acompanhar.'
questions.explanation: Depressa is an adverb, and adverbs and adjectives take tão.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Havia ___ autocarros na avenida que os moradores fizeram uma petição.'
questions.explanation: Before a masculine plural noun the intensifier agrees with it.

questions.prompt: Complete with the intensifier that goes directly after a verb: 'O bairro mudou ___ que já não reconheço as lojas.'
questions.explanation: After a verb the invariable form is used, and que opens the consequence.

questions.prompt: Complete the middle word of the formal consequence pattern 'de tal ___ que': 'A pressão sobre a habitação aumentou de tal ___ que o município limitou as novas licenças.'
questions.explanation: Three nouns fit this frame and all three mean roughly the same thing here.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'There are so many tourists that the residents have left the neighbourhood.'
questions.explanation: The intensifier agrees with the plural noun and que introduces the result.

activities.title: Conversa à porta da mercearia
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You live in a heavily visited neighbourhood and explain the consequences to a visitor.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O bairro é lindíssimo. Deve ser muito bom viver aqui.
lines.en: The neighbourhood is beautiful. It must be lovely to live here.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Era. Agora há tanta gente na rua que levo dez minutos a chegar ao autocarro.
lines.en: It was. Now there are so many people in the street that it takes me ten minutes to get to the bus.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas o turismo não trouxe dinheiro e emprego?
lines.en: But hasn't tourism brought money and jobs?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Trouxe, só que as rendas subiram de tal modo que metade dos meus vizinhos saiu.
lines.en: It has, only rents went up to such an extent that half my neighbours left.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as lojas? Continuam as mesmas?
lines.en: And the shops? Are they still the same?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fecharam quase todas, ao ponto de eu ter de apanhar o comboio para comprar sapatos.
lines.en: Almost all of them closed, to the point where I have to take the train to buy shoes.

days.title: O desporto, o dinheiro e a sociedade
days.objective: You can weigh up the role of money in sport using correlative structures such as não só ... como também, tanto ... como and quanto mais ... mais.
days.paretoFocus: Correlative pairs let you add and balance arguments in one sentence, which is what separates a fluent B2 speaker from a list of short opinions.
drills: Write three sentences about a sport you follow using 'não só ... como também'.
Build four sentences on the pattern 'quanto mais ..., mais ...' and two on 'quanto menos ..., menos ...'.
Argue in five lines whether clubs should have spending limits, using at least two correlative pairs.

items: Recall three correlative pairs used to add or balance two ideas.
Recall eight words about clubs, sponsorship and youth training.
Say one sentence contrasting the richest team with the best team.

activities.title: Quanto mais dinheiro, mais pressão
activities.intro: Correlative structures come in two halves that must both appear. Não só ... como também adds a second element, tanto ... como puts two elements on the same level, and quanto mais ... mais links two variables that move together. They are common in journalism and in spoken argument.

items.hr: O futebol não só move milhões como também molda a agenda mediática.
items.en: Football not only moves millions but also shapes the media agenda.
items.note: in Portugal 'como também' is more usual than 'mas também'

items.hr: Quanto mais dinheiro entra no desporto, mais difícil se torna garantir a transparência.
items.en: The more money comes into sport, the harder it becomes to guarantee transparency.
items.note: both halves start with a comparative

items.hr: Tanto os clubes como os patrocinadores lucram com a exposição televisiva.
items.en: Both the clubs and the sponsors profit from television exposure.
items.note: tanto ... como = both ... and

items.hr: Nem sempre o melhor plantel ganha o campeonato, mas quase sempre ganha o mais rico.
items.en: The best squad does not always win the league, but the richest one almost always does.
items.note: nem sempre softens an absolute claim

items.hr: Ou se investe na formação de base, ou se continua a comprar resultados.
items.en: Either you invest in youth development or you go on buying results.
items.note: ou ... ou presents two exclusive options

activities.title: Equilibrar os argumentos
activities.intro: Practise the two halves of correlative structures.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Quanto ___ se paga aos jogadores, mais se afasta o adepto comum.'
questions.explanation: The pattern needs a comparative in each half: quanto mais ..., mais ...

questions.prompt: Complete: 'O patrocínio beneficia ___ o clube ___ a marca.'
questions.explanation: Tanto ... como joins two nouns on equal terms; tão ... como compares qualities, not nouns.

questions.prompt: Complete the second half of the pair opened by 'não só', using two words: 'A modalidade cresceu não só nas escolas, ___ nos clubes de bairro.'
questions.explanation: The first half stays incomplete until the additive second half arrives.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (eles) of the verb meaning 'to invest': 'É pouco provável que os clubes ___ na formação enquanto puderem comprar jogadores feitos.'
questions.explanation: É pouco provável que expresses low probability and governs the subjunctive; this verb changes its stem vowel.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The more the ticket costs, the fewer young people go to the stadium.'
questions.explanation: Quanto opens the first variable and the second half begins directly with its own comparative.

activities.title: Depois do jogo, no café
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You question how much your friend's club spends, using correlative structures.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Gastámos uma fortuna no avançado, mas valeu a pena. Ganhámos por três.
lines.en: We spent a fortune on the striker, but it was worth it. We won three nil.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quanto mais se gasta em transferências, menos sobra para a formação de base.
lines.en: The more you spend on transfers, the less is left for youth development.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A formação não enche o estádio ao sábado.
lines.en: Youth development doesn't fill the stadium on a Saturday.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não só enche como também sai mais barata a médio prazo.
lines.en: It not only fills it but also works out cheaper in the medium term.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estás a falar como um contabilista, não como um adepto.
lines.en: You are talking like an accountant, not like a supporter.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tanto o adepto como o contabilista ficam sem clube se as contas rebentarem.
lines.en: Both the supporter and the accountant end up with no club if the accounts blow up.

days.title: A diversidade linguística
days.objective: You can talk about languages and their speakers using relative pronouns after prepositions and the possessive relative cujo.
days.paretoFocus: Relative clauses with prepositions are what allow you to pack a definition into one sentence instead of three, and cujo is unavoidable in careful written Portuguese.
drills: Write five definitions of words you know, each with a relative clause introduced by a preposition plus que.
Write three sentences with cujo, cuja, cujos and cujas agreeing with the noun that follows.
Describe a person you admire in five lines, using com quem and de quem at least once each.

items: Say which relative pronoun is used for people after a preposition.
Explain what cujo agrees with.
Recall eight words about accents, varieties and mother tongues.

activities.title: Línguas de que pouco se fala
activities.intro: When a relative clause needs a preposition, the preposition comes first, never at the end as in English: a língua de que falo. For people use quem after a preposition: a pessoa com quem falei. Cujo is a possessive relative and agrees with the noun that follows it, not with the owner.

items.hr: O mirandês é uma língua de que poucos portugueses ouviram falar.
items.en: Mirandese is a language that few Portuguese people have heard of.
items.note: ouvir falar de -> de que

items.hr: Há regiões em que se mantêm sotaques muito distintos.
items.en: There are regions where very distinct accents survive.
items.note: em que is often preferable to onde in writing

items.hr: As comunidades cuja língua não é ensinada na escola tendem a perdê-la.
items.en: Communities whose language is not taught at school tend to lose it.
items.note: cuja agrees with língua, not with comunidades

items.hr: A senhora com quem falei alterna crioulo e português na mesma frase.
items.en: The lady I spoke to switches between creole and Portuguese in the same sentence.
items.note: com quem for people

items.hr: O respeito a que qualquer falante tem direito não depende do prestígio da sua variedade.
items.en: The respect every speaker is entitled to does not depend on the prestige of their variety.
items.note: ter direito a -> a que

activities.title: Preposição antes do relativo
activities.intro: Practise placing the preposition required by the verb before the relative pronoun.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Esta é a variedade ___ me identifico.'
questions.explanation: The verb here is identificar-se com, so the preposition com must precede the relative.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Um país ___ fronteiras mudaram pouco pode ter grande diversidade interna.'
questions.explanation: The possessive relative agrees with the following noun, a feminine plural.

questions.prompt: Complete with the possessive relative agreeing with a masculine singular noun: 'Um dialeto ___ vocabulário se perdeu dificilmente regressa.'
questions.explanation: The possessive relative takes the gender and number of the noun that follows it, not of the thing that owns it.

questions.prompt: Complete with the relative pronoun used for people after a preposition: 'Os investigadores com ___ trabalhei recolheram histórias de vida em duas línguas.'
questions.explanation: For human antecedents Portuguese uses this pronoun after a preposition, never que.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'This is a language whose speakers are fewer and fewer.'
questions.explanation: The possessive relative takes the gender and number of falantes, a masculine plural.

activities.title: A língua da avó
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You describe a relative who speaks a regional variety, using relative clauses with prepositions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A tua avó falava mesmo outra língua ou era só um sotaque carregado?
lines.en: Did your grandmother really speak another language or was it just a strong accent?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Era uma variedade de que ela própria tinha vergonha, porque a escola a corrigia sempre.
lines.en: It was a variety she herself was ashamed of, because school kept correcting her.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E ninguém da família a aprendeu?
lines.en: And nobody in the family learned it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Só o meu tio, cuja mulher é da mesma aldeia. Com ela, ele muda de registo em dois segundos.
lines.en: Only my uncle, whose wife is from the same village. With her he switches register in two seconds.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso é uma riqueza a que ninguém dá valor.
lines.en: That is a richness nobody values.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois não. As pessoas com quem cresci acham que falar assim é falar mal.
lines.en: No, nobody does. The people I grew up with think that speaking like that is speaking badly.

days.title: A Europa e a cooperação
days.objective: You can express purpose with para que, a fim de que and sem que plus subjunctive, and with de modo a plus infinitive.
days.paretoFocus: Purpose clauses are the backbone of any argument about policy: they say what a measure is for, and Portuguese chooses between subjunctive and infinitive by whether the subject changes.
drills: Write three sentences with para que plus present subjunctive and three with the same idea in the past.
Rewrite three of your purpose sentences with de modo a, keeping the subject the same.
Write two sentences with sem que, noticing that it always takes the subjunctive.

items: Say which purpose connector keeps the infinitive and why.
Recall eight words about funds, agreements and joint decisions.
Give one sentence with para que where the main verb is in the past.

activities.title: Decidir em conjunto
activities.intro: Para que and a fim de que introduce a purpose with a new subject and always take the subjunctive. If the main verb is in the past, the subjunctive moves to the imperfect. When the subject does not change, use para or de modo a plus a plain infinitive. Sem que always takes the subjunctive too.

items.hr: Os fundos foram criados para que as regiões mais pobres pudessem convergir.
items.en: The funds were created so that the poorest regions could catch up.
items.note: past main verb -> imperfeito do conjuntivo

items.hr: É preciso decidir depressa, sem que isso signifique decidir mal.
items.en: Decisions have to be quick, without that meaning they are bad.
items.note: sem que + conjuntivo

items.hr: A fim de que as decisões sejam legítimas, têm de ser explicadas aos cidadãos.
items.en: In order for decisions to be legitimate, they have to be explained to citizens.
items.note: a fim de que is the formal register of para que

items.hr: Os países coordenam-se de modo a evitar respostas contraditórias.
items.en: The countries coordinate so as to avoid contradictory responses.
items.note: same subject -> de modo a + infinitive

items.hr: Sem uma política comum, cada Estado negoceia por si e todos perdem força.
items.en: Without a common policy, each state negotiates alone and everyone loses leverage.
items.note: sem + noun needs no verb at all

activities.title: Para quê e com que fim
activities.intro: Practise choosing between subjunctive and infinitive in purpose clauses.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Reformaram as regras para que ninguém ___ bloquear sozinho uma decisão.'
questions.explanation: Para que takes the subjunctive, and a past main verb pulls it into the imperfect.

questions.prompt: Which connector keeps the same subject and is therefore followed by an infinitive?
questions.explanation: The other three introduce a clause with a new subject and require the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (eles) of the verb meaning 'to know a fact': 'Publicam-se os documentos para que os cidadãos ___ como se chegou ao acordo.'
questions.explanation: This verb has an irregular subjunctive stem and keeps it in all persons.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive (ele) of the verb meaning 'to lose': 'O acordo foi adiado sem que o país ___ o direito de veto.'
questions.explanation: Sem que always takes the subjunctive, and the past main verb requires the imperfect.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'They negotiated for months so that all the countries would sign.'
questions.explanation: A past main verb forces the imperfect subjunctive in the purpose clause.

activities.title: Reunião sobre uma candidatura
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You explain to a colleague why a joint application is worth the paperwork, using purpose clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vamos gastar meses em papelada para receber uma verba que talvez nem chegue.
lines.en: We are going to spend months on paperwork for funding that may not even arrive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Candidatamo-nos em conjunto de modo a repartir o trabalho entre as três câmaras.
lines.en: We are applying jointly so as to share the work between the three councils.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se uma delas desistir a meio?
lines.en: And if one of them drops out halfway?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Assinamos um protocolo para que ninguém possa sair sem avisar com seis meses.
lines.en: We sign an agreement so that nobody can leave without six months' notice.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Continua a parecer-me trabalho a mais.
lines.en: It still looks like too much work to me.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É, mas sem que apresentemos um projeto comum, ficamos todos fora do concurso.
lines.en: It is, but unless we present a joint project, we are all left out of the call.

days.title: As migrações contemporâneas
days.objective: You can discuss migration with unreal conditions, using se plus pluperfect subjunctive with the compound conditional.
days.paretoFocus: Talking about what would have happened otherwise is how people explain family history and evaluate policy, and it needs a two part verb pattern that has to be automatic.
drills: Write four sentences about your own family beginning with 'se' plus a pluperfect subjunctive.
Turn three present unreal conditions into past unreal conditions.
Write two sentences with caso plus present subjunctive about a decision you may still take.

items: Say the two verb forms that make up a past unreal condition.
Recall eight words about residence permits, paperwork and qualifications.
Explain the difference between se and caso in a conditional sentence.

activities.title: O que teria acontecido
activities.intro: For a past that did not happen, Portuguese pairs se plus tivesse and a participle with teria and a participle. For a present or general hypothesis, the imperfect subjunctive pairs with the simple conditional. Caso plus the present subjunctive states a condition that is still open.

items.hr: Se o meu avô não tivesse emigrado, eu teria crescido noutro país.
items.en: If my grandfather had not emigrated, I would have grown up in another country.
items.note: tivesse + particípio / teria + particípio

items.hr: Muitos teriam ficado se houvesse trabalho digno nas suas terras.
items.en: Many would have stayed if there had been decent work back home.
items.note: the two halves can come in either order

items.hr: Se as vias legais fossem mais simples, menos gente arriscaria a vida no mar.
items.en: If the legal routes were simpler, fewer people would risk their lives at sea.
items.note: present hypothesis: fosse + condicional simples

items.hr: Ninguém emigra por gosto, emigra porque as alternativas se esgotaram.
items.en: Nobody emigrates for pleasure, they emigrate because the alternatives have run out.
items.note: por gosto is a fixed expression

items.hr: Caso o processo demore anos, a integração torna-se muito mais difícil.
items.en: If the process takes years, integration becomes much harder.
items.note: caso + presente do conjuntivo, never the indicative

activities.title: E se tivesse sido de outra maneira
activities.intro: Practise matching the two halves of a conditional sentence.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Se tivessem reconhecido o diploma dela, ela ___ exercido medicina cá.'
questions.explanation: The result of a past unreal condition uses the conditional of ter plus the participle.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Caso a autorização ___ recusada, há dez dias para recorrer.'
questions.explanation: Caso takes the present subjunctive; the future subjunctive form belongs after se.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive of ter (nós), as the auxiliary of a past unreal condition: 'Se ___ chegado dez anos antes, teríamos apanhado outro país.'
questions.explanation: The auxiliary carries the mood and person while the participle stays unchanged.

questions.prompt: Complete with the simple conditional (eles) of the verb meaning 'to leave, to depart': 'Poucos ___ se lhes dessem condições para ficar.'
questions.explanation: An imperfect subjunctive in the se clause pairs with a simple conditional in the result.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'If they had learned the language sooner, they would have found work.'
questions.explanation: Both halves are compound: subjunctive auxiliary plus participle, then conditional auxiliary plus participle.

activities.title: A história da família dele
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague tells you about his parents' move and you respond with unreal conditions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Os meus pais chegaram cá em novembro, com uma mala e sem falar uma palavra de português.
lines.en: My parents arrived here in November, with one suitcase and without a word of Portuguese.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se tivessem tido apoio na chegada, os primeiros anos teriam sido menos duros.
lines.en: If they had had support on arrival, the first years would have been less harsh.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A minha mãe era enfermeira, mas nunca lhe reconheceram o curso.
lines.en: My mother was a nurse, but they never recognised her qualification.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se lho tivessem reconhecido, ela não teria passado quinze anos a limpar escadas.
lines.en: If they had recognised it for her, she would not have spent fifteen years cleaning stairwells.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Agora dizem que faltam enfermeiros. Tem piada.
lines.en: Now they say there is a shortage of nurses. Funny, that.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Caso o país queira mesmo resolver isso, mais vale começar por quem já cá está.
lines.en: If the country really wants to solve that, it had better start with the people already here.

days.title: A igualdade entre mulheres e homens
days.objective: You can contrast two situations and concede partially, using ao passo que, em contrapartida, além disso and 'concordo em parte, mas'.
days.paretoFocus: Partial agreement plus a sharp contrast connector is the politest and most effective way to disagree in a discussion.
drills: Write four sentences contrasting two facts with 'ao passo que'.
Reply to three opinions you disagree with, each beginning with a partial agreement.
Write a short paragraph of five lines using one additive connector and two contrastive ones.

items: List three connectors that introduce a contrast and one that adds an argument.
Recall eight words about pay, careers and unpaid domestic work.
Say two ways to agree in part before objecting.

activities.title: Concordar em parte e contrapor
activities.intro: Ao passo que sets two simultaneous facts against each other. Em contrapartida introduces a compensating fact. Além disso adds a further argument on the same side. Opening with 'concordo em parte, mas' shows you have listened before you object.

items.hr: As mulheres são maioria nas universidades, ao passo que continuam minoria nos conselhos de administração.
items.en: Women are the majority at university, whereas they are still a minority on boards.
items.note: ao passo que contrasts two things that are true at once

items.hr: Concordo em parte, mas atribuir tudo à escolha individual é ignorar o contexto.
items.en: I agree up to a point, but putting it all down to individual choice ignores the context.
items.note: a standard opener for polite disagreement

items.hr: Em contrapartida, a licença parental partilhada mudou hábitos em muitas famílias.
items.en: On the other hand, shared parental leave has changed habits in many families.
items.note: em contrapartida introduces the compensating side

items.hr: Não se trata de dar privilégios, trata-se de corrigir uma desigualdade antiga.
items.en: It is not about giving privileges, it is about correcting an old inequality.
items.note: tratar-se de is impersonal and stays in the singular

items.hr: O trabalho doméstico continua a recair sobretudo sobre elas, mesmo quando ambos trabalham a tempo inteiro.
items.en: Housework still falls mainly on them, even when both work full time.
items.note: recair sobre = to fall on

activities.title: Ligar ideias opostas
activities.intro: Practise connectors of contrast, addition and partial agreement.

questions.prompt: Which connector sets two simultaneous facts against each other?
questions.explanation: The others introduce cause, consequence and time respectively.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Ganham menos pelo mesmo trabalho e, ___, são elas que interrompem a carreira.'
questions.explanation: The second fact adds to the first rather than opposing or explaining it.

questions.prompt: Complete with the noun that turns this into an agreement only up to a point: 'Concordo em ___, mas os números contam outra história.'
questions.explanation: This fixed expression signals that your agreement is limited.

questions.prompt: Complete with the third person singular of the verb used in the impersonal expression meaning 'it is about': 'Não se ___ de generosidade, é uma questão de justiça.'
questions.explanation: This impersonal construction never agrees with a plural and is always followed by de.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Wages have gone up, whereas the gap has stayed the same.'
questions.explanation: The subordinate clause pulls the pronoun in front of the verb: se manteve.

activities.title: Critérios de promoção
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A colleague says promotions at work are already fair and you object with partial agreement and contrast.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Aqui promove-se por mérito. Ninguém olha se és homem ou mulher.
lines.en: Here people are promoted on merit. Nobody looks at whether you are a man or a woman.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo em parte, mas repara que a equipa é metade mulheres e a chefia é toda masculina.
lines.en: I agree up to a point, but notice that the team is half women and the management is all male.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso é porque elas próprias não se candidatam.
lines.en: That is because they don't apply themselves.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Umas não se candidatam, ao passo que outras se candidatam e ficam sempre em segundo.
lines.en: Some don't apply, whereas others do apply and always end up second.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Em contrapartida, temos horários flexíveis, o que ajuda muito quem tem filhos.
lines.en: On the other hand, we have flexible hours, which helps a lot for people with children.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ajuda, sim. Além disso, valia a pena publicar os critérios, para toda a gente os conhecer.
lines.en: It does help. Besides, it would be worth publishing the criteria so that everyone knows them.

days.title: A deficiência e a inclusão
days.objective: You can discuss accessibility using verbs that govern the subjunctive, such as impedir que, permitir que, exigir que and fazer com que, and use person first wording.
days.paretoFocus: These four verbs appear in almost every sentence about rights and barriers, and each one forces a subjunctive clause that learners tend to get wrong.
drills: Write four sentences about your town's pavements and buildings, one with each of impedir que, permitir que, exigir que and fazer com que.
Rewrite three sentences that label people by their condition so that the person comes first.
Describe in five lines what would make a local event accessible to everyone.

items: Name four verbs that force a subjunctive in the clause that follows.
Recall eight words about ramps, subtitles, sign language and accessibility.
Say why the barrier, not the person, is usually the subject of these sentences.

activities.title: A barreira não está na pessoa
activities.intro: Verbs of influence and obligation govern the subjunctive when the second clause has a different subject: impedir que, permitir que, exigir que, fazer com que. In current Portuguese usage the person comes before the condition: uma pessoa com deficiência, not a label. Notice that the subject of these sentences is usually the barrier itself.

items.hr: Não é a cadeira de rodas que limita, é o passeio sem rampa que impede que a pessoa circule.
items.en: It is not the wheelchair that limits people, it is the pavement with no ramp that stops a person from getting around.
items.note: impedir que + conjuntivo

items.hr: Dizemos pessoa com deficiência e não deficiente, porque a pessoa vem primeiro.
items.en: We say person with a disability rather than disabled, because the person comes first.
items.note: person first wording is the current standard

items.hr: A legendagem e a audiodescrição permitem que muito mais gente aceda ao mesmo conteúdo.
items.en: Subtitling and audio description allow far more people to access the same content.
items.note: permitir que + conjuntivo

items.hr: A lei exige que os edifícios públicos sejam acessíveis, mas a fiscalização é fraca.
items.en: The law requires public buildings to be accessible, but enforcement is weak.
items.note: exigir que + conjuntivo

items.hr: A falta de intérpretes de língua gestual faz com que muitos processos fiquem parados.
items.en: The lack of sign language interpreters means that many procedures get stuck.
items.note: fazer com que + conjuntivo

activities.title: Verbos que exigem conjuntivo
activities.intro: Practise the subjunctive after verbs of influence and the wording that puts the person first.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'O ruído da sala impede que os alunos com surdez parcial ___ o professor.'
questions.explanation: Impedir que introduces a clause with its own subject and requires the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Which wording puts the person before the condition?
questions.explanation: The noun is the person and the condition is added afterwards, which is the wording associations themselves ask for.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (ela) of the verb meaning 'to install': 'A associação exige que a câmara ___ o piso tátil até ao final do ano.'
questions.explanation: Exigir que always governs the subjunctive, here in the present because the demand is current.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (ela) of the verb meaning 'to stay, to remain': 'A ausência de transporte adaptado faz com que muita gente ___ em casa.'
questions.explanation: Fazer com que introduces a consequence clause and takes the subjunctive; gente is grammatically singular.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The law requires the ramps to be free of obstacles.'
questions.explanation: Where English uses an infinitive after require, Portuguese needs que plus a subjunctive verb.

activities.title: Preparar um evento para todos
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are organising a local event and argue for accessibility using verbs of influence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A sala tem três degraus à entrada, mas nunca ninguém se queixou.
lines.en: The room has three steps at the entrance, but nobody has ever complained.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ninguém se queixa porque os degraus impedem que essas pessoas cheguem sequer à porta.
lines.en: Nobody complains because the steps stop those people from even reaching the door.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Uma rampa custa dinheiro que não temos este ano.
lines.en: A ramp costs money we don't have this year.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Há rampas amovíveis. E a lei exige que o espaço seja acessível, não que seja caro.
lines.en: There are removable ramps. And the law requires the space to be accessible, not expensive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quem for surdo? Não vamos ter intérprete.
lines.en: And anyone who is deaf? We are not going to have an interpreter.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Podemos legendar a apresentação, o que já permite que muita gente acompanhe tudo.
lines.en: We can subtitle the presentation, which already lets a lot of people follow everything.

days.title: A condição animal e a ética
days.objective: You can argue about animal ethics using the subjunctive in relative clauses, the expression há quem, and hedging forms such as seria de esperar.
days.paretoFocus: The subjunctive inside a relative clause is what marks something as sought or non existent rather than known, a distinction that changes the meaning of the sentence.
drills: Write three sentences with 'não conheço nenhum ... que' plus subjunctive.
Write three sentences opening with 'há quem' and a subjunctive verb.
State two opinions on animal ethics using hedged forms such as seria de esperar or deveria.

items: Explain when a relative clause takes the subjunctive instead of the indicative.
Recall eight words about farming, welfare and labelling.
Say one hedged criticism using a conditional verb form.

activities.title: Direitos ou bem-estar
activities.intro: A relative clause takes the indicative when the thing exists and is known, and the subjunctive when it is only wished for, sought, or denied. Há quem always takes the subjunctive. Conditional forms such as seria and deveria soften a criticism and are typical of careful argument.

items.hr: Há quem defenda que os animais têm direitos e não apenas o direito a não sofrer.
items.en: There are people who argue that animals have rights, not just the right not to suffer.
items.note: há quem + conjuntivo, always third person singular

items.hr: Não conheço nenhum argumento que justifique o abate por mera conveniência.
items.en: I know of no argument that justifies culling out of sheer convenience.
items.note: a denied antecedent forces the subjunctive

items.hr: Seria de esperar que a lei distinguisse animal de companhia e animal de produção.
items.en: You would expect the law to distinguish between companion animals and farm animals.
items.note: seria de esperar + imperfeito do conjuntivo

items.hr: Procura-se um modelo de criação que respeite o bem-estar sem inviabilizar o setor.
items.en: What is needed is a farming model that respects welfare without making the sector unviable.
items.note: the model does not yet exist, hence the subjunctive

items.hr: Muitos deixaram de comer carne, outros limitaram-se a reduzir o consumo.
items.en: Many have stopped eating meat, others have merely cut down.
items.note: limitar-se a + infinitivo = to do no more than

activities.title: Existe ou procura-se
activities.intro: Practise choosing indicative or subjunctive inside a relative clause.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Não há nenhuma quinta na zona que ___ certificação de bem-estar animal.'
questions.explanation: The antecedent is denied, so the relative clause goes into the present subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Há quem ___ que a questão é sobretudo cultural.'
questions.explanation: Há quem refers to an unidentified group and always takes the third person singular subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive (ela) of the verb meaning 'to include': 'Seria de esperar que a legislação ___ também os animais selvagens em cativeiro.'
questions.explanation: A conditional main verb pulls the subordinate clause into the imperfect subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (ele) of the verb meaning 'to reduce': 'Queremos um rótulo que ___ a confusão do consumidor.'
questions.explanation: The label is wanted but does not yet exist, so the relative clause takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We need rules that protect these animals.'
questions.explanation: Precisar governs de, and the rules are only wished for, so the relative verb is subjunctive.

activities.title: Discussão ao jantar
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. A friend defends current farming practices and you answer with hedged arguments and subjunctive relative clauses.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Comer carne é natural. Sempre foi assim e ninguém morreu disso.
lines.en: Eating meat is natural. It has always been that way and nobody died of it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não é o comer que discuto, é a criação intensiva. Há quem separe muito bem as duas coisas.
lines.en: It is not the eating I am questioning, it is intensive farming. Some people separate the two things very clearly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E querias o quê? Carne a dez euros o quilo?
lines.en: And what would you want? Meat at ten euros a kilo?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Seria de esperar que o preço refletisse o custo real, incluindo o que os animais pagam.
lines.en: You would expect the price to reflect the real cost, including what the animals pay.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Isso é para quem pode. As famílias com pouco dinheiro compram o mais barato.
lines.en: That is for those who can afford it. Families with little money buy the cheapest.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Por isso é que não conheço nenhuma solução que funcione sem apoio a quem ganha menos.
lines.en: That is exactly why I know of no solution that works without support for those who earn less.

days.title: O consumo responsável
days.objective: You can set conditions and reservations on your choices using desde que, contanto que, a menos que, a não ser que and nem que.
days.paretoFocus: These connectors turn a flat opinion into a qualified position, and all of them require the subjunctive, which is exactly where B1 speakers stall.
drills: Write four sentences about shopping habits, one for each of desde que, a menos que, nem que and caso.
Add a reservation to three opinions you already hold, using a subjunctive clause.
Write five lines arguing for repairing rather than replacing, with two conditional connectors.

items: List four connectors that introduce a condition with the subjunctive.
Recall eight words about waste, repair, second hand goods and labelling.
Say the difference in meaning between desde que and a menos que.

activities.title: Comprar menos, comprar melhor
activities.intro: Desde que and contanto que set a condition that must be met. A menos que and a não ser que set the exception that would cancel your statement. Nem que means even if, presenting an extreme case. All of them take the subjunctive, never the indicative.

items.hr: Comprar em segunda mão faz todo o sentido, desde que não se compre o dobro.
items.en: Buying second hand makes complete sense, as long as you don't buy twice as much.
items.note: desde que + conjuntivo

items.hr: A menos que mudemos a forma de produzir, reciclar não chega.
items.en: Unless we change the way we produce, recycling is not enough.
items.note: a menos que states the exception that would undo the claim

items.hr: Continuarei a comprar aqui perto, nem que fique um pouco mais caro.
items.en: I will go on buying locally, even if it works out slightly more expensive.
items.note: nem que = even if, with the subjunctive

items.hr: Só faz sentido rotular um produto de sustentável contanto que toda a cadeia seja verificada.
items.en: It only makes sense to label a product sustainable provided the whole chain is checked.
items.note: contanto que is more formal than desde que

items.hr: Reparar em vez de substituir é a decisão mais ecológica que existe.
items.en: Repairing instead of replacing is the most environmentally sound decision there is.
items.note: em vez de + infinitivo

activities.title: Condições e ressalvas
activities.intro: Practise conditional connectors and the subjunctive they require.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Levo sempre o saco de casa, a não ser que me ___ completamente.'
questions.explanation: A não ser que introduces an exception and always takes the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Which pair introduces a condition with the subjunctive?
questions.explanation: The second pair gives causes with the indicative, the third gives time with the future subjunctive, and the fourth gives consequences.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (nós) of the verb meaning 'to reduce': 'Nada muda a menos que ___ o desperdício em casa.'
questions.explanation: A menos que takes the subjunctive; this verb keeps the z of the stem in all its subjunctive forms.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present subjunctive (ele) of the verb meaning 'to cost': 'Compro este casaco desde que ___ menos do que o outro.'
questions.explanation: Desde que sets a condition and therefore governs the subjunctive.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Unless we buy less, nothing is going to change.'
questions.explanation: The exception clause comes first with a subjunctive verb, and the main clause stays in the indicative.

activities.title: Reparar ou comprar um novo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your phone is broken and you argue for repairing it, using conditional connectors.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Com esse ecrã partido, mais vale comprares um telemóvel novo.
lines.en: With that cracked screen, you may as well buy a new phone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prefiro repará-lo, nem que demore duas semanas a ficar pronto.
lines.en: I would rather have it repaired, even if it takes two weeks to be ready.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A reparação fica quase pelo mesmo preço de um em segunda mão.
lines.en: The repair costs almost the same as a second hand one.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fica, mas o mais ecológico é sempre o aparelho que já temos, desde que ainda funcione.
lines.en: It does, but the greenest device is always the one we already have, as long as it still works.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se a bateria durar meio dia?
lines.en: And if the battery lasts half a day?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Troco a bateria. A menos que a placa esteja queimada, não vale a pena deitar tudo fora.
lines.en: I'll change the battery. Unless the board is burnt out, there is no point throwing it all away.

days.title: A memória histórica e a identidade
days.objective: You can report what someone said about the past, shifting tenses correctly into the imperfect, the pluperfect and the conditional.
days.paretoFocus: Reporting other people's words is half of everyday conversation, and Portuguese moves every tense one step back exactly as the sequence of tenses requires.
drills: Report five sentences a relative once told you, all introduced by disse que or contou que.
Take three direct questions and turn them into reported questions with perguntou se.
Write five lines summarising a conversation about the past, without any direct quotation.

items: Say what the present, the perfect and the future become in reported speech after a past verb.
Recall eight words about memory, archives, testimony and silence.
Give one reported sentence with a conditional verb.

activities.title: O que os mais velhos contavam
activities.intro: After a reporting verb in the past, the present becomes the imperfect, the simple past and the perfect become the pluperfect with tinha, and vou plus infinitive becomes ia plus infinitive. A future in a time clause becomes the imperfect subjunctive. Time and place words shift too: hoje becomes nesse dia, aqui becomes ali.

items.hr: O meu avô dizia que tinha havido silêncio durante quarenta anos.
items.en: My grandfather used to say that there had been silence for forty years.
items.note: houve -> tinha havido

items.hr: Perguntaram-lhe se ainda se lembrava daquela madrugada e ele respondeu que se lembrava de tudo.
items.en: They asked him whether he still remembered that dawn and he answered that he remembered everything.
items.note: reported questions use se, with no question mark

items.hr: Disse que iria contar tudo aos netos quando eles fossem mais velhos.
items.en: He said he would tell his grandchildren everything when they were older.
items.note: vou contar -> iria contar; quando forem -> quando fossem

items.hr: Contou que nunca tinha falado daquilo com ninguém.
items.en: He told us he had never talked about it with anyone.
items.note: nunca falei -> nunca tinha falado

items.hr: Explicou que a memória não era o passado, mas aquilo que fazemos com ele.
items.en: He explained that memory was not the past, but what we do with it.
items.note: the present stays only for general truths

activities.title: Passar ao discurso indireto
activities.intro: Practise shifting tenses after a reporting verb in the past.

questions.prompt: Report this in the past: 'Não sei nada disso.' becomes 'Ele afirmou que ___ nada daquilo.'
questions.explanation: A present tense in the original becomes the imperfect after a past reporting verb.

questions.prompt: Report this in the past: 'Vou publicar as cartas.' becomes 'Ela avisou que ___ as cartas.'
questions.explanation: The near future with ir becomes the imperfect of ir plus the infinitive.

questions.prompt: Complete with the past participle of the verb meaning 'to see': 'Jurou que tinha ___ os tanques na rua naquela manhã.'
questions.explanation: The pluperfect uses tinha plus a participle, and this verb has an irregular one.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfect subjunctive (eles) of the verb meaning 'to want': 'Prometeu que mostraria os documentos se os historiadores ___.'
questions.explanation: A conditional in the reported clause pairs with the imperfect subjunctive in the se clause.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'She explained that the archive would open the following year.'
questions.explanation: A future in the original becomes the conditional, and no ano que vem becomes no ano seguinte.

activities.title: As gravações da tia
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You report to a cousin what an elderly aunt told you about the family's past.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, ela aceitou ser gravada? Pensei que nunca falasse do assunto.
lines.en: So she agreed to be recorded? I thought she never talked about it.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aceitou. Disse que já tinha esperado tempo de mais para contar aquilo.
lines.en: She did. She said she had already waited far too long to tell it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E falou do irmão que emigrou em sessenta e três?
lines.en: And did she talk about the brother who emigrated in sixty three?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Contou que ele tinha partido a pé e que a mãe nunca mais dormira descansada.
lines.en: She told me he had left on foot and that their mother never slept peacefully again.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Achas que ela deixa ouvir isso aos mais novos?
lines.en: Do you think she will let the younger ones hear that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Explicou que só mostraria as gravações quando todos quisessem mesmo ouvir.
lines.en: She explained that she would only show the recordings when everyone really wanted to listen.

days.title: O voluntariado e a sociedade civil
days.objective: You can talk about volunteering while placing unstressed pronouns correctly, after the verb by default and before it after negatives, conjunctions and question words.
days.paretoFocus: Pronoun placement is the clearest single marker of European Portuguese, and getting it wrong makes otherwise good Portuguese sound foreign.
drills: Write six sentences with me, te, nos, lhe or o, three with the pronoun after the verb and three with it before.
Turn three affirmative sentences into negatives and move the pronoun accordingly.
Write two sentences in the future with the pronoun inserted inside the verb.

items: List four words that pull the pronoun in front of the verb.
Say what happens to the verb ending before the pronouns o, a, os and as.
Recall eight words about associations, shifts, donations and local groups.

activities.title: Inscrevi-me e não me arrependo
activities.intro: In Portugal the unstressed pronoun normally comes after the verb, joined by a hyphen. It moves in front of the verb after negative words, after most conjunctions and relative pronouns, after question words and after adverbs like já and também. In simple future and conditional forms it goes inside the verb.

items.hr: Inscrevi-me numa associação de bairro e não me arrependo.
items.en: I signed up with a neighbourhood association and I do not regret it.
items.note: after the verb by default, before it after não

items.hr: Ninguém nos obrigou a ir, fomos porque quisemos.
items.en: Nobody forced us to go, we went because we wanted to.
items.note: ninguém is a negative word and pulls the pronoun forward

items.hr: Quando me pediram ajuda, aceitei sem hesitar.
items.en: When they asked me for help, I accepted without hesitating.
items.note: a subordinate conjunction pulls the pronoun forward

items.hr: Vamos organizá-lo em conjunto com a junta de freguesia.
items.en: We are going to organise it together with the parish council.
items.note: organizar + o -> organizá-lo, with the r dropped

items.hr: Dar-lhes-emos formação antes de começarem.
items.en: We will give them training before they start.
items.note: in the future the pronoun sits inside the verb

activities.title: Onde fica o pronome
activities.intro: Practise pronoun placement in European Portuguese.

questions.prompt: Which sentence follows European Portuguese pronoun placement?
questions.explanation: A negative word before the verb pulls the pronoun in front of it, with no hyphen.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'Se quiseres participar, ___ até sexta-feira.'
questions.explanation: An affirmative command takes the pronoun after the verb, joined by a hyphen.

questions.prompt: Complete with one word: the verb meaning 'to help' joined to the object pronoun for them, masculine: 'Fomos lá para ___ a montar as tendas.'
questions.explanation: Before this pronoun the final r of the infinitive drops and the stressed vowel takes an acute accent.

questions.prompt: Complete with the first person singular pronoun that nunca pulls in front of the verb: 'Nunca ___ arrependi de ter aceitado aquele turno.'
questions.explanation: Nunca is a negative adverb, so the reflexive pronoun goes before the verb with no hyphen.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'They asked me to coordinate the group.'
questions.explanation: Nothing precedes the verb, so the pronoun attaches to it with a hyphen.

activities.title: Na sede da associação
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are signing up as a volunteer and the coordinator explains how it works, with pronouns in both positions.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa tarde. Quer inscrever-se como voluntário ou só pedir informações?
lines.en: Good afternoon. Do you want to sign up as a volunteer or just ask for information?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Quero inscrever-me, mas não me comprometo a mais de uma tarde por semana.
lines.en: I want to sign up, but I can't commit to more than one afternoon a week.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ninguém lhe vai exigir mais do que isso. As escalas fazem-se de mês a mês.
lines.en: Nobody is going to demand more than that. The rotas are drawn up month by month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E a formação? Dão-na antes de começarmos?
lines.en: And the training? Do you give it before we start?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Damos. Quando o grupo estiver completo, avisamo-lo por telefone.
lines.en: We do. When the group is full, we let you know by phone.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então deixo-lhe o meu contacto e trago o formulário preenchido na quinta-feira.
lines.en: Then I'll leave you my number and bring the completed form on Thursday.

days.title: Analisar um texto argumentativo: tese, argumentos, exemplos
days.objective: You can break an argumentative Portuguese text into its thesis, its supporting arguments and its examples, and describe each part in Portuguese.
days.paretoFocus: Reading tasks in the exam almost always ask what the author defends and how, so naming the parts turns a vague impression into a precise answer.
drills: Take any opinion text in Portuguese and write its thesis in one sentence of fifteen words or fewer.
Mark every example in that text and label each one as a figure, a fact or a single case.
Write two sentences starting with 'O autor defende que' and 'Para o provar, recorre a'.

items: Say aloud the difference between an argument and an example.
Recall three Portuguese verbs used to attribute a position to an author.
Explain where the thesis usually sits in an argumentative text and why.

activities.title: A anatomia de um texto de opinião
activities.intro: An argumentative text has three layers: one position, several reasons for it, and illustrations of those reasons. Exam questions target each layer separately, so you need to tell them apart quickly. These sentences give you the Portuguese wording to name what you find.

items.hr: A tese é a posição que o autor defende, geralmente enunciada no primeiro ou no último parágrafo.
items.en: The thesis is the position the author defends, usually stated in the first or in the last paragraph.
items.note: a tese = the claim, not the topic

items.hr: Cada argumento é uma razão que sustenta a tese e cabe sempre numa frase.
items.en: Each argument is a reason that supports the thesis and always fits into one sentence.
items.note: sustentar uma tese = to back up a claim

items.hr: O exemplo não prova nada sozinho: serve para ilustrar o argumento a que pertence.
items.en: The example proves nothing on its own: it serves to illustrate the argument it belongs to.
items.note: ilustrar is not the same as provar

items.hr: Expressões como 'com efeito' ou 'de facto' anunciam uma justificação e não uma ideia nova.
items.en: Expressions such as 'com efeito' or 'de facto' announce a justification and not a new idea.
items.note: 'facto' keeps the c in European Portuguese

items.hr: Quando o autor escreve 'é certo que', está a preparar uma objeção que vai rebater logo a seguir.
items.en: When the author writes 'é certo que', he is preparing an objection that he will refute straight afterwards.
items.note: estar a + infinitive for an action in progress

activities.title: Localizar a tese e os argumentos
activities.intro: Practise telling a defended position apart from the material used to support it.

questions.prompt: Which of these sentences works as a thesis rather than as an example?
options: Em 2023, apenas um terço dos inquiridos leu um livro.
O teletrabalho deve ser um direito e não um favor da empresa.
A minha vizinha trabalha em casa desde março.
Segundo um estudo recente, o trânsito diminuiu na baixa.
questions.answer: O teletrabalho deve ser um direito e não um favor da empresa.
questions.explanation: A thesis states a position that someone could contradict. The other three report a figure or a single case, which is the raw material of examples.

questions.prompt: Which connector signals that the author is conceding a point before refuting it?
questions.explanation: 'É certo que' opens a concession. 'Por conseguinte' draws a conclusion, 'em suma' sums up and 'ou seja' reformulates.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present tense of the verb meaning to back up an idea: 'Cada argumento é uma razão que ___ a posição defendida.'
questions.explanation: 'Sustentar' is the standard verb for an argument that holds up a claim. 'Apoiar' is possible but less formal in written analysis.

questions.prompt: Fill the gap with the preposition that opens the adverbial announcing a justification: 'A medida falhou; ___ efeito, ninguém foi consultado.'
questions.explanation: 'Com efeito' introduces the reason for the statement just made. It never introduces a new idea, which is why it marks an argument and not a thesis.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The author defends that the measure is unfair.'
questions.explanation: 'Defender que' takes the indicative when the author is reporting a firm position.

activities.title: A preparar a prova de leitura
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You help a friend separate the thesis, the arguments and the examples in a text he has just read.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Li o texto duas vezes e continuo sem perceber qual é a tese.
lines.en: I have read the text twice and I still cannot see what the thesis is.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Procura a frase que podes contradizer. Se ninguém consegue discordar dela, não é a tese.
lines.en: Look for the sentence you can contradict. If nobody can disagree with it, it is not the thesis.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então 'o número de leitores caiu' não serve?
lines.en: So 'the number of readers has fallen' is no good?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, isso é um dado. A tese seria 'as escolas deviam dedicar mais tempo à leitura'.
lines.en: No, that is a piece of data. The thesis would be 'schools should devote more time to reading'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E os exemplos, como é que os distingo dos argumentos?
lines.en: And the examples, how do I tell them apart from the arguments?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: O argumento explica porquê, o exemplo mostra um caso. Sublinha os argumentos e deixa os exemplos entre parênteses.
lines.en: The argument explains why, the example shows a case. Underline the arguments and put the examples in brackets.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Vou tentar assim no próximo texto.
lines.en: I will try it that way with the next text.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Faz isso e depois resume-me a tese numa só frase.
lines.en: Do that and then sum the thesis up for me in a single sentence.

days.title: Identificar e refutar um argumento falacioso
days.objective: You can name the flaw in a weak argument and refute it in Portuguese in three moves: concede, name the error, give a counterexample.
days.paretoFocus: Both the writing and the speaking parts reward a candidate who answers the reasoning instead of repeating an opinion louder.
drills: Write down three claims you have heard recently and mark which ones generalise from one case.
Turn one of them into a polite refutation of three sentences in Portuguese.
Say aloud a counterexample for each of the three claims, without raising your voice.

items: Recall the three moves of a clean refutation, in order.
Give one Portuguese phrase that concedes a point before disagreeing.
Explain in English why attacking the speaker weakens your own position.

activities.title: Falácias e como as desmontar
activities.intro: A faulty argument can be dismantled without any raised voice. The reliable pattern is to accept whatever is true, name the logical error, and then produce one case the argument cannot explain. These sentences give you the Portuguese for each move.

items.hr: Quando alguém responde à pessoa e não ao argumento, está a fugir da discussão.
items.en: When somebody answers the person and not the argument, they are dodging the discussion.
items.note: fugir de = to run away from

items.hr: A generalização abusiva parte de um caso isolado para concluir sobre toda a gente.
items.en: The hasty generalisation starts from one isolated case in order to draw a conclusion about everybody.
items.note: toda a gente takes a singular verb

items.hr: Para refutar, começa por reconhecer o que é válido: 'Compreendo o teu ponto de vista'.
items.en: To refute, start by acknowledging what is valid: 'I understand your point of view'.
items.note: tu form, normal between peers in Portugal

items.hr: Depois nomeia o erro: 'O raciocínio confunde uma coincidência com uma causa'.
items.en: Then name the error: 'The reasoning confuses a coincidence with a cause'.
items.note: confundir A com B

items.hr: Por fim, apresenta um contraexemplo concreto que o argumento não consegue explicar.
items.en: Finally, present a concrete counterexample that the argument cannot explain.
items.note: contraexemplo is written as one word

activities.title: Desmontar o raciocínio
activities.intro: Practise separating an attack on the person from a genuine answer to the reasoning.

questions.prompt: Which reply refutes the argument instead of attacking the speaker?
options: Isso é o que diz quem nunca trabalhou.
Tu nem sequer és da área.
Esse número refere-se a 2010 e a situação mudou desde então.
Só um ingénuo acreditaria numa coisa dessas.
questions.answer: Esse número refere-se a 2010 e a situação mudou desde então.
questions.explanation: Only this reply engages with the evidence. The other three judge the speaker, which leaves the original argument untouched.

questions.prompt: 'Desde que a lei entrou em vigor, os acidentes aumentaram, logo a lei causou os acidentes.' Which flaw is this?
options: Generalização a partir de um único caso
questions.explanation: Two facts happening one after the other are not proof that the first produced the second. Anything else that changed in the same period could explain the rise.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present tense of the verb meaning to mix two things up: 'O raciocínio ___ uma coincidência com uma causa.'
questions.explanation: 'Confundir' is the standard verb for naming this error, and it takes the pattern confundir A com B.

questions.prompt: Complete the concession that opens a polite refutation: 'Compreendo o teu ponto de vista, ___ os dados apontam noutra direção.'
questions.explanation: A concession only works if an adversative connector opens the refutation, otherwise the listener hears agreement.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'This argument is based on an isolated case.'
questions.explanation: In an affirmative main clause with nothing before the verb, the pronoun goes after it: baseia-se, not se baseia.

activities.title: Não me convences com isso
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner defends a sweeping claim and you dismantle it calmly, move by move.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Toda a gente sabe que quem estuda línguas sozinho nunca aprende a falar.
lines.en: Everybody knows that people who study languages on their own never learn to speak.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Toda a gente? Isso é uma generalização a partir de dois ou três casos.
lines.en: Everybody? That is a generalisation built on two or three cases.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Conheço três pessoas que desistiram ao fim de um mês.
lines.en: I know three people who gave up after a month.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Três pessoas não são uma prova, e eu conheço quem tenha chegado a um nível avançado assim.
lines.en: Three people are not proof, and I know someone who has reached an advanced level that way.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas tu nem sequer és professor de línguas.
lines.en: But you are not even a language teacher.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estás a atacar-me a mim e não ao argumento. Responde antes ao contraexemplo.
lines.en: You are attacking me and not the argument. Answer the counterexample instead.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Está bem, admito que o meu exemplo era fraco.
lines.en: All right, I admit that my example was weak.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Compreendo o teu ponto de vista, mas precisas de dados e não de conhecidos.
lines.en: I understand your point of view, but you need data and not acquaintances.

days.title: A síntese de dois documentos contraditórios
days.objective: You can write a neutral Portuguese synthesis of two texts that disagree, organised by theme and with every idea attributed to its source.
days.paretoFocus: Synthesis is the writing task where candidates lose most marks by slipping into personal opinion or by writing two summaries in a row.
drills: Find two short Portuguese texts on the same subject and list the themes they share.
Write one paragraph that crosses both texts on a single theme, without using the word 'eu'.
Write the closing sentence that states exactly where the two sources disagree.

items: Say why a synthesis must never be organised text by text.
Recall two Portuguese phrases that attribute an idea to a specific source.
Explain what belongs in the final sentence of a synthesis.

activities.title: Sintetizar sem tomar partido
activities.intro: A synthesis reports a disagreement, it does not settle it. The reader must always know which text an idea comes from, and the structure must be thematic so the two voices meet in every paragraph. These sentences supply the neutral wording you need.

items.hr: Numa síntese, a tua opinião não entra: o teu papel é confrontar as duas fontes.
items.en: In a synthesis your opinion has no place: your role is to set the two sources against each other.
items.note: confrontar = to put side by side

items.hr: Organiza por temas e nunca documento a documento, para não escreveres dois resumos seguidos.
items.en: Organise by theme and never text by text, so that you do not write two summaries in a row.
items.note: para não escreveres = personal infinitive

items.hr: Marca sempre a origem de cada ideia: 'O primeiro documento sublinha os salários'.
items.en: Always mark where each idea comes from: 'The first document stresses wages'.
items.note: sublinhar = to stress, to highlight

items.hr: Onde as fontes convergem, escreve 'ambos os textos concordam em que o problema é antigo'.
items.en: Where the sources agree, write 'both texts agree that the problem is an old one'.
items.note: ambos os + masculine plural noun

items.hr: Termina com o ponto exato do desacordo, sem o resolveres.
items.en: Finish with the exact point of disagreement, without resolving it.
items.note: sem o resolveres, clitic before the personal infinitive

activities.title: Confrontar duas fontes
activities.intro: Practise the neutral register and the thematic structure a synthesis requires.

questions.prompt: Which opening sentence belongs to a synthesis and not to an opinion piece?
options: Na minha opinião, o primeiro texto tem toda a razão.
Ambos os textos abordam o custo da habitação, mas divergem quanto à causa.
Acho que este debate está mal colocado desde o início.
