Vou explicar porque discordo do segundo autor.
questions.answer: Ambos os textos abordam o custo da habitação, mas divergem quanto à causa.
questions.explanation: It names the shared theme and the point of divergence without the writer taking sides, which is exactly the job of a synthesis.

questions.prompt: How should a synthesis of two conflicting documents be structured?
options: Um parágrafo por documento, pela ordem em que aparecem
Por temas, cruzando os dois documentos em cada parágrafo
Pela ordem cronológica dos factos citados
Do argumento mais fraco para o argumento mais forte
questions.answer: Por temas, cruzando os dois documentos em cada parágrafo
questions.explanation: Only a thematic plan forces the two sources to meet. A paragraph per document produces two summaries and no comparison.

questions.prompt: Complete the adverbial that marks the opposition between the sources: 'O segundo texto, pelo ___, insiste na falta de oferta.'
questions.explanation: 'Pelo contrário' placed between commas signals that the second source states the opposite of what was just reported.

questions.prompt: Complete with the Portuguese word for 'both' before a masculine plural noun: '___ os textos citam o mesmo relatório.'
questions.explanation: 'Ambos' agrees in gender and number and keeps the definite article after it: ambos os textos, ambas as fontes.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The two texts disagree about the cause.'
questions.explanation: 'Divergir quanto a' is the neutral verb for reporting disagreement between sources, with the contraction a + a giving à.

activities.title: Dois textos, uma síntese
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner has two contradictory articles and does not know how to combine them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tenho aqui dois artigos sobre o preço das casas e dizem o contrário um do outro.
lines.en: I have two articles here about house prices and they say the opposite of each other.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda melhor. Faz uma lista dos temas comuns antes de escreveres uma linha.
lines.en: Even better. Make a list of the shared themes before you write a single line.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ambos falam de salários e de oferta de habitação.
lines.en: Both talk about wages and about the supply of housing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então já tens dois parágrafos. Em cada um mostras o que cada texto defende.
lines.en: Then you already have two paragraphs. In each one you show what each text argues.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E digo no fim qual deles tem razão?
lines.en: And do I say at the end which of them is right?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não. Numa síntese a tua opinião fica de fora; limita-te a marcar onde eles discordam.
lines.en: No. In a synthesis your opinion stays out; just mark where they disagree.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E como é que acabo o texto?
lines.en: And how do I finish the text?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fecha com o ponto exato do desacordo, sem o resolveres.
lines.en: Close with the exact point of disagreement, without resolving it.

days.title: Redigir uma introdução e uma conclusão eficazes
days.objective: You can open a Portuguese argumentative text with context, a central question and an announced plan, and close it with an answer plus a short widening.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners form an impression in the first six lines and confirm it in the last four, so these two paragraphs carry more weight than any other.
drills: Write a three-move introduction of no more than six lines on a topic of your choice.
Rewrite an old introduction of yours by deleting every sentence that says nothing.
Write a conclusion that answers the question and widens the subject in two lines.

items: Name the three movements of an effective introduction.
Say what a conclusion must never contain.
Recall the Portuguese phrase used to ask to what extent something holds.

activities.title: Abrir e fechar com eficácia
activities.intro: An introduction does three jobs in a few lines: it anchors the subject in something real, it states the question, and it announces the parts. A conclusion answers that same question and then lifts the subject one step wider. Anything else is padding.

items.hr: Uma boa introdução tem três movimentos: o contexto, o problema e o anúncio do plano.
items.en: A good introduction has three movements: the context, the problem and the announcement of the plan.
items.note: o anúncio do plano = telling the reader the parts

items.hr: Evita começar por 'Desde sempre que o ser humano se interroga': é vago e faz perder tempo.
items.en: Avoid opening with 'Human beings have always wondered': it is vague and wastes time.
items.note: desde sempre que + indicative, a common empty opener

items.hr: A pergunta central deve caber numa frase: 'Até que ponto vale a pena limitar o turismo?'
items.en: The central question must fit into one sentence: 'To what extent is it worth limiting tourism?'
items.note: valer a pena + infinitive

items.hr: A conclusão responde à pergunta inicial e não introduz argumentos novos.
items.en: The conclusion answers the initial question and does not introduce new arguments.
items.note: responder a + noun

items.hr: O último movimento é o alargamento: em duas linhas, liga o tema a uma questão mais vasta.
items.en: The last movement is the widening: in two lines, link the subject to a broader question.
items.note: alargamento = opening out at the end

activities.title: Introduções e conclusões
activities.intro: Practise the wording that makes an opening and a closing paragraph do real work.

questions.prompt: Which sentence works best as the problem statement of an introduction?
options: Vamos falar um pouco sobre o teletrabalho.
Até que ponto o teletrabalho beneficia realmente quem trabalha?
Este texto está dividido em três partes.
questions.answer: Até que ponto o teletrabalho beneficia realmente quem trabalha?
questions.explanation: It formulates a debatable question in one sentence. The others announce a topic, state a vague claim, or describe the structure without posing a problem.

questions.prompt: What must a conclusion avoid at all costs?
options: Retomar em poucas linhas as ideias principais
Apresentar um argumento que ainda não foi tratado
Alargar o tema a uma questão mais vasta
questions.answer: Apresentar um argumento que ainda não foi tratado
questions.explanation: A new argument in the final paragraph cannot be developed or answered, so it makes the whole text look unfinished.

questions.prompt: Complete the announced plan with the plural noun that opposes 'custos': 'Analisaremos primeiro os custos e, em seguida, os ___.'
questions.explanation: Announcing two opposed elements tells the reader exactly how many parts to expect and in which order.

questions.prompt: Complete with the Portuguese for 'to what extent': '___ ponto é legítimo limitar o direito à greve?'
questions.explanation: 'Até que ponto' turns a yes or no topic into a question of degree, which is what an argumentative text needs.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The conclusion answers the initial question.'
questions.explanation: 'Responder' takes the preposition a, which contracts with the feminine article into à.

activities.title: A minha introdução está fraca
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner has written a long empty opening and you cut it down to three movements.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A minha introdução tem doze linhas e ainda não disse nada de concreto.
lines.en: My introduction is twelve lines long and it has not said anything concrete yet.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Corta o princípio. Começa por um facto atual em duas linhas.
lines.en: Cut the beginning. Start with a current fact in two lines.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a seguir?
lines.en: And after that?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: A seguir formulas a pergunta central e anuncias as partes. Três movimentos, mais nada.
lines.en: After that you formulate the central question and announce the parts. Three movements, nothing else.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: A conclusão posso deixar mesmo para o fim?
lines.en: Can I really leave the conclusion for the very end?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pensa nela antes de escreveres. Se sabes onde queres chegar, o texto anda sozinho.
lines.en: Think it out before you write. If you know where you want to arrive, the text moves by itself.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E posso acrescentar um argumento forte no último parágrafo?
lines.en: And can I add a strong argument in the last paragraph?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nunca. Respondes à pergunta e alargas o tema em duas linhas.
lines.en: Never. You answer the question and widen the subject in two lines.

days.title: O plano dialético: tese, antítese, síntese
days.objective: You can build a three-part argumentative plan in Portuguese where the second part shows the limits of the first and the third displaces the question.
days.paretoFocus: A visible three-part plan is the single fastest way to show an examiner that you can handle nuance instead of one flat opinion.
drills: Pick a yes or no question and write three reasons for yes in keyword form.
Write two limits of that same position, in full Portuguese sentences.
Write one sentence that moves the question somewhere else entirely.

items: Say what the second part of a dialectical plan does that a simple opposite does not.
Recall a formal adversative adverb used between commas.
Explain in English what it means to displace a question in the third part.

activities.title: Tese, antítese, síntese
activities.intro: The dialectical plan defends a position, then shows where that position stops working, then moves the discussion onto better ground. The third part is not a compromise between the first two: it changes what the question is about. Transitions between the parts are what makes the plan visible.

items.hr: O plano dialético desenvolve-se em três tempos: uma posição, a sua contestação e uma saída.
items.en: The dialectical plan unfolds in three moments: a position, its challenge and a way out.
items.note: desenvolve-se, enclitic pronoun in an affirmative clause

items.hr: Na primeira parte, defende a tese com convicção, mesmo que não seja a tua.
items.en: In the first part, defend the thesis with conviction, even if it is not your own.
items.note: mesmo que + subjunctive

items.hr: A antítese não é o contrário mecânico: mostra os limites da primeira posição.
items.en: The antithesis is not the mechanical opposite: it shows the limits of the first position.
items.note: os limites de = where something stops working

items.hr: A síntese ultrapassa o conflito ao deslocar a questão para outro terreno.
items.en: The synthesis goes beyond the conflict by moving the question onto other ground.
items.note: ao + infinitive expresses the means

items.hr: Cada parte precisa de uma transição: 'Esta leitura, porém, deixa de fora um aspeto essencial'.
items.en: Each part needs a transition: 'This reading, however, leaves out an essential aspect'.
items.note: aspeto without the c in European Portuguese

activities.title: Construir um plano em três partes
activities.intro: Practise the moves and the transitions that hold a three-part plan together.

questions.prompt: In a dialectical plan, what is the job of the antithesis?
options: Repetir a tese por outras palavras
Mostrar os limites da primeira posição
Apresentar a opinião pessoal de quem escreve
questions.answer: Mostrar os limites da primeira posição
questions.explanation: The antithesis is not a flat denial. It accepts the first part and shows the cases in which it no longer holds.

questions.prompt: Which sentence works as a transition into the third part of the plan?
options: Em primeiro lugar, convém definir os termos.
Por outras palavras, o problema é económico.
Talvez o verdadeiro problema não esteja onde o temos colocado.
Como já vimos, os dados são bastante claros.
questions.answer: Talvez o verdadeiro problema não esteja onde o temos colocado.
questions.explanation: It announces a displacement of the question, which is what opens the third part. The others open, reformulate or look back.

questions.prompt: Complete the transition with a formal adversative adverb placed between commas: 'Esta leitura, ___, deixa de fora um aspeto essencial.'
questions.explanation: Placing the adversative inside the sentence rather than at the start raises the register and keeps the sentence flowing.

questions.prompt: Complete with the present tense of the verb meaning to go beyond something: 'A terceira parte ___ a oposição em vez de escolher um lado.'
questions.explanation: 'Ultrapassar' describes moving past a conflict rather than resolving it in favour of one side.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The real problem is not the cost but rather the access.'
questions.explanation: The pair não ... mas sim corrects one term with another and is the standard way to displace a question.

activities.title: Preciso de um plano em três partes
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner only has one opinion and you turn it into a dialectical plan.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: O tema é 'devemos limitar o turismo nas cidades?' e só me ocorre dizer que sim.
lines.en: The subject is 'should we limit tourism in cities?' and all I can think of is saying yes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ótimo, essa é a tua primeira parte. Defende o sim com três razões sólidas.
lines.en: Great, that is your first part. Defend the yes with three solid reasons.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a segunda parte, escrevo o quê?
lines.en: And in the second part, what do I write?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na segunda mostras os limites: quem vive do turismo e as regiões que dependem dele.
lines.en: In the second you show the limits: the people who live off tourism and the regions that depend on it.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Assim fico a contradizer-me a mim próprio.
lines.en: That way I end up contradicting myself.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não, estás a mostrar que pensaste no assunto. A terceira parte desloca a pergunta.
lines.en: No, you are showing that you have thought about the subject. The third part displaces the question.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Desloca como, exatamente?
lines.en: Displaces it how, exactly?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Talvez o problema não seja o número de visitantes mas sim a forma como a cidade os distribui.
lines.en: Perhaps the problem is not the number of visitors but rather the way the city spreads them out.

days.title: Escrever sob pressão de tempo: método em cinco passos
days.objective: You can allocate a fixed writing time across five stages in Portuguese, from reading the task to a final check of agreements and accents.
days.paretoFocus: Most marks lost in written production come from bad time management rather than from bad Portuguese.
drills: Set a timer for sixty minutes and write a full text, respecting five, ten, forty and five minute blocks.
Write a keyword outline for any topic in under ten minutes, with no full sentences.
Reread an old text of yours looking only at agreements, accents and verb tenses.

items: Recall how many minutes go to reading the task and how many to the final check.
Say what to do when a word will not come to mind while drafting.
Explain why an unfinished text costs more than a short one.

activities.title: Cinco passos contra o relógio
activities.intro: Under time pressure the order of your work matters more than your vocabulary. Five stages, each with a fixed budget, keep you from spending twenty minutes on a first paragraph. These sentences describe the method in Portuguese so you can rehearse it in the language.

items.hr: Reserva cinco minutos para ler o enunciado e sublinhar as palavras que impõem o formato.
items.en: Set aside five minutes to read the task and underline the words that impose the format.
items.note: o enunciado = the task wording

items.hr: Nos dez minutos seguintes, faz um plano em palavras-chave, sem frases completas.
items.en: In the following ten minutes, make a keyword outline, with no complete sentences.
items.note: palavras-chave keeps the hyphen

items.hr: Escreve de seguida sem parar à procura da palavra perfeita: deixa um espaço e avança.
items.en: Then write without stopping in search of the perfect word: leave a space and move on.
items.note: à procura de = looking for

items.hr: Guarda sempre cinco minutos finais para reler concordâncias, acentos e tempos verbais.
items.en: Always keep a final five minutes to reread agreements, accents and verb tenses.
items.note: concordância = agreement between words

items.hr: Se o tempo estiver a acabar, fecha com duas linhas: um texto interrompido custa mais do que um texto curto.
items.en: If time is running out, close with two lines: an interrupted text costs more than a short one.
items.note: se + future subjunctive estiver

activities.title: Gerir o relógio
activities.intro: Practise deciding what to do with each block of writing time.

questions.prompt: You have sixty minutes to write. How should the last five be used?
options: A reler as concordâncias e os acentos
A copiar o texto todo a limpo
A contar as palavras uma a uma
questions.answer: A reler as concordâncias e os acentos
questions.explanation: These are the cheapest marks to recover, because the errors are already on the page and take seconds to fix.

questions.prompt: While drafting you cannot remember a word. What is the best move?
options: Parar e pensar até te lembrares dela
Mudar de assunto para evitar a palavra
Deixar um espaço e continuar a escrever
Apagar a frase toda e recomeçar
questions.answer: Deixar um espaço e continuar a escrever
questions.explanation: The word often comes back on its own once the paragraph is finished, and the gap costs nothing if you never return to it.

questions.prompt: Complete with the infinitive of the verb meaning to read something a second time: 'Reserva os últimos cinco minutos para ___ o texto com atenção.'
questions.explanation: The prefix re- attaches to ler and keeps its irregular forms, as in eu releio, tu relês.

questions.prompt: Complete the outline instruction: 'Faz um plano em ___-chave antes de começares a redigir.'
questions.explanation: A keyword outline is faster to produce than full sentences and it survives the pressure of the clock.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I always keep five minutes to reread.'
questions.explanation: The adverb sempre goes right after the conjugated verb in European Portuguese.

activities.title: O relógio não perdoa
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner ran out of time in a practice test and you rebuild his method.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Na semana passada não acabei o texto. Faltaram-me uns dez minutos.
lines.en: Last week I did not finish the text. I was about ten minutes short.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fizeste um plano ou foste logo para a folha?
lines.en: Did you make an outline or did you go straight to the page?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Fui logo. Achei que assim poupava tempo.
lines.en: I went straight in. I thought I was saving time that way.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É ao contrário. Dez minutos de plano poupam-te vinte de hesitação.
lines.en: It is the other way round. Ten minutes of outline save you twenty of hesitation.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quando não me lembro de uma palavra a meio da frase?
lines.en: And when I cannot remember a word in the middle of a sentence?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Deixas um espaço e segues em frente. Voltas lá no fim, se houver tempo.
lines.en: You leave a space and carry on. You go back at the end, if there is time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se o tempo acabar mesmo antes da conclusão?
lines.en: And if the time really runs out before the conclusion?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fechas com duas linhas. Um texto sem fim custa-te muito mais do que um texto curto.
lines.en: You close with two lines. A text with no ending costs you far more than a short one.

days.title: A carta formal de reclamação
days.objective: You can write a formal Portuguese letter of complaint that states the facts, the damage suffered and the request, in the expected register.
days.paretoFocus: The complaint letter is the most predictable written task and it is won or lost on register and structure, both of which can be learned once.
drills: Write the first paragraph of a complaint with two dates and one reference number, using no adjectives.
Turn an angry sentence you might say aloud into a cold formal sentence.
Write the request paragraph and the closing formula from memory.

items: Recall the opening and closing formulas of a formal Portuguese letter.
Say in which paragraph the request belongs and why.
Explain why adjectives weaken a complaint.

activities.title: Reclamar por escrito
activities.intro: A formal complaint works by being colder than the situation. Facts with dates come first, the damage second, the request third, and the tone never rises. These are the standard European Portuguese formulas for each part.

items.hr: Uma carta de reclamação começa por 'Exmos. Senhores,' e nunca por uma saudação informal.
items.en: A letter of complaint begins with 'Exmos. Senhores,' and never with an informal greeting.
items.note: Exmos. = Excelentíssimos

items.hr: No primeiro parágrafo, expõe os factos com datas e números de referência, sem adjetivos.
items.en: In the first paragraph, set out the facts with dates and reference numbers, with no adjectives.
items.note: expor os factos = to lay out the facts

items.hr: No segundo, explica o prejuízo: 'Em consequência, fiquei sem serviço durante nove dias'.
items.en: In the second, explain the harm: 'As a result, I was left without service for nine days'.
items.note: o prejuízo = the loss or damage suffered

items.hr: No terceiro, formula o pedido: 'Solicito o reembolso integral do valor pago'.
items.en: In the third, make the request: 'I request the full refund of the amount paid'.
items.note: solicitar is more formal than pedir

items.hr: Fecha com 'Com os melhores cumprimentos,' e o teu nome completo por baixo.
items.en: Close with 'Com os melhores cumprimentos,' and your full name underneath.
items.note: the standard neutral sign off

activities.title: O tom certo para reclamar
activities.intro: Practise the register and the ordering that a formal complaint requires.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has the right register for a formal complaint?
options: Isto é uma vergonha e vocês sabem disso.
Venho por este meio manifestar o meu desagrado relativamente ao serviço prestado.
Estou farto de esperar, resolvam isto já.
Alguém me explica esta palhaçada toda?
questions.answer: Venho por este meio manifestar o meu desagrado relativamente ao serviço prestado.
questions.explanation: It expresses dissatisfaction through a fixed formal formula. The other three carry emotion, which gives the company an excuse to answer the tone instead of the facts.

questions.prompt: Where does the request belong in a letter of complaint?
options: Logo na primeira linha, antes dos factos
Depois da exposição dos factos e do prejuízo
questions.answer: Depois da exposição dos factos e do prejuízo
questions.explanation: The request only sounds legitimate once the reader has seen the facts and the harm that justify it.

questions.prompt: Complete with the first person present of the formal verb meaning to request: '___ o reembolso integral do valor pago.'
questions.explanation: 'Solicitar' belongs to the administrative register, while 'pedir' would sound conversational in this letter.

questions.prompt: Complete the standard closing formula of a formal letter: 'Com os melhores ___,'
questions.explanation: This is the neutral sign off used with an organisation you do not know personally.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I was left without service for nine days.'
questions.explanation: 'Ficar sem' expresses being deprived of something, and durante marks the length of the period.

activities.title: A carta à operadora
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner has drafted an angry complaint and you correct its register and structure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Escrevi 'Olá, estou muito chateado com a vossa empresa'. Serve assim?
lines.en: I wrote 'Hi, I am very annoyed with your company'. Does that work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não serve. Numa reclamação formal o tom mantém-se frio, mesmo quando tens toda a razão.
lines.en: It does not work. In a formal complaint the tone stays cold, even when you are entirely right.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então como é que começo?
lines.en: So how do I begin?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: 'Exmos. Senhores,' e depois os factos com as datas e o número do contrato.
lines.en: 'Exmos. Senhores,' and then the facts with the dates and the contract number.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Digo que fiquei sem internet nove dias?
lines.en: Do I say that I was left without internet for nine days?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dizes, mas como prejuízo e não como desabafo. A seguir pedes o reembolso.
lines.en: You do, but as harm suffered and not as a rant. Then you ask for the refund.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E no fim, assino como?
lines.en: And at the end, how do I sign off?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: 'Com os melhores cumprimentos,' e o teu nome completo por baixo.
lines.en: 'Com os melhores cumprimentos,' and your full name underneath.

days.title: O artigo de opinião
days.objective: You can write a Portuguese opinion piece with a stated authorial voice, a title that takes a position, and a closing appeal rather than a summary.
days.paretoFocus: The opinion piece is where personality earns marks, and knowing exactly how much 'eu' is allowed keeps you from writing either a report or a rant.
drills: Write three possible titles for an opinion piece, each under eight words and each taking a side.
Write a paragraph that opens with a personal experience and closes with a figure.
Write a final appeal of two sentences that adds nothing new but demands something.

items: Say how many rhetorical questions one opinion piece can carry.
Recall what separates an opinion piece from a report.
Explain why a summary makes a weak ending.

activities.title: A voz do artigo de opinião
activities.intro: An opinion piece has an author who shows himself, but the personal voice has to carry an argument. Rhythm matters as much as content: long reasoning sentences alternate with short ones that land. These sentences give you the tools in Portuguese.

items.hr: O artigo de opinião assume um autor: podes escrever 'eu', desde que argumentes.
items.en: The opinion piece has an author who shows himself: you may write 'eu', as long as you argue.
items.note: desde que + subjunctive

items.hr: O título deve ser curto e tomar posição: 'O comboio das sete já não chega a horas'.
items.en: The title must be short and take a side: 'The seven o'clock train no longer arrives on time'.
items.note: o comboio, the European Portuguese word for train

items.hr: Uma pergunta retórica a meio do texto acorda o leitor, mas uma por artigo chega.
items.en: A rhetorical question halfway through wakes the reader up, but one per piece is enough.
items.note: a meio de = halfway through

items.hr: Alterna frases longas de raciocínio com frases curtas de remate.
items.en: Alternate long reasoning sentences with short closing ones.
items.note: o remate = the finishing blow

items.hr: Fecha com uma imagem ou com um apelo, nunca com um resumo do que já disseste.
items.en: Close with an image or with an appeal, never with a summary of what you have already said.
items.note: o apelo = the call to act

activities.title: Escrever com voz própria
activities.intro: Practise the voice, the title and the ending that an opinion piece needs.

questions.prompt: Which title fits an opinion piece best?
options: O autocarro das sete já não é um serviço público
Uma análise neutra da mobilidade urbana
questions.answer: O autocarro das sete já não é um serviço público
questions.explanation: It is short, concrete and already states a judgement. The others announce a neutral document, which sets the wrong expectation.

questions.prompt: What distinguishes an opinion piece from a report?
options: Assume abertamente a posição de quem escreve
É mais longo do que um relatório
questions.answer: Assume abertamente a posição de quem escreve
questions.explanation: Both may use figures and paragraphs. Only the opinion piece openly commits its author to a position.

questions.prompt: Complete with the European Portuguese noun for a train: 'Todos os dias espero vinte minutos pelo ___ das sete.'
questions.explanation: In Portugal the word is comboio, and esperar takes por when you wait for a specific service.

questions.prompt: Complete the rhetorical question with the two words meaning 'until when': '___ vamos aceitar esta situação?'
questions.explanation: This question puts pressure on the reader without accusing anyone directly, which is why it suits an opinion piece.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'We cannot wait any longer.'
questions.explanation: Já não expresses that a situation has stopped being possible, and mais closes the sentence with the sense of any longer.

activities.title: Um artigo sobre os transportes
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner must write an opinion piece and is unsure how personal it may be.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pediram-me um artigo de opinião sobre os transportes da minha cidade. Posso usar 'eu'?
lines.en: They have asked me for an opinion piece about the transport in my city. May I use 'I'?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Podes e deves. O que não podes é ficar-te pelo desabafo.
lines.en: You may and you should. What you cannot do is stop at a rant.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então uso a minha experiência e depois os dados?
lines.en: So I use my experience and then the data?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Exatamente. A experiência abre o texto e os números sustentam-na.
lines.en: Exactly. The experience opens the text and the figures back it up.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Que título é que me sugeres?
lines.en: What title do you suggest to me?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Curto e com posição. Algo como 'O autocarro das sete já não é um serviço público'.
lines.en: Short and committed. Something like 'The seven o'clock bus is no longer a public service'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E o último parágrafo?
lines.en: And the last paragraph?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Termina com um apelo. Um resumo no fim só faz o leitor sentir que já leu aquilo.
lines.en: Finish with an appeal. A summary at the end only makes the reader feel he has read that already.

days.title: O relatório objetivo
days.objective: You can write an objective Portuguese report using impersonal constructions, measured facts instead of evaluative adjectives, and impersonal recommendations.
days.paretoFocus: The neutral register of the report is the one most learners never practise, and it is exactly what a professional writing task tests.
drills: Rewrite three evaluative sentences of yours by replacing each adjective with a measurement.
Write four section headings for a report on a service you use.
Write two recommendations using impersonal forms and no imperative.

items: Name the four standard sections of a report, in order.
Recall one impersonal construction used to state findings.
Say why recommendations avoid naming a person.

activities.title: Relatar sem opinar
activities.intro: A report earns trust by removing the writer from the page. Facts are measured rather than judged, findings are stated impersonally, and recommendations are phrased so that nobody feels accused. These sentences give you the grammar that does that work.

items.hr: Num relatório, os factos vêm primeiro e as conclusões apenas no fim.
items.en: In a report, the facts come first and the conclusions only at the end.
items.note: vêm, third person plural of vir, with the circumflex

items.hr: Usa construções impessoais: 'Foram registadas quinze ocorrências durante o mês'.
items.en: Use impersonal constructions: 'Fifteen incidents were recorded during the month'.
items.note: passive with ser + past participle agreeing

items.hr: Substitui os adjetivos avaliativos por medidas: em vez de 'muito lento', escreve o tempo exato.
items.en: Replace evaluative adjectives with measurements: instead of 'very slow', write the exact time.
items.note: em vez de + noun or infinitive

items.hr: Estrutura o texto em secções: objetivo, método, resultados e recomendações.
items.en: Structure the text in sections: aim, method, results and recommendations.
items.note: objetivo without the c in European Portuguese

items.hr: As recomendações usam formas impessoais: 'A equipa deverá rever o procedimento'.
items.en: The recommendations use impersonal forms: 'The team should review the procedure'.
items.note: deverá softens an instruction into a recommendation

activities.title: O registo do relatório
activities.intro: Practise turning judgements into measured statements in the neutral register.

questions.prompt: Which sentence belongs in an objective report?
options: O atendimento foi péssimo do princípio ao fim.
O tempo médio de espera foi de 42 minutos.
Achamos que isto tem mesmo de mudar.
questions.answer: O tempo médio de espera foi de 42 minutos.
questions.explanation: It states something a reader can verify. The other three judge, which belongs to an opinion piece and not to a report.

questions.prompt: How should recommendations be worded in a report?
options: Com imperativos dirigidos a uma pessoa concreta
Com formas impessoais como 'deverá ser revisto'
Com frases exclamativas para dar ênfase
questions.answer: Com formas impessoais como 'deverá ser revisto'
questions.explanation: An impersonal form places the recommendation on the procedure rather than on a colleague, so the reader does not feel accused.

questions.prompt: Complete the impersonal passive with the third person plural past of the verb 'ser': '___ registadas quinze ocorrências durante o mês de maio.'
questions.explanation: The passive agrees with ocorrências, which is feminine plural, hence foram registadas.

questions.prompt: Replace the judgement with a measurement by writing the number forty in words: 'O processo demorou ___ e dois minutos.'
questions.explanation: A figure cannot be argued with, whereas an adjective such as lento invites the reader to disagree.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'The team should review the procedure.'
questions.explanation: The future of dever expresses a recommendation rather than an order, which is the register a report needs.

activities.title: O relatório do estágio
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner has written a report full of judgements and you convert it into neutral register.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Escrevi que o sistema informático é uma desgraça. E é mesmo.
lines.en: I wrote that the computer system is a disaster. And it really is.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pode ser verdade, mas num relatório isso não se escreve assim.
lines.en: That may be true, but in a report you do not write it like that.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então escrevo o quê no lugar disso?
lines.en: So what do I write in its place?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mede. 'O sistema esteve indisponível durante onze horas em junho.'
lines.en: Measure it. 'The system was unavailable for eleven hours in June.'

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E a minha opinião fica toda de fora?
lines.en: And does my opinion stay out completely?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Fica para as recomendações, e mesmo aí em forma impessoal.
lines.en: It goes into the recommendations, and even there in an impersonal form.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Dá-me um exemplo, se faz favor.
lines.en: Give me an example, please.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: 'A equipa deverá rever o procedimento de manutenção.' Ninguém se sente atacado e a mensagem passa.
lines.en: 'The team should review the maintenance procedure.' Nobody feels attacked and the message gets through.

days.title: O debate contraditório: tomar e manter a palavra
days.objective: You can enter a Portuguese debate politely, keep the floor when interrupted, buy thinking time and hand a question back.
days.paretoFocus: In spoken interaction the marks go to whoever keeps the exchange alive, and that depends on a handful of memorised turn-taking formulas.
drills: Say three ways of entering a conversation without cutting the other person off.
Rehearse aloud a sentence that keeps the floor when someone interrupts you.
Practise handing a question back with a conditional verb form.

items: Recall the formula that buys you a few seconds of thinking time.
Say what to do when you have no data to answer a question.
Explain why you should signal clearly when your turn has ended.

activities.title: Tomar e manter a palavra
activities.intro: Debating in a foreign language is mostly a matter of managing turns. A few fixed formulas let you break in, hold your ground, stall for a second and pass the pressure back. Learn them until they come out without thinking.

items.hr: Para entrar na conversa sem seres rude, começa por 'Se me permites, gostava de acrescentar uma coisa'.
items.en: To enter the conversation without being rude, start with 'If I may, I would like to add something'.
items.note: gostava de + infinitive, the usual polite form in Portugal

items.hr: Se te interrompem, mantém o tom calmo: 'Deixa-me só terminar a ideia, é rápido'.
items.en: If they interrupt you, keep the tone calm: 'Just let me finish the idea, it will be quick'.
items.note: deixa-me, enclitic pronoun with the imperative

items.hr: Ganha tempo para pensar com 'É uma boa pergunta, e a resposta não é simples'.
items.en: Buy thinking time with 'That is a good question, and the answer is not simple'.
items.note: a filler that still says something

items.hr: Devolve a pergunta quando te faltam dados: 'E tu, como explicarias esse aumento?'
items.en: Hand the question back when you lack data: 'And you, how would you explain that rise?'
items.note: conditional explicarias for a hypothetical answer

items.hr: Fecha a tua intervenção com uma frase de remate para o outro perceber que acabaste.
items.en: Close your turn with a finishing sentence so that the other person realises you have ended.
items.note: para + subject + subjunctive perceber

activities.title: Na mesa do debate
activities.intro: Practise the formulas that let you take, hold and hand over the floor.

questions.prompt: Somebody cuts you off mid argument. Which reply keeps the floor politely?
options: Deixa-me só terminar a ideia, é rápido.
Não interessa nada o que estás a dizer.
Isso já eu tinha dito há bocado.
questions.answer: Deixa-me só terminar a ideia, é rápido.
questions.explanation: It asks for a small, measurable amount of time, which is much harder to refuse than a demand for silence.

questions.prompt: Which formula buys thinking time without sounding empty?
options: É uma boa pergunta, e a resposta não é simples.
questions.answer: É uma boa pergunta, e a resposta não é simples.
questions.explanation: It gives you three seconds and already announces that a nuanced answer is coming, so the pause reads as thought and not as hesitation.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite request to speak, using the tu form of the verb meaning to allow: 'Se me ___, gostava de acrescentar uma coisa.'
questions.explanation: The present indicative is normal here between peers; the future subjunctive permitires is also heard and slightly more formal.

questions.prompt: Hand the question back with the tu conditional of the verb meaning to explain: 'E tu, como ___ esse aumento?'
questions.explanation: The conditional asks for a hypothetical account and shifts the burden of proof onto the other speaker.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Let me finish the idea.'
questions.explanation: With an affirmative imperative the pronoun attaches after the verb with a hyphen: deixa-me.

activities.title: O debate na aula
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. You are challenged in a debate and must keep the floor while answering.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Os números que apresentaste estão errados, isso vê-se logo.
lines.en: The figures you presented are wrong, that is obvious straight away.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se me permites, deixa-me só terminar a ideia e depois respondo a isso.
lines.en: If I may, just let me finish the idea and then I will answer that.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas o dado principal está claramente desatualizado.
lines.en: But the main figure is clearly out of date.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É uma boa pergunta, e a resposta não é simples. O valor é de 2023 e não de 2010.
lines.en: That is a good question, and the answer is not simple. The figure is from 2023 and not from 2010.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Continuo a achar que estás a exagerar.
lines.en: I still think that you are exaggerating.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: E tu, como explicarias então a subida dos últimos dois anos?
lines.en: And you, how would you explain the rise of the last two years, then?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Não tenho os números à mão neste momento.
lines.en: I do not have the figures to hand at the moment.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Então fiquemos pelos que temos. Era isto que eu queria dizer.
lines.en: Then let us stick with the ones we have. This is what I wanted to say.

days.title: A exposição estruturada a partir de um documento
days.objective: You can present a document in Portuguese for a few minutes: identify it, announce two points, link back to the text and finish with a personal example.
days.paretoFocus: In spoken production the examiner is listening for structure, so announcing your two points in the first thirty seconds already secures a large share of the marks.
drills: Identify any Portuguese text in three sentences: what it is, its subject, its main idea.
Announce two points aloud using a fixed opening formula.
Prepare one personal example that fits a current social topic and time yourself at forty seconds.

items: Recall the three sentences that identify a document.
Say why summarising the whole text is the classic mistake.
Recall two Portuguese phrases that link a point back to the source.

activities.title: Apresentar um documento em cinco minutos
activities.intro: A structured presentation is not a summary. You identify the document, pick out the question it raises, treat that question in two announced points, and keep referring back to the text. A personal example at the end shows vocabulary that is genuinely yours.

items.hr: Começa por identificar o documento: a natureza, o tema e a ideia principal, em três frases.
items.en: Begin by identifying the document: its nature, its subject and its main idea, in three sentences.
items.note: a natureza do documento = what kind of text it is

items.hr: Anuncia a estrutura: 'Vou abordar dois pontos: primeiro o custo, depois o acesso'.
items.en: Announce the structure: 'I am going to deal with two points: first the cost, then the access'.
items.note: vou + infinitive for an immediate plan

items.hr: Não resumas o documento todo: escolhe a questão que ele levanta e trata-a.
items.en: Do not summarise the whole document: choose the question it raises and deal with it.
items.note: trata-a, enclitic pronoun with the imperative

items.hr: Liga cada ponto à fonte com 'como o texto sublinha' ou 'ao contrário do que ali se afirma'.
items.en: Link each point to the source with 'as the text stresses' or 'unlike what is claimed there'.
items.note: se afirma, impersonal se

items.hr: Guarda um exemplo pessoal para o fim: dá calor à exposição e mostra vocabulário teu.
items.en: Keep a personal example for the end: it warms up the presentation and shows vocabulary of your own.
items.note: dar calor a = to bring warmth to

activities.title: Estruturar a exposição oral
activities.intro: Practise opening, structuring and closing a short oral presentation of a text.

questions.prompt: How should a short oral presentation of a document open?
options: Por uma opinião pessoal muito forte
Pela identificação do documento e da sua ideia principal
Pela leitura em voz alta do primeiro parágrafo
questions.answer: Pela identificação do documento e da sua ideia principal
questions.explanation: The listener needs to know what is being talked about before any judgement makes sense.

questions.prompt: What is the classic mistake when presenting a document orally?
options: Anunciar a estrutura logo no início
Resumir o documento do princípio ao fim
Dar um exemplo pessoal no final
Citar uma frase curta do texto
questions.answer: Resumir o documento do princípio ao fim
questions.explanation: A running summary shows only that you understood the words. The task asks you to treat the question the text raises.

questions.prompt: Complete with the infinitive of the verb meaning to tackle a subject: 'Vou ___ dois pontos: primeiro o custo, depois o acesso.'
questions.explanation: 'Abordar' is the standard verb for taking up a point in a presentation and it sounds more prepared than falar de.

questions.prompt: Complete the link to the source with the present of the verb meaning to stress: 'Como o texto ___, o problema não é recente.'
questions.explanation: Referring back to the document with a verb of emphasis proves that your point comes from the text and not from nowhere.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'This document raises a difficult question.'
questions.explanation: 'Levantar uma questão' is the fixed expression for a text that opens up a problem.

activities.title: Cinco minutos para apresentar
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner has a text and a few minutes to speak about it, and needs a structure.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Deram-me um texto sobre o trabalho ao domingo e cinco minutos para falar.
lines.en: They gave me a text about working on Sundays and five minutes to speak.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não resumas tudo. Diz o que é o texto e qual é a questão que ele levanta.
lines.en: Do not summarise everything. Say what the text is and what question it raises.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E depois disso falo de quê?
lines.en: And after that what do I talk about?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Escolhe dois pontos e anuncia-os logo nos primeiros trinta segundos.
lines.en: Choose two points and announce them within the first thirty seconds.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Posso dar um exemplo da minha própria vida?
lines.en: May I give an example from my own life?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guarda-o para o fim. Fechas com o teu caso e a exposição fica viva.
lines.en: Keep it for the end. You close with your own case and the presentation comes alive.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se me esquecer do que ia dizer a meio?
lines.en: And if I forget what I was going to say halfway through?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Volta ao documento. Uma citação curta dá-te tempo e mostra que o leste bem.
lines.en: Go back to the document. A short quotation gives you time and shows that you read it properly.

days.title: A tomada de notas eficaz durante a audição
days.objective: You can take usable notes while listening in Portuguese, writing only content words and abbreviations and recovering after a gap.
days.paretoFocus: Listening marks are lost while writing, not while hearing, so a compact note system buys back the sentence you would otherwise miss.
drills: Write down five abbreviations you will always use and stick to them for a week.
Listen to two minutes of Portuguese speech and note only nouns, verbs and numbers.
Reconstruct full sentences from those notes ten minutes later, from memory.

items: Say what to do the moment you realise you have missed a sentence.
Recall which items are worth writing down exactly.
Explain what to check on a second listening.

activities.title: Tirar notas enquanto ouves
activities.intro: Notes taken during listening are a memory aid, not a transcript. Writing full sentences guarantees that you lose the next one. Content words, fixed abbreviations and a marked gap when you fall behind will get you through.

items.hr: Antes de o áudio começar, lê as perguntas e escreve as palavras que esperas ouvir.
items.en: Before the audio starts, read the questions and write down the words you expect to hear.
items.note: antes de + personal infinitive

items.hr: Escreve apenas substantivos, verbos e números: os artigos fazem-te perder o som seguinte.
items.en: Write only nouns, verbs and numbers: articles make you lose the next sound.
items.note: fazer alguém perder algo

items.hr: Usa abreviaturas fixas: 'pq' para porque, 'tb' para também, uma seta para consequência.
items.en: Use fixed abbreviations: 'pq' for because, 'tb' for also, an arrow for consequence.
items.note: always the same set, decided in advance

items.hr: Se perderes uma parte, marca um ponto de interrogação e continua a ouvir.
items.en: If you lose part of it, mark a question mark and carry on listening.
items.note: se + future subjunctive perderes

items.hr: Na segunda audição, confirma apenas os números, as datas e os nomes próprios.
items.en: On the second listening, check only the numbers, the dates and the proper names.
items.note: the general meaning is already in your head

activities.title: Notas em tempo real
activities.intro: Practise deciding in a split second what goes onto the page and what does not.

questions.prompt: You miss a whole sentence while writing. What do you do?
options: Parar e reconstruir a frase de memória
Marcar um ponto de interrogação e continuar a ouvir
Voltar atrás nas notas e relê-las
Passar a escrever tudo na tua língua materna
questions.answer: Marcar um ponto de interrogação e continuar a ouvir
questions.explanation: Stopping to repair one gap creates a second and a third. The mark tells you later exactly where the hole is.

questions.prompt: Which items are worth writing down exactly as heard?
options: Os números, as datas e os nomes próprios
questions.answer: Os números, as datas e os nomes próprios
questions.explanation: These cannot be reconstructed from context, whereas grammar words can always be put back afterwards.

questions.prompt: Complete with the personal infinitive of the verb meaning to begin: 'Antes de o áudio ___, lê as perguntas com atenção.'
questions.explanation: After antes de with an expressed subject, European Portuguese uses the personal infinitive: antes de o áudio começar.

questions.prompt: Complete the abbreviation rule with the two letters that stand for 'também': 'Escrevo ___ em vez da palavra inteira.'
questions.explanation: A fixed personal set of abbreviations only saves time if you never hesitate over what each one means.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I write down only nouns and numbers.'
questions.explanation: Apenas goes immediately before the element it restricts, here the two nouns.

activities.title: Não consigo escrever e ouvir ao mesmo tempo
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner keeps losing the audio while taking notes and you fix his system.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Enquanto escrevo, perco sempre a frase seguinte. É todas as vezes.
lines.en: While I am writing, I always lose the next sentence. It happens every time.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Estás a escrever frases inteiras? É aí que está o erro.
lines.en: Are you writing whole sentences? That is where the mistake is.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Escrevo aquilo que vou ouvindo.
lines.en: I write down whatever I am hearing.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Escreve só o essencial: nomes, números e verbos. O resto reconstróis depois.
lines.en: Write only the essentials: names, numbers and verbs. The rest you rebuild afterwards.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E quando perco mesmo uma parte inteira?
lines.en: And when I really lose a whole part?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Marcas um ponto de interrogação e continuas. Quem para perde tudo o que vem a seguir.
lines.en: You mark a question mark and carry on. Whoever stops loses everything that comes next.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Na segunda vez confirmo o quê?
lines.en: On the second time round, what do I check?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Só os números, as datas e os nomes próprios. O sentido geral já o tens na cabeça.
lines.en: Only the numbers, the dates and the proper names. You already have the general meaning in your head.

days.title: O texto longo e denso: ler depressa e bem
days.objective: You can survey a long Portuguese text in under a minute, find the idea of each paragraph and locate answers without reading every word.
days.paretoFocus: Reading tasks are timed, and a candidate who reads linearly from the first word rarely reaches the last question.
drills: Take a long Portuguese article and read only the title, the first paragraph and the last sentence.
Write the idea of each paragraph in three words, using only its first sentence.
Mark every connector in one page and follow the reasoning through them alone.

items: Say which three parts of a text you read in the first thirty seconds.
Recall where a paragraph usually hides its main idea.
Explain what to do with an unknown word in the middle of a page.

activities.title: Ler depressa sem perder o fio
activities.intro: Fast reading is a sequence of passes, not one slow crawl. The first pass tells you what the text is about and how it is organised, and only then do the questions send you back to precise places. Connectors carry the argument better than vocabulary does.

items.hr: Lê primeiro o título, o primeiro parágrafo e a última frase: em trinta segundos sabes de que trata.
items.en: First read the title, the first paragraph and the last sentence: in thirty seconds you know what it is about.
items.note: tratar de = to be about

items.hr: A primeira frase de cada parágrafo costuma conter a ideia desse parágrafo.
items.en: The first sentence of each paragraph usually contains the idea of that paragraph.
items.note: costumar + infinitive for what is usually the case

items.hr: Não pares numa palavra desconhecida: o sentido vem quase sempre da frase seguinte.
items.en: Do not stop at an unknown word: the meaning almost always comes from the next sentence.
items.note: negative imperative pares, from parar

items.hr: Assinala os conectores: mostram o movimento do raciocínio melhor do que o vocabulário.
items.en: Mark the connectors: they show the movement of the reasoning better than the vocabulary does.
items.note: assinalar = to mark, to flag

items.hr: Depois desta leitura rápida, vai às perguntas e regressa ao texto só para localizar respostas.
items.en: After this quick reading, go to the questions and return to the text only to locate answers.
items.note: regressar a = to go back to

activities.title: Estratégias de leitura rápida
activities.intro: Practise the decisions that let you cover a long text in the time available.

questions.prompt: Where is the main idea of a paragraph usually found?
questions.explanation: Argumentative prose announces the point and then supports it, so the opening sentence is the fastest route to the content.

questions.prompt: You meet an unknown word halfway through a dense page. What is the best reaction?
options: Parar e tentar adivinhar durante um minuto
Continuar a ler e deduzir pelo contexto
Sublinhar todas as palavras parecidas na página
questions.answer: Continuar a ler e deduzir pelo contexto
questions.explanation: If the word matters it will come back, and the surrounding sentences will define it for you at no cost in time.

questions.prompt: Complete with the plural noun that covers words such as porém, portanto and aliás: 'Os ___ mostram o movimento do raciocínio.'
questions.explanation: These words carry the logic of the argument, so following them alone already gives you the shape of the text.

questions.prompt: Complete with the first person present of the verb meaning to know a fact: 'Em trinta segundos já ___ de que trata o texto.'
questions.explanation: Saber is used for facts and information, while conhecer would be used for being acquainted with a person or a place.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I read the title and the last sentence first.'
questions.explanation: Here primeiro is an adverb of order placed right after the verb, not an adjective agreeing with a noun.

activities.title: Quatro páginas em dez minutos
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner panics in front of a long dense text and you give him a reading order.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: São quatro páginas e eu leio devagar. Nunca vou conseguir acabar.
lines.en: It is four pages and I read slowly. I am never going to manage to finish.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não leias tudo à primeira. Título, primeiro parágrafo e última frase.
lines.en: Do not read everything the first time. Title, first paragraph and last sentence.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Assim não percebo os pormenores.
lines.en: That way I do not understand the details.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ainda não precisas deles. Precisas de saber onde é que eles estão.
lines.en: You do not need them yet. You need to know where they are.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E as palavras que eu não conheço?
lines.en: And the words I do not know?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Passa por cima delas. Se forem importantes, aparecem outra vez e o contexto explica-as.
lines.en: Skip over them. If they are important, they will come up again and the context will explain them.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Só depois é que volto ao texto?
lines.en: Only afterwards do I go back to the text?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Voltas, mas já com as perguntas na cabeça. Assim procuras em vez de leres.
lines.en: You go back, but with the questions already in your head. That way you search instead of reading.

days.title: Últimos reflexos antes do exame
days.objective: You can apply a short set of last minute habits in Portuguese: consolidate rather than learn, respect your timing plan, paraphrase missing words and check accents.
days.paretoFocus: The final days change nothing about your level but a great deal about your score, because most remaining losses are avoidable.
drills: Write your time plan for each part of the exam on a single sheet.
Practise paraphrasing five objects without naming them, in Portuguese.
Reread one of your old texts checking only accents and agreements, nothing else.

items: Say what should not be studied the evening before.
Recall the paraphrase formula for a word you cannot retrieve.
Explain why a simple correct sentence beats an ambitious broken one.

activities.title: Os últimos reflexos
activities.intro: The last stretch is about protecting what you already have. New grammar the night before mostly creates doubt, while a rehearsed timing plan and a paraphrase reflex protect real marks. Keep the final habits few enough to remember under stress.

items.hr: Na véspera, não estudes gramática nova: revê o que já sabes e dorme cedo.
items.en: The day before, do not study new grammar: go over what you already know and sleep early.
items.note: a véspera = the day before

items.hr: Leva um plano de tempos para cada prova e cumpre-o, mesmo que uma resposta fique por acabar.
items.en: Take a timing plan for each part and stick to it, even if one answer is left unfinished.
items.note: ficar por + infinitive = to be left undone

items.hr: Se te faltar uma palavra, contorna-a: 'aquilo que serve para' resolve quase tudo.
items.en: If a word escapes you, go around it: 'the thing that is used for' solves almost everything.
items.note: contornar = to go around an obstacle

items.hr: Corrige sempre as concordâncias e os acentos: são os erros mais fáceis de evitar.
items.en: Always correct agreements and accents: they are the easiest errors to avoid.
items.note: fácil de + infinitive

items.hr: No oral, vale mais uma frase simples e correta do que uma frase complicada que se desmorona.
items.en: In the speaking part, a simple correct sentence is worth more than a complicated one that falls apart.
items.note: valer mais do que = to be worth more than

activities.title: Reflexos de última hora
activities.intro: Practise the small decisions that protect marks in the last days and hours.

questions.prompt: The evening before, what is the most useful thing to do?
options: Aprender uma regra de gramática nova
Rever o que já se sabe e descansar
Decorar uma lista de mil palavras
questions.answer: Rever o que já se sabe e descansar
questions.explanation: New material learned in a hurry competes with what is already solid and adds hesitation exactly where you need automatic answers.

questions.prompt: In the speaking part a word escapes you. What works best?
options: Ficar em silêncio até a palavra aparecer
Dizer a palavra na tua língua materna
Descrever a ideia por outras palavras
questions.answer: Descrever a ideia por outras palavras
questions.explanation: Paraphrasing keeps the conversation running and is itself a skill the examiner is looking for at this level.

questions.prompt: Complete the paraphrase formula with the demonstrative pronoun used for an unnamed object: 'É ___ que serve para abrir garrafas.'
questions.explanation: Aquilo refers to a thing that has not been named and is the backbone of any paraphrase under pressure.

questions.prompt: Complete with the tu imperative of the verb meaning to go over something again: 'Na véspera, ___ as tuas notas e dorme cedo.'
questions.explanation: The imperative of rever carries the circumflex, revê, and the accent is graded strictly in written answers.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'A simple and correct sentence is worth more.'
questions.explanation: Simples has the same form in the singular and the plural, and correta is written without the c in European Portuguese.

activities.title: Véspera de exame
activities.intro: Play 'Me'. Your partner wants to cram the night before and you talk him into consolidating instead.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Estou a pensar rever o conjuntivo todo esta noite.
lines.en: I am thinking of going through the whole subjunctive tonight.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Não faças isso. Na véspera consolida-se, não se aprende.
lines.en: Do not do that. The day before you consolidate, you do not learn.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então faço o quê durante a tarde?
lines.en: So what do I do during the afternoon?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Relês as tuas notas, preparas o material e deitas-te cedo.
lines.en: You reread your notes, get your things ready and go to bed early.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se me faltar uma palavra no oral?
lines.en: And if a word escapes me in the speaking part?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Contorna-a. Dizes 'aquilo que serve para' e explicas a ideia por outras palavras.
lines.en: Go around it. You say 'the thing that is used for' and explain the idea in other words.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E na parte escrita, o que é que não posso esquecer?
lines.en: And in the written part, what must I not forget?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Guarda cinco minutos para os acentos e as concordâncias. É aí que se ganham pontos fáceis.
lines.en: Keep five minutes for the accents and the agreements. That is where the easy marks are won.

days.title: Revisão B2 I
days.objective: Consolidate the B2 grammar core in one sitting: the four subjunctives, the personal infinitive and mesóclise recognition — mixed, the way real Portuguese serves them.
days.paretoFocus: These structures are exactly what separates B1 from B2; switching between conjuntivo tenses and the infinitivo pessoal inside one conversation is the certificate skill.
drills: Run 'fazer' through the whole system out loud: faça, fizesse, fizer, tenha feito, tivesse feito.
Build one 'se' chain of your own: 'Se puderes...', 'Se pudesse...', 'Se tivesse podido...'
Take 'dir-te-ei' and say it in two natural spoken ways.

items: Recall which trigger takes which form: talvez, quando (future), para + subject, para que.
Say the unreal-past pattern out loud: se + tivesse feito, ... what follows in speech?
Translate: 'When you can, call me.'

activities.title: O sistema do conjuntivo, num relance
activities.intro: One flagship sentence per structure — read each one, name the structure, and say it aloud until it feels obvious.

items.hr: Duvido que ele venha à reunião de amanhã.
items.en: I doubt he'll come to tomorrow's meeting.
items.note: presente do conjuntivo after doubt, wish, emotion: duvido que, espero que, é pena que

items.hr: Se pudesse, viajava muito mais.
items.en: If I could, I would travel much more.
items.note: se + imperfeito do conjuntivo = unreal condition; spoken pt-PT replaces the conditional with the imperfect

items.hr: Quando puderes, telefona-me.
items.en: When you can, call me.
items.note: futuro do conjuntivo after quando, se, assim que pointing at the future

items.hr: Espero que tenhas dormido bem.
items.en: I hope you slept well.
items.note: conjuntivo composto: tenha + particípio looks back at a completed action from a present trigger

items.hr: Se tivesses estudado, terias passado no exame.
items.en: If you had studied, you would have passed the exam.
items.note: the unreal past: tivesse + particípio; in speech the second half becomes 'tinhas passado'

items.hr: Comprei fruta para fazermos uma salada ao jantar.
items.en: I bought fruit for us to make a salad at dinner.
items.note: infinitivo pessoal after prepositions: para fazermos, ao chegarem, antes de saíres

items.hr: Dir-te-ei a verdade quando chegar a altura certa.
items.en: I shall tell you the truth when the right time comes.
items.note: mesóclise — pronoun inside the future/conditional; recognise it, but in speech say 'vou dizer-te'

activities.title: Miscelânea gramatical
activities.intro: Mixed B2 grammar, no labels: decide first WHICH structure the sentence needs, then build it.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quando ___ a Lisboa, avisa-me.'
questions.explanation: 'Quando' pointing at the future takes the futuro do conjuntivo: vieram → vier, vieres.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfeito do conjuntivo of 'ter': 'Se ___ mais tempo, aprendia alemão também.'
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: se + imperfeito do conjuntivo (tiveram → tivesse), with the spoken imperfect 'aprendia' in the main clause.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses the personal infinitive correctly?
options: É melhor irmos embora antes de escurecer.
É melhor vamos embora antes que escurecer.
É melhor irem nós embora antes de escureça.
questions.answer: É melhor irmos embora antes de escurecer.
questions.explanation: After impersonal expressions and prepositions the infinitive conjugates: é melhor irmos; 'que' would demand the conjuntivo instead.

questions.prompt: 'Far-lhe-ia esse favor com todo o gosto.' What is the everyday spoken equivalent?
options: Fazia-lhe esse favor com todo o gosto.
Far-ia-lhe esse favor com todo o gosto.
Lhe faria esse favor com todo o gosto.
questions.answer: Fazia-lhe esse favor com todo o gosto.
questions.explanation: Mesóclise is formal/written; conversation restructures with the imperfect and normal ênclise: fazia-lhe.

questions.prompt: Complete with the compound subjunctive of 'ter': 'Duvido que eles ___ chegado a horas com este trânsito.'
questions.explanation: Present trigger (duvido que) looking back at a completed action: tenham + particípio (chegado).

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'If I had known, I would have come earlier.'
questions.explanation: The unreal past, spoken version: se + tivesse sabido (mais-que-perfeito do conjuntivo) + tinha vindo instead of teria vindo.

activities.title: A viagem aos Açores
activities.intro: You and a friend daydream about a trip to the Azores — the whole conjuntivo system slips naturally into the talk. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Se pudesses tirar férias já amanhã, para onde ias?
lines.en: If you could take holidays starting tomorrow, where would you go?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Para os Açores, sem pensar duas vezes. Se tivesse ido no ano passado, tinha apanhado preços muito melhores.
lines.en: To the Azores, no second thoughts. If I had gone last year, I would have caught much better prices.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Pois, agora está tudo caríssimo. Quando decidires, diz-me, que eu se calhar vou contigo.
lines.en: Yeah, everything is super expensive now. When you decide, tell me, because I might just go with you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Combinado. É melhor reservarmos cedo para termos lugar no barco para as Furnas.
lines.en: Deal. We'd better book early so we get a place on the boat to Furnas.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Duvido que haja bilhetes em agosto. Talvez seja mais fácil em setembro.
lines.en: I doubt there are tickets in August. Maybe it's easier in September.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Por mim, tudo bem — desde que não chova o tempo todo.
lines.en: Fine by me — provided it doesn't rain the whole time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Prometo-te uma coisa: quando chegarmos lá, far-te-ei o melhor roteiro da tua vida... como dizem nos livros.
lines.en: I promise you one thing: when we get there, I shall make you the best itinerary of your life... as they say in books.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prefiro a versão falada: 'vou fazer-te o melhor roteiro'. Soa muito mais natural!
lines.en: I prefer the spoken version: 'I'm going to make you the best itinerary'. It sounds much more natural!

days.title: Revisão B2 II
days.objective: Consolidate B2 vocabulary across work, society, culture and science, and deploy argumentation connectors in the right register.
days.paretoFocus: B2 is measured by range: one topic argued with varied connectors and controlled register proves more than a hundred isolated words — this is the exact skill the DIPLE week will test.
drills: Argue for and against remote work in four sentences, using embora, no entanto, por conseguinte and desde que.
Upgrade a colloquial line to formal register: 'A malta está-se nas tintas para as regras' → a formal equivalent.
Pick any science headline and give your opinion in three sentences with 'a meu ver' and 'convém ter em conta'.

items: Can-do check: can you argue an opinion for two minutes using at least three different connectors — without repeating 'mas' and 'porque'?
Can-do check: can you describe a health or science topic using words like investigação, tratamento and prevenção?
Can-do check: can you say the same message twice — once colloquially, once formally — on demand?

activities.title: Quatro temas, um discurso
activities.intro: One loaded sentence per B2 theme, each carrying a connector or register tool you must now command.

items.hr: O desemprego baixou; no entanto, os salários continuam baixos.
items.en: Unemployment fell; however, wages remain low.
items.note: work theme + contrast connector (no entanto, contudo, porém) with the indicative

items.hr: Embora haja progressos, a habitação continua cara nas grandes cidades.
items.en: Although there is progress, housing remains expensive in the big cities.
items.note: society theme + concession: embora + conjuntivo

items.hr: O festival foi um sucesso; por conseguinte, haverá nova edição no próximo ano.
items.en: The festival was a success; consequently, there will be a new edition next year.
items.note: culture theme + consequence: por conseguinte, portanto, por isso

items.hr: A investigação avança, desde que haja financiamento público.
items.en: Research advances, provided there is public funding.
items.note: science theme + condition: desde que + conjuntivo = provided that

items.hr: Visto que ninguém se opôs, a proposta foi aprovada.
items.en: Since no one objected, the proposal was approved.
items.note: cause with formal flavour: visto que, uma vez que, dado que + indicativo

items.hr: Solicita-se o envio dos documentos até sexta-feira.
items.en: You are requested to send the documents by Friday.
items.note: the impersonal se — backbone of formal register, alongside nominalisation (o envio)

items.hr: Pois é, se calhar tens razão — deixa lá isso.
items.en: That's right, maybe you have a point — let it go.
items.note: the same language at the café: register is a dial, and B2 means turning it at will

activities.title: Conectores e registo
activities.intro: Choose the connector each argument needs, and place every sentence in its proper register.

questions.prompt: Choose the connector: '___ haja riscos, o projeto deve avançar.'
questions.explanation: The conjuntivo 'haja' signals concession: embora + conjuntivo = although.

questions.prompt: Which connector expresses CONSEQUENCE?
questions.explanation: Por conseguinte (like portanto, por isso) introduces a consequence; embora concedes, caso conditions, visto que gives a cause.

questions.prompt: Complete with the 'provided that' connector: 'Podes usar o carro, ___ que o devolvas até às oito.'
questions.explanation: Desde que + conjuntivo = provided that; note the conjuntivo 'devolvas' it triggers.

questions.prompt: Which sentence belongs in a formal complaint letter?
options: Solicito o reembolso do valor pago.
Epá, quero o meu dinheiro de volta.
Devolvam lá o dinheiro, pode ser?
questions.answer: Solicito o reembolso do valor pago.
questions.explanation: Formal register uses solicitar and nominalisation (o reembolso); the other options are café Portuguese.

questions.prompt: Complete with the science word for research: 'Sem financiamento, a ___ portuguesa perde os melhores cientistas.'
questions.explanation: 'A investigação' = research — the flagship word of the science theme.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'Although the topic is difficult, it's worth discussing.'
questions.explanation: Embora + conjuntivo (seja) up front; 'discuti-lo' shows the clitic mutating the infinitive: discutir + o → discuti-lo.

activities.title: Debate de café
activities.intro: A friend brings up a news story about banning phones in schools — a full B2 debate over two bicas. Play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Viste a notícia? Querem proibir os telemóveis nas escolas.
lines.en: Did you see the news? They want to ban mobile phones in schools.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Vi. A meu ver, faz sentido: embora sejam úteis, distraem imenso os miúdos.
lines.en: I did. In my view it makes sense: although they're useful, they distract the kids enormously.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Concordo em parte. No entanto, proibir nunca resolve tudo — é preciso educar.
lines.en: I partly agree. However, banning never solves everything — you have to educate.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pois, mas convém ter em conta os dados: as escolas que proibiram viram os resultados melhorar.
lines.en: Right, but it's worth taking the data into account: schools that banned them saw results improve.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Desde que haja alternativas nos intervalos, até posso apoiar a ideia.
lines.en: Provided there are alternatives at break time, I can even support the idea.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Visto que estamos de acordo no essencial, só falta convencer o resto do país.
lines.en: Since we agree on the essentials, all that's left is convincing the rest of the country.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Escreve uma carta ao ministério — mas em registo formal, sem 'epá' nem 'fixe'!
lines.en: Write a letter to the ministry — but in formal register, no 'epá' or 'fixe'!

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Prometo: 'Venho por este meio expor a minha posição...' Já estou pronto para o DIPLE.
lines.en: I promise: 'I hereby set out my position...' I'm ready for the DIPLE already.

days.title: DIPLE: Compreensão da Leitura
days.objective: Tackle DIPLE reading tasks: opinion articles and formal correspondence, answering inference and author's-stance questions.
days.paretoFocus: DIPLE reading rewards stance-spotting: find the sentence where the author tips their hand and the inference questions solve themselves — a quarter of the exam decided by one skill.
resources: CAPLE — Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
drills: Read any Portuguese opinion column and find the one sentence that carries the author's position.
Scan a formal letter for its fixed formulas: 'Exmos. Senhores', 'Venho por este meio', 'Sem outro assunto'.
For one paragraph of any article, ask yourself: what is implied but never stated?

items: Recall the weight of the reading component in the DIPLE: a quarter of the total, like each of the other three.
Say three verbs that signal an author's stance in a text: defender, criticar, admitir.
Translate: 'The author is against the measure, although he admits its advantages.'

activities.title: Ler a posição do autor
activities.intro: The stance markers and formal-letter formulas that DIPLE reading texts are built from — spot these and the questions answer themselves.

items.hr: O autor defende que o turismo deve ser limitado no centro histórico.
items.en: The author argues that tourism should be limited in the historic centre.
items.note: defender que = to argue that; criticar, apoiar and admitir are its stance siblings

items.hr: Admito que haja vantagens; contudo, os custos superam-nas.
items.en: I admit there may be advantages; however, the costs outweigh them.
items.note: the concession move — the author's REAL position comes after 'contudo'

items.hr: Não deixa de ser curioso que ninguém tenha protestado.
items.en: It is rather curious that nobody protested.
items.note: irony marker: 'não deixa de ser curioso' politely signals criticism

items.hr: Por outras palavras, a medida beneficia poucos e prejudica muitos.
items.en: In other words, the measure benefits few and harms many.
items.note: 'por outras palavras' rephrases the thesis — a favourite spot for exam questions

items.hr: Venho por este meio apresentar uma reclamação relativa à fatura de junho.
items.en: I hereby present a complaint regarding the June invoice.
items.note: the formal correspondence opener — DIPLE reading loves official letters

items.hr: Agradecendo desde já a atenção dispensada, apresento os melhores cumprimentos.
items.en: Thanking you in advance for your attention, I present my best regards.
items.note: the standard formal closing — recognise it to identify letter types quickly

items.hr: Ler nas entrelinhas: o que o texto sugere sem afirmar.
items.en: Reading between the lines: what the text suggests without stating.
items.note: inference questions test exactly this — implied meaning, not quoted words

activities.title: Compreensão tipo DIPLE
activities.intro: Read this opinion text as in the exam, then answer. — 'As trotinetas e nós' (crónica de opinião): 'Confesso que, ao início, olhei para as trotinetas elétricas com simpatia. Prometiam menos carros, menos ruído e uma cidade mais leve. Dois anos depois, o balanço é outro: passeios transformados em pistas, peões assustados e aparelhos abandonados em cada esquina de Lisboa. Admito que o problema não está na tecnologia, mas na ausência de regras claras. Contudo, enquanto a câmara hesitar, quem paga são os mais frágeis — os idosos e as crianças. Não deixa de ser curioso que as empresas falem tanto de mobilidade suave e tão pouco de responsabilidade. Regular não é proibir; é, por outras palavras, devolver o passeio a quem caminha.'

questions.prompt: What was the author's INITIAL attitude towards e-scooters?
options: He viewed them with sympathy at first.
He was hostile to them from the start.
He was completely indifferent to them.
He was afraid of them from day one.
questions.answer: He viewed them with sympathy at first.
