questions.explanation: 'Confesso que, ao início, olhei para as trotinetas elétricas com simpatia' — the stance then shifts with 'dois anos depois, o balanço é outro'.

questions.prompt: According to the text, where does the real problem lie?
options: in the absence of clear rules
in the price of the scooters
questions.answer: in the absence of clear rules
questions.explanation: 'O problema não está na tecnologia, mas na ausência de regras claras' — the concession isolates the true cause.

questions.prompt: What is the author's overall stance?
options: Regulate the scooters without banning them.
Leave the situation exactly as it is.
Turn all pavements into scooter lanes.
questions.answer: Regulate the scooters without banning them.
questions.explanation: The closing thesis: 'Regular não é proibir; é devolver o passeio a quem caminha' — regulation, not prohibition.

questions.prompt: 'Não deixa de ser curioso que as empresas falem tanto de mobilidade suave...' — what is IMPLIED?
options: The author is criticising the companies' hypocrisy.
The author finds the companies genuinely admirable.
The author works for a scooter company.
The author thinks the companies talk too little about mobility.
questions.answer: The author is criticising the companies' hypocrisy.
questions.explanation: The 'curioso' formula is ironic: companies preach 'soft mobility' while dodging responsibility — an inference, never stated outright.

questions.prompt: While the city council hesitates, who pays the price, according to the author?
questions.explanation: 'Quem paga são os mais frágeis — os idosos e as crianças': the dash introduces exactly who 'os mais frágeis' are.

questions.prompt: Complete the formal letter opener you must recognise in the exam: 'Venho por este ___ apresentar uma reclamação.'
questions.explanation: 'Venho por este meio + infinitive' = I hereby...; the fixed opener of Portuguese formal letters.

activities.title: Treino de leitura com a tutora
activities.intro: Your tutor quizzes you on the scooter article, exam-style. Play 'Me' and defend your reading.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então, leste a crónica sobre as trotinetas. Qual é a posição do autor?
lines.en: So, you read the column about the e-scooters. What is the author's position?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Ele defende a regulação: não quer proibir, quer regras claras e passeios livres para os peões.
lines.en: He argues for regulation: he doesn't want a ban, he wants clear rules and pavements free for pedestrians.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E como é que ele marca a concessão, lembras-te?
lines.en: And how does he mark the concession, do you remember?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Com 'admito que' seguido de 'contudo' — admite as vantagens, mas a posição verdadeira vem depois da vírgula.
lines.en: With 'I admit that' followed by 'however' — he admits the advantages, but the true position comes after the comma.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muito bem. E a ironia, onde está escondida?
lines.en: Very good. And the irony, where is it hidden?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Na frase 'não deixa de ser curioso': parece neutra, mas é uma crítica às empresas.
lines.en: In the phrase 'it is rather curious': it looks neutral, but it's a criticism of the companies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfeito. No exame, procura sempre essas marcas antes de responderes às perguntas.
lines.en: Perfect. In the exam, always look for those markers before answering the questions.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: É o que farei: primeiro a tese, depois as entrelinhas.
lines.en: That's what I'll do: first the thesis, then between the lines.

days.title: DIPLE: Produção e Interação Escritas
days.objective: Write DIPLE-style texts: a structured opinion essay and a formal complaint letter built on the right fixed formulas.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners mark structure first: an essay with introdução, argumentos and conclusão plus the letter formulas earns the pass before style even enters the equation.
resources: CAPLE — Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
drills: Outline an essay on remote work out loud: thesis, two arguments, one concession, conclusion.
Recite the complaint-letter skeleton: 'Exmos. Senhores... Venho por este meio... Solicito... Sem outro assunto...'
Rewrite one colloquial opinion ('isto não presta') in essay register ('esta solução revela-se insuficiente').

items: Recall the three parts of the DIPLE opinion essay and one connector for each.
Say the opening and closing formulas of a carta de reclamação from memory.
Translate: 'I hereby request the refund of the amount paid.'

activities.title: O esqueleto dos textos que passam
activities.intro: The reusable skeleton of the two DIPLE writing tasks: the opinion essay and the formal complaint letter.

items.hr: Muito se tem discutido sobre o teletrabalho e os seus efeitos.
items.en: There has been much discussion about remote work and its effects.
items.note: a classic essay INTRODUCTION opener — present the topic without giving your verdict yet

items.hr: Em primeiro lugar... Em segundo lugar... Por último...
items.en: In the first place... Secondly... Lastly...
items.note: the argument scaffold of the development paragraphs

items.hr: É certo que existem desvantagens; no entanto, os benefícios são maiores.
items.en: It is true that disadvantages exist; however, the benefits are greater.
items.note: the concession move — admit, then counter; examiners reward it every time

items.hr: Em suma, o equilíbrio parece ser a resposta mais sensata.
items.en: In short, balance seems to be the most sensible answer.
items.note: 'em suma' or 'em conclusão' opens the closing paragraph — never end without one

items.hr: Exmos. Senhores,
items.en: Dear Sirs,
items.note: formal letter greeting; use 'Exmo. Senhor Diretor' when you know the addressee

items.hr: Venho por este meio apresentar uma reclamação relativa à encomenda n.º 4531.
items.en: I hereby present a complaint regarding order no. 4531.
items.note: the complaint formula: venho por este meio + infinitive, then the concrete facts

items.hr: Solicito, assim, o reembolso do valor pago no prazo de dez dias úteis.
items.en: I therefore request the refund of the amount paid within ten working days.
items.note: solicito = formal 'I request'; always state exactly what you want, with a deadline

items.hr: Sem outro assunto, apresento os melhores cumprimentos.
items.en: With no further matters, I present my best regards.
items.note: the standard sign-off line before your name

activities.title: Montar o texto
activities.intro: Exam-style structure work: place each sentence in its slot and complete the fixed formulas from memory.

questions.prompt: Which sentence belongs in the INTRODUCTION of an opinion essay?
options: Muito se tem discutido sobre o impacto das redes sociais.
Em suma, as redes sociais devem ser reguladas.
Em segundo lugar, há o problema da privacidade.
Sem outro assunto, apresento os melhores cumprimentos.
questions.answer: Muito se tem discutido sobre o impacto das redes sociais.
questions.explanation: The introduction presents the topic; 'em suma' closes, 'em segundo lugar' develops, and the last option belongs in a letter.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct order of a carta de reclamação:
options: saudação → motivo da reclamação → pedido → despedida
questions.answer: saudação → motivo da reclamação → pedido → despedida
questions.explanation: Greeting (Exmos. Senhores), the facts and reason, the concrete request with deadline, and the formal sign-off — always in that order.

questions.prompt: Complete the request formula (formal 'I request'): '___, assim, o reembolso do valor pago.'
questions.explanation: 'Solicito' is the formal request verb of official letters; 'quero' would be a register error.

questions.prompt: Your essay needs a concession. Which sentence does the job?
options: É certo que o teletrabalho isola; no entanto, aumenta a produtividade.
O teletrabalho é bom e pronto.
Epá, cada um trabalha onde quiser.
O teletrabalho aumenta a produtividade porque aumenta a produtividade.
questions.answer: É certo que o teletrabalho isola; no entanto, aumenta a produtividade.
questions.explanation: 'É certo que... no entanto...' admits the opposing point and counters it — the highest-scoring essay move.

questions.prompt: Complete the letter closing: 'Sem outro ___, apresento os melhores cumprimentos.'
questions.explanation: 'Sem outro assunto' — literally 'with no other matter' — is the fixed pre-signature line.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'I hereby wish to make a complaint.'
questions.explanation: The complaint opener is a frozen formula — learn it as one block and deploy it word for word.

activities.title: Rever a carta de reclamação
activities.intro: Your tutor reviews the draft complaint letter you wrote for practice. Play 'Me' and take the corrections on board.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Trouxeste a carta de reclamação? Deixa-me dar uma vista de olhos.
lines.en: Did you bring the complaint letter? Let me have a quick look.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Aqui está. Comecei com 'Exmos. Senhores' e expliquei logo o motivo.
lines.en: Here it is. I started with 'Dear Sirs' and explained the reason straight away.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Boa abertura. Mas aqui escreveste 'quero o dinheiro de volta' — isso é registo de café.
lines.en: Good opening. But here you wrote 'I want my money back' — that's café register.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Tens razão. Fica melhor 'solicito o reembolso do valor pago'.
lines.en: You're right. 'I request the refund of the amount paid' works better.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Exatamente. E falta o prazo: dá-lhes dez dias úteis, como nas cartas a sério.
lines.en: Exactly. And the deadline is missing: give them ten working days, like in real letters.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Anotado. E fecho com 'Sem outro assunto, apresento os melhores cumprimentos'.
lines.en: Noted. And I close with 'With no further matters, I present my best regards'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Perfeito. Com esta estrutura, a parte escrita do DIPLE não te vai assustar.
lines.en: Perfect. With this structure, the DIPLE written part won't scare you.

days.title: DIPLE: Compreensão do Oral
days.objective: Handle DIPLE listening: news reports and interviews, catching the speaker's stance and what is implied but never stated.
days.paretoFocus: Broadcast Portuguese runs on formulas — 'segundo fontes oficiais', 'ao que tudo indica' — know them and half of every news item decodes itself before the details even arrive.
resources: CAPLE — Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
drills: Listen to two minutes of Portuguese radio news and note: who, what, where, and is anything unconfirmed?
Say five broadcast openers from memory: 'Segundo fontes oficiais...', 'Ao que tudo indica...', 'Em direto de...'
After any interview clip, decide in one sentence: is the guest for, against, or hedging?

items: Recall three phrases that report unconfirmed information: segundo, ao que tudo indica, alegadamente.
Say how an interviewer politely interrupts: 'Se me permite...'
Translate: 'According to official sources, the strike will continue until Friday.'

activities.title: A língua da rádio e da televisão
activities.intro: The stock phrases of Portuguese news reports and interviews — the skeleton every DIPLE listening text hangs on.

items.hr: Segundo fontes oficiais, a greve vai manter-se até sexta-feira.
items.en: According to official sources, the strike will continue until Friday.
items.note: segundo = according to; the workhorse of news reporting

items.hr: Ao que tudo indica, o acordo será assinado ainda este mês.
items.en: By all indications, the agreement will be signed later this month.
items.note: 'ao que tudo indica' hedges a prediction — the reporter is not certain

items.hr: Alegadamente, a empresa conhecia os riscos desde 2019.
items.en: Allegedly, the company had known about the risks since 2019.
items.note: alegadamente = allegedly — marks an unproven claim; listen for it in stance questions

items.hr: O nosso convidado de hoje dispensa apresentações.
items.en: Today's guest needs no introduction.
items.note: the classic interview opener

items.hr: Se me permite, gostaria de voltar à pergunta anterior.
items.en: If you'll allow me, I'd like to return to the previous question.
items.note: the polite interruption — used by interviewers and interviewees alike

items.hr: O ministro escusou-se a comentar o caso.
items.en: The minister declined to comment on the case.
items.note: escusar-se a = to decline to; implies discomfort without ever saying it — pure inference material

items.hr: Em direto do Porto, com a informação de última hora.
items.en: Live from Porto, with the breaking news.
items.note: em direto = live; de última hora = breaking — orientation cues at the top of a bulletin

activities.title: Noticiário tipo DIPLE
activities.intro: Read this transcript of a radio news item as if you were hearing it once, then answer. — 'Bom dia. Segundo fontes do sindicato, a greve dos ferroviários vai manter-se até sexta-feira, apesar da reunião de ontem com o Governo. Ao que tudo indica, as partes continuam longe de um acordo sobre os aumentos salariais. O ministro escusou-se a comentar, mas fonte próxima do gabinete admite que há margem para negociar. Os passageiros, esses, queixam-se: «Já perdi duas consultas no hospital», contava-nos esta manhã uma passageira na estação de Campanhã. A circulação deverá normalizar-se no sábado.'

questions.prompt: Until when will the strike continue, according to the report?
questions.explanation: 'A greve vai manter-se até sexta-feira'; Saturday is when circulation should return to normal, a classic distractor.

questions.prompt: What does 'o ministro escusou-se a comentar' tell us?
questions.explanation: Escusar-se a + infinitive = to decline to do something — nothing to do with apologising.

questions.prompt: What is IMPLIED by 'fonte próxima do gabinete admite que há margem para negociar'?
options: A deal is still possible, though nothing is official.
The government has already signed the deal.
The source works for the union.
questions.answer: A deal is still possible, though nothing is official.
questions.explanation: An unnamed source admitting 'margem para negociar' signals openness the minister would not state on the record — inference, not quotation.

questions.prompt: Where was the passenger interviewed?
questions.explanation: 'Contava-nos esta manhã uma passageira na estação de Campanhã' — the Porto station, a detail question of the pure exam kind.

questions.prompt: Complete the news formula (according to): '___ fontes do sindicato, a greve vai manter-se.'
questions.explanation: 'Segundo + source' attributes information — the single most frequent opener in Portuguese news.

questions.prompt: What is the tone of the passengers in the report?
questions.explanation: 'Os passageiros queixam-se' and the lost hospital appointments convey frustration — a stance question about tone, not facts.

activities.title: Em direto de Campanhã
activities.intro: A radio journalist stops you at the station for a live vox pop about the strike. Play 'Me' — clear stance, polite hedging.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia, estamos em direto de Campanhã. Pode dizer-nos como esta greve o afeta?
lines.en: Good morning, we're live from Campanhã. Can you tell us how this strike affects you?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Afeta-me bastante: já perdi uma reunião importante e hoje vou chegar tarde outra vez.
lines.en: Good morning. It affects me quite a lot: I've already missed an important meeting and today I'll be late again.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Segundo o sindicato, a greve mantém-se até sexta. Compreende os motivos dos trabalhadores?
lines.en: According to the union, the strike continues until Friday. Do you understand the workers' reasons?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Até certo ponto, sim. Os salários são baixos; contudo, quem fica a pagar é o passageiro.
lines.en: Up to a point, yes. The wages are low; however, it's the passenger who ends up paying.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ao que tudo indica, haverá nova reunião amanhã. Está otimista?
lines.en: By all indications, there will be a new meeting tomorrow. Are you optimistic?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Se me permite a franqueza, não muito. Mas espero que cheguem a acordo antes do fim de semana.
lines.en: If you'll allow me to be frank, not very. But I hope they reach an agreement before the weekend.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Obrigado pelo seu testemunho. Devolvo a emissão ao estúdio.
lines.en: Thank you for your account. Back to the studio.

days.title: DIPLE: Produção e Interação Orais
days.objective: Deliver a structured B2 monologue and hold your own in the exam debate: agree partially, disagree politely, and back everything with a reason.
days.paretoFocus: 'Concordo até certo ponto' plus one reason is the highest-scoring move of the oral exam — it shows stance, nuance and grammar control in a single line.
resources: CAPLE — Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
drills: Give a two-minute monologue on a B2 topic with a clear plan: position → two arguments → concession → conclusion.
Disagree politely five times in a row: 'Permita-me discordar', 'Vejo as coisas de outra forma'...
Record one answer and check: did you vary the connectors, or was everything 'mas' and 'porque'?

items: Recall the monologue plan: position, two arguments, one concession, conclusion.
Say three ways to disagree in Portuguese without being rude.
Translate: 'I agree up to a point, but the data suggest otherwise.'

activities.title: As frases do debate
activities.intro: The interaction toolkit of the DIPLE oral: partial agreement, polite disagreement, reformulation and a clean landing.

items.hr: Concordo até certo ponto, mas os dados sugerem outra coisa.
items.en: I agree up to a point, but the data suggest otherwise.
items.note: partial agreement — the examiner's favourite; always attach a reason

items.hr: Permita-me discordar: a experiência mostra precisamente o contrário.
items.en: Allow me to disagree: experience shows precisely the opposite.
items.note: formal polite disagreement — perfect register for the exam room

items.hr: Vejo as coisas de outra forma, se me permite.
items.en: I see things differently, if you'll allow me.
items.note: a softer alternative — vary your disagreement moves

items.hr: Por um lado poupa tempo; por outro lado, aumenta o isolamento.
items.en: On the one hand it saves time; on the other hand, it increases isolation.
items.note: the balancing structure of every good monologue

items.hr: O que quero dizer é que a medida, na prática, não funciona.
items.en: What I mean is that, in practice, the measure doesn't work.
items.note: 'o que quero dizer é que' buys time and reformulates — a legitimate exam tool

items.hr: Não tenho a certeza, mas diria que se trata de uma tendência recente.
items.en: I'm not sure, but I would say it is a recent trend.
items.note: hedging with 'diria que' keeps you talking even without facts — silence costs more

items.hr: Para concluir, mantenho que os benefícios superam os custos.
items.en: To conclude, I maintain that the benefits outweigh the costs.
items.note: signal your landing — examiners reward a clear, announced conclusion

activities.title: Estratégia oral
activities.intro: Pick the highest-scoring move for each exam moment — the difference between a monologue and a shrug.

questions.prompt: The examiner defends an opinion you reject. Which reply scores highest?
options: Concordo até certo ponto, mas os números mostram outra realidade.
questions.answer: Concordo até certo ponto, mas os números mostram outra realidade.
questions.explanation: Partial agreement plus a justified counter shows nuance and keeps the interaction alive — exactly what the descriptors reward.

questions.prompt: Which sequence structures a balanced exam monologue?
questions.explanation: State your position, support it, concede one point to show nuance, then land on an announced conclusion.

questions.prompt: Complete the polite disagreement: '___-me discordar, mas a experiência mostra o contrário.' (formal imperative of 'permitir')
questions.explanation: 'Permita-me discordar' — the você-register imperative with ênclise, the classic exam-room disagreement.

questions.prompt: You don't know the answer to the examiner's question. Best move?
options: Não tenho a certeza, mas diria que depende do contexto.
Ficar em silêncio até a pergunta mudar.
Mudar de tema sem qualquer explicação.
questions.answer: Não tenho a certeza, mas diria que depende do contexto.
questions.explanation: Hedge and keep talking: 'diria que' shows range and maintains interaction; silence and language-switching lose marks.

questions.prompt: Complete the balancing structure: 'Por um ___ poupa tempo; por outro, aumenta o isolamento.'
questions.explanation: 'Por um lado... por outro (lado)...' frames the two sides of any monologue topic.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'To conclude, I maintain that it is worth investing.'
questions.explanation: Announce the landing (para concluir), restate the stance (mantenho que) and close with the idiom 'vale a pena'.

activities.title: O debate da prova oral
activities.intro: DIPLE oral simulation: the examiner opens a debate on remote work. Play 'Me' — agree in part, disagree politely, land your conclusion.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Passemos ao debate. Defendo que o teletrabalho devia ser um direito de todos os trabalhadores. Concorda?
lines.en: Let's move to the debate. I argue that remote work should be a right for all workers. Do you agree?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo até certo ponto. Para muitas funções faz sentido; contudo, há setores onde é simplesmente impossível.
lines.en: I agree up to a point. For many roles it makes sense; however, there are sectors where it is simply impossible.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Mas os estudos mostram que a produtividade aumenta em casa.
lines.en: But studies show that productivity increases at home.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Permita-me discordar em parte: aumenta nalguns casos, mas os mesmos estudos falam de isolamento e de dias sem fim.
lines.en: Allow me to disagree in part: it increases in some cases, but the same studies speak of isolation and endless days.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Então que solução propõe?
lines.en: So what solution do you propose?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Por um lado, manter dois ou três dias em casa; por outro, garantir que a equipa se encontra regularmente.
lines.en: On the one hand, keep two or three days at home; on the other, ensure the team meets regularly.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E se a empresa recusar?
lines.en: And if the company refuses?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Nesse caso, diria que a lei deve fixar um mínimo, desde que a função o permita. Para concluir: direito sim, mas com bom senso.
lines.en: In that case, I would say the law should set a minimum, provided the role allows it. To conclude: a right, yes, but with common sense.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muito bem argumentado. Terminámos o debate.
lines.en: Very well argued. We have finished the debate.

days.title: Simulação DIPLE — Ponto de controlo B2
days.objective: Walk into the DIPLE knowing exactly what happens: four equal components, the pass mark, and a full oral simulation to close 105 days of European Portuguese.
days.paretoFocus: Knowing the mechanics — four parts at 25% each, Suficiente from 55% overall — removes every exam-day surprise, so all your attention goes where it belongs: the Portuguese.
resources: CAPLE — Exames de PLE (Universidade de Lisboa)
drills: Recite the four DIPLE components and their equal weight without looking.
Do a one-minute lightning monologue on a random B2 topic: science, work or your city.
Write the first sentence of an opinion essay and the first sentence of a complaint letter, back to back.

items: Look back over the 105 days: from 'chamo-me' on day one to debating in the conjuntivo — name the three structures that were hardest and are now yours.
Recall the ladder you climbed: A1 survival, A2 (CIPLE level), B1 (DEPLE), and now B2 (DIPLE) — proof of job and academic proficiency.
Plan the next step out loud, in Portuguese: 'Quando fizer o DIPLE, começarei a preparar o DAPLE.'

activities.title: O dia do exame, sem surpresas
activities.intro: The full DIPLE rundown: components, weights, grades, what the certificate proves, and the exam-day habits that protect your score.

items.hr: O DIPLE tem quatro componentes: Compreensão da Leitura, Produção e Interação Escritas, Compreensão do Oral e Produção e Interação Orais.
items.en: The DIPLE has four components: Reading Comprehension, Written Production and Interaction, Listening Comprehension, and Oral Production and Interaction.
items.note: each component counts 25% — no part matters more than another

items.hr: A classificação é global: Suficiente a partir de 55%.
items.en: The grade is global: Suficiente (pass) from 55%.
items.note: the average across the four components decides — no per-component minimum is published

items.hr: Acima disso: Bom a partir de 70% e Muito Bom a partir de 85%.
items.en: Above that: Bom from 70% and Muito Bom from 85%.
items.note: the same classification scale used across the CAPLE exams

items.hr: O DIPLE certifica o nível B2: autonomia para trabalhar e estudar em português.
items.en: The DIPLE certifies level B2: the autonomy to work and study in Portuguese.
items.note: employers and universities accept it as proof of professional and academic proficiency

items.hr: Leve o documento de identificação e chegue com meia hora de antecedência.
items.en: Take your ID document and arrive half an hour early.
items.note: practical exam-day rules — late arrival can mean exclusion

items.hr: Na prova escrita, planifique cinco minutos antes de começar a redigir.
items.en: In the written paper, plan for five minutes before starting to write.
items.note: a planned text beats a long one — structure is marked first

items.hr: Se não souber uma palavra, contorne-a: 'o aparelho que serve para...'.
items.en: If you don't know a word, work around it: 'the device used for...'.
items.note: paraphrase is a tested B2 skill, not a weakness — never let one word stop you

activities.title: Simulação mista
activities.intro: A final mixed drill in exam format: rules of the game, grammar under pressure and one last notice to decode.

questions.prompt: How much does each DIPLE component weigh in the final grade?
questions.explanation: The four components — reading, writing, listening, oral — are weighted equally at 25% each.

questions.prompt: With a global score of 60%, your DIPLE result is:
questions.explanation: Suficiente runs from 55% to 69%; Bom starts at 70% and Muito Bom at 85% — 60% is a solid pass.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'Quando ___ o diploma, vou emoldurá-lo.'
questions.explanation: Quando + future reference takes the futuro do conjuntivo; for regular verbs it looks identical to the infinitive: receber.

questions.prompt: Complete with the imperfeito do conjuntivo of 'ser': 'Se o exame ___ hoje, estarias pronto.'
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: se + imperfeito do conjuntivo — foram → fosse; the conditional/imperfect follows in the main clause.

questions.prompt: A notice at the exam centre says: 'Não é permitida a utilização de dicionários durante a prova.' What must you do?
options: Leave the dictionary outside the exam room.
Bring two dictionaries, just in case.
Ask the examiner to lend you a dictionary.
questions.answer: Leave the dictionary outside the exam room.
questions.explanation: 'Não é permitida a utilização' — the formal passive of prohibition: dictionaries are banned during the exam.

questions.prompt: Build the Portuguese for: 'When you finish the exam, we'll celebrate together.'
questions.explanation: One last futuro do conjuntivo (acabares) — and a promise: 105 days of European Portuguese deserve a celebration.

activities.title: A prova oral completa
activities.intro: The full DIPLE oral simulation: presentation, monologue and debate, from greeting to verdict. Play 'Me' — this is the finale of the 105-day journey.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bom dia. Sou a examinadora da prova oral do DIPLE. Comecemos: fale-me um pouco de si e da sua relação com o português.
lines.en: Good morning. I am the examiner for the DIPLE oral test. Let's begin: tell me a little about yourself and your relationship with Portuguese.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bom dia. Estudo português europeu há vários anos e, nos últimos cento e cinco dias, segui um plano diário até ao nível B2.
lines.en: Good morning. I've been studying European Portuguese for several years and, over the last one hundred and five days, I followed a daily plan up to level B2.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Interessante. Passemos ao monólogo: 'A tecnologia aproxima ou afasta as pessoas?' Tem dois minutos.
lines.en: Interesting. Let's move to the monologue: 'Does technology bring people together or drive them apart?' You have two minutes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Por um lado, a tecnologia aproxima quem está longe: falo com a família todas as semanas por videochamada. Por outro lado, afasta quem está perto, quando cada um olha apenas para o seu ecrã.
lines.en: On the one hand, technology brings together those who are far away: I talk to my family every week by video call. On the other hand, it drives apart those who are near, when everyone looks only at their own screen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: E qual é a sua conclusão?
lines.en: And what is your conclusion?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Embora os riscos sejam reais, mantenho que tudo depende do uso: a ferramenta não substitui a vontade de estar com os outros.
lines.en: Although the risks are real, I maintain that everything depends on how it's used: the tool doesn't replace the will to be with others.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Passemos à interação: eu defendo que as redes sociais deviam ter idade mínima de dezasseis anos.
lines.en: Let's move to the interaction: I argue that social networks should have a minimum age of sixteen.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Concordo até certo ponto: protege os mais novos; contudo, pode empurrá-los para plataformas escondidas. Talvez a educação digital resolva mais do que a proibição.
lines.en: I agree up to a point: it protects the youngest; however, it may push them towards hidden platforms. Perhaps digital education solves more than prohibition.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Muito bem. A prova terminou. Fez um excelente percurso — bem-vindo ao nível B2.
lines.en: Very good. The test is over. You've come a long way — welcome to level B2.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Muito obrigado. Quando receber o resultado, celebrarei com um pastel de nata... e começarei a pensar no DAPLE.
lines.en: Thank you very much. When I get the result, I'll celebrate with a custard tart... and start thinking about the DAPLE.
